topics.name: Binomial Theorem for Positive Integral Indices (XI-7.1)

questions.question: Coefficient of x^3 in the expansion of tan x is
questions.question_raw: Coefficient of x3 in the expansion of tan x is
questions.explanation:
tan x = {tex} x + \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + \frac{{2{x^5}}}{{15}} + ...{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} coefficient of x3 = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: What is the middle term of (4 + 2x)^6?
questions.question_raw: What is the middle term of (4 + 2x)6?
questions.explanation:
The middle term will be the 4th term
4th term = 6C3 (4)6 – 3(2x)3
= 20 (64) (8x3)
= 10240 x3

questions.question: If n is a +ve integer, then the binomial coefficients equidistant from the beginning and the end in the expansion of (x + a)^n are
questions.question_raw: If n is a +ve integer, then the binomial coefficients equidistant from the beginning and the end in the expansion of (x + a)n are
questions.explanation:
(x + a)n = {tex}^{n} C_{0} x^{n}+^{n} C_{1} x^{n-1} a^{1}+^{n} C_{2}x^{n-2} a^{2}{/tex} + ..... + {tex}^{n} C_{n-1} x a^{n-1}+^{n} C_{n} a^{n}{/tex} 
Now {tex}^{n} C_{0}=^{n} C_{n}, ^{n} C_{1}=^{n} C_{n-1},^{n} C_{2}=^{n} C_{n-2}{/tex} ...... [ {tex}\because^{n} C_{r}=^{n} C_{n-r}{/tex}]

questions.question: If the rth term in the expansion of $\left(\frac{x^3}{3}-\frac{2}{x^2}\right)^{10}$ contains x^20, then r =
questions.question_raw: If the rth term in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x^3}{3}-\frac{2}{x^2}\right)^{10}{/tex} contains x20, then r =
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}-\frac{2}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{10} C_{r}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right)^{10-r}\left(-\frac{2}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} 
Hence rth term = Tr = {tex}^{10} C_{r-1}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{2}{x^{2}}\right)^{r-1}{/tex} 
Comparing the indices of x in x20 and in Tr, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 33 - 3r - 2r + 2 = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5r = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 3

questions.question: If the coefficients of ${x^{ - 7}}$ and ${x^{ - 8}}$ in the expansion of $\left(2+\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^n$ are equal then n =
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of {tex}{x^{ - 7}}{/tex} and {tex}{x^{ - 8}}{/tex} in the expansion of {tex}\left(2+\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^n{/tex} are equal then n =
questions.explanation:
We have the general term in the expansion of {tex} \left( { 2+{ \frac { 1 }{ 3x } } } \right) ^{ n }{/tex} is given by
{tex}T_{ r+1 }=^{ n }{ C_{ r} }(2)^{ (n-r) }\left( \frac { 1 }{ 3x } \right) ^{ r }{/tex}
Now x-r = x-7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 7
and x-r = x-8 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 8
{tex}\therefore T_{ 8 }=T_{ 7+1 }=^{ n }{ C_{ 7} }(2)^{ (n-7) }\left( \frac { 1 }{ 3x } \right) ^{ 7 }{/tex}
{tex}T_{ 9 }=T_{ 8+1 }=^{ n }{ C_{ 8} }(2)^{ (n-8) }\left( \frac { 1 }{ 3x } \right) ^{ 8 }{/tex}
Given, {tex}\frac { ^{ n }{ C_{ 7\quad } }{ 2 }^{ n-7 } }{ { 3 }^{ 7 } } =\frac { ^{ n }{ C_{ 8\quad } }{ 2 }^{ n-8 } }{ { 3 }^{ 8 } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { n! }{ \left( n-7 \right) !.7! } \frac { { 2 }^{ n-7 } }{ { 3 }^{ 7 } } =\frac { n! }{ \left( n-8 \right) !.8! } \frac { { 2 }^{ n-8 } }{ { 3 }^{ 8 } }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac { { 2 }^{ n-7 } }{ n-7 } =\frac { { 2 }^{ n-8 } }{ { 8\times 3 } }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac { { 2 }^{ n-7 } }{ { 2 }^{ n-8 } } =\frac { \left( n-7 \right) }{ 24 }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 24\times 2=n-7{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow n=55{/tex}

questions.question: The largest term in the expansion of (1 + x)^19 when x = $ \frac 1 2$ is
questions.question_raw: The largest term in the expansion of (1 + x)19 when x = {tex} \frac 1 2{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term in the expansion of (1 + x)19 is given by 
Tr+1 = {tex}^{19} C_{r}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
Now {tex}\frac{T_{r+1}}{T_{r}}=\frac{^{19} C_{r}}{_{19} C_{r-1}}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{19 !(x)^{r}}{(19-r) ! \cdot r !} \times \frac{(20-r) !(r-1) !}{19 ! \cdot(x)^{r-1}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{(20-r) x}{r}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{(20-r)}{2 r}{/tex} since given x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
Now {tex}\frac{T_{r+1}}{T_{r}} \geq 1{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(20-r)}{2 r} \geq 1{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20 - r {tex}\geq{/tex} 2r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}r \leq \frac{20}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}r \leq 6 \frac{2}{3}{/tex} 
Hence the maximum value of r is 6 and hence the greatest term is Tr+1 = T7

questions.question: If coefficients of three successive terms in the expansion of (x + 1)^n are in the ratio 1 : 3 : 5, then n is equal to
questions.question_raw: If coefficients of three successive terms in the expansion of (x + 1)n are in the ratio 1 : 3 : 5, then n is equal to
questions.explanation:
Let the coefficients of the three successive terms be nCr, nCr+1 and nCr+2 
Now given nCr : nCr+1 : nCr+2 = 1 : 3 : 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{^{n} C_{r}}{^{n} C_{r+1}}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{^{n} C_{r+1}}{^{n} C_{r+2}}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{r+1}{n-r}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{r+2}{n-r-1}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n - r = 3r + 3 and 3n - 3r - 3 = 5r + 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n - 4r = 3 ....(i) and 3n - 8r = 13 ....(ii)
Now solving equations (i) and (ii), we get
4r = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 1
Now put r = 1 in eqn (i), we get n = 7

questions.question: The exponent of x occurring in the 7^th term of expansion of $\left(\frac{3 x}{2}-\frac{8}{7 x}\right)^9$ is
questions.question_raw: The exponent of x occurring in the 7th term of expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{3 x}{2}-\frac{8}{7 x}\right)^9{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{3 x}{2}-\frac{8}{7 x}\right)^{9}{/tex} 
Here n = 9 and r + 1 = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
Also x = {tex}\frac{3 x}{2}{/tex} and a = {tex}-\frac{8}{7 x}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} T7 = T6+1 = {tex}^{9} C_{6} \left(\frac{3 x}{2}\right)^{3}\left(\frac{-8}{7 x}\right)^{6}{/tex} 
= {tex}^{9} C_{6}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{3}\left(\frac{-8}{7}\right)^{6} x^{-3}{/tex} 
Hence the exponent of x = -3

questions.question: The term independent of x in the expansion of $\left(x-\frac{3}{x^2}\right)^{18}$ is
questions.question_raw: The term independent of x in the expansion of {tex}\left(x-\frac{3}{x^2}\right)^{18}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of {tex}(x+a)^{n} {/tex} is {tex}T_{r+1}=^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex}
Now consider {tex}\left(x-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{18}{/tex}
Here {tex}T_{r+1}=^{18} C_{r} (x)^{18-r}\left(-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex}
The term independent of x means index of  x is 0.
Comparing the indices of x in {tex}x^{0}{/tex} and in {tex}T_{r+1}{/tex}, we get 18 - r - 2r = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow r=\frac{18}{3}=6{/tex}
Therefore the required term is {tex}T_{6+1}=^{18} C_{6}{/tex}
{tex}(x)^{18-6}\left(-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}=^{ 18 }{ C_{ 6 } }{ .3 }^{ 6 }{/tex}

questions.question: The number of dissimilar terms in the expansion of (a + b + c)^12 is
questions.question_raw: The number of dissimilar terms in the expansion of (a + b + c)12 is
questions.explanation:
We have the number of dissimilar terms in the expansion of (x + y + z)n is 
(n + 1) + n + (n - 1) + .... + 3 + 2 + 1 = {tex}\frac{(n+1)(n+2)}{2}{/tex} 
Hence the number of dissimilar terms in the expansion of (a + b + c)12 = {tex}\frac{(12+1)(12+2)}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{13.14}{2}{/tex} = 91

questions.question: The term containing x^3 in the expansion of (x - 2y)^7 is
questions.question_raw: The term containing x3 in the expansion of (x - 2y)7 is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (x - 2y)7 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Comparing the indices of x in x3 and Tr+1, we get
7 - r = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 4
Therefore, the required term is T4+1 = T5

questions.question: The coefficients of ${x^p}$ and ${x^q}$ ( p, q are + ve integers) in the binomial expansion of ${(1 + x)^{p\; + \;q}}$ are
questions.question_raw: The coefficients of {tex}{x^p}{/tex} and {tex}{x^q}{/tex} ( p, q are + ve integers) in the binomial expansion of {tex}{(1 + x)^{p\; + \;q}}{/tex} are
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x+a)n is Tr+1 = nCr (x)n-r ar
Now consider {tex}(1+x)^{p+q}{/tex}
Here {tex}T_{r+1}=^{p+q} C_{r} \quad(1)^{(p+q)-r}(x)^{r}{/tex}
Comparing the indices of x in xp and in {tex}T_{r+1}{/tex},we get p=r
Therefore the coefficient of xp is {tex}T_{p+1}=^{p+q} C_{p}{/tex}
Now again comparing the indices of x in xq and in {tex}T_{r+1}{/tex} ,we get q=r
Therefore the coefficient of xq is {tex}T_{p+1}=^{p+q} C_{p}{/tex}
But we have {tex}^{p+q} C_{p}=^{p+q} C_{q}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because^{n} C_{r}=^{n} C_{n-r}\right]{/tex}

questions.question: If 2nd, 3rd and 4th terms in the expansion of (x + a)^n are 240, 720 and 1080 respectively, then the value of n is
questions.question_raw: If 2nd, 3rd and 4th terms in the expansion of (x + a)n are 240, 720 and 1080 respectively, then the value of n is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (x - 2y)7 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{7} C_{r}(x)^{7-r}(-2 y)^{r}{/tex} 
Comparing the indices of x in x3 and in Tr+1, we get 
7 - r = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 4
Therefore the required term is T4+1 = T5 = 18328
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1  = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
According to the equation
T2 = {tex}^{n} C_{1}(x)^{n-1} a^{1}{/tex} = 240 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n(x)n - 1 a = 240 .....(i)
T3 = {tex}^{n} C_{2}(x)^{n-2} a^{2}{/tex} = 720 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2}(x)^{n-2} a^{2}{/tex} = 720 ....(ii)
T4 = {tex}^{n} C_{3}(x)^{n-3} a^{3}{/tex} = 1080 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{6}(x)^{n-3} a^{3}{/tex} = 1080 ....(iii)
Multiply equations (i) and (iii), we get {tex}\frac{n^{2}(n-1)(n-2)}{6}(x)^{2 n-4} a^{4}{/tex} = 240 {tex}\times{/tex} 1080 ....(iv)
Now squaring equation (ii), we get {tex}\frac{n^{2}(n-1)^{2}}{4}(x)^{2 n-4} a^{4}{/tex} = 720 {tex}\times{/tex} 720 ....(v)
Now dividing equations (iv) by (v), we get {tex}\frac{(n-2)}{(n-1)} \cdot \frac{4}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{240 \times 1080}{720 \times 720}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(n-2)}{(n-1)}=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{6}{4}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4n - 8 = 3n - 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 5

questions.question: If the first three terms in the expansion of (x + a)^n are 729, 7290 and 30375 respectively, then the value of n is
questions.question_raw: If the first three terms in the expansion of (x + a)n are 729, 7290 and 30375 respectively, then the value of n is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
According to the question
T4 = {tex}^{n} C_{0}(x)^{n} a^{0}{/tex} = 729 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x)n = 729 ....(i)
T2 = {tex}^{n} C_{1} (x)^{n-1} a^{1}{/tex} = 240 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n(x)n-1 a = 7290 ....(ii)
T3 = {tex}^{n} C_{2}(x)^{n-2} a^{2}{/tex} = 720 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2}(x)^{n-2} a^{2}{/tex} = 30375 ....(iii)
Multiply equations (i) and (iii), we get {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2}(x)^{2 n-2} a^{2}{/tex} = 729 {tex}\times{/tex} 30375 ...(iv)
Now squaring equation (ii) we get n2(x)2n-2 a2 = 7290 {tex}\times{/tex} 7290 ....(v)
Now dividing equations (iv) by (v), we get {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2 n^{2}}=\frac{729 \times 30375}{7290 \times 7290}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(n-1)}{2 n}=\frac{5}{12}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6n - 6 = 5n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 6

questions.question: The coefficient of $\frac { 1 }{ { x }^{ 17 } } $ in the expansion of ${ \left( { { x }^{ 4 }-{ \frac { 1 }{ { { x^{ 3 } } } } } } \right) ^{ 15 } }$ is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of {tex}\frac { 1 }{ { x }^{ 17 } } {/tex} in the expansion of {tex}{ \left( { { x }^{ 4 }-{ \frac { 1 }{ { { x^{ 3 } } } } } } \right) ^{ 15 } }{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We quad have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{ n }{ C_{ r\quad } }\left( x \right) ^{ n-r }{ a }^{ r }\quad{/tex}
Now consider {tex}\left(x^{4}-\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{15}{/tex} 
Here {tex}T_{ r+1 }=^{ 15 }{ C_{ r\quad } }\left( { x }^{ 4 } \right) ^{ 15-r }{ \left( { -\frac { 1 }{ { { x^{ 3 } } } } } \right) }^{ r }\\ {/tex}
Comparing the indices of x in x-17 and in Tr+1, we get 60 - 4r - 3r = -17 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7r = 77 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 11
Therefore the required term is T11+1 = T12 = {tex}{ }^{15} C_{11} \quad\left(x^{4}\right)^{4}\left(-\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{11}{/tex}
Hence the coefficient of {tex}\frac { 1 }{ { x }^{ 17 } } =-^{ 15 }{ C_{ 11} }{/tex} = -1365

questions.question: The two consecutive terms in the expansion of (3 + 2x)^74, which have equal coefficients, are
questions.question_raw: The two consecutive terms in the expansion of (3 + 2x)74, which have equal coefficients, are
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n}C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (3 + 2x)74 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{74} C_{r}(3)^{74-r}(2 x)^{r}{/tex} 
Let the coefficient of Tr+1 = coefficient of Tr+2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{74} C_{r}(3)^{74-r}(2 x)^{r}{/tex} = {tex}^{74} C_{r+1}(3)^{73-r}(2 x)^{r+1}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{74 ! \cdot(3)^{74-r} \cdot(2)^{r}}{(74-r) ! \cdot r !}=\frac{74 ! \cdot(3)^{73-r} \cdot(2)^{r+1}}{(73-r) ! \cdot(r+1) !}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{(74-r)}=\frac{2}{(r+1)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3r + 3 = 148 - 2r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5r = 145
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 29

questions.question: The coefficient of second, third and fourth terms in the binomial expansion of ${(1 + x)^n}$ ( ‘n’, a + ve integer) are in A.P.., if n is equal to
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of second, third and fourth terms in the binomial expansion of {tex}{(1 + x)^n}{/tex} ( ‘n’, a + ve integer) are in A.P.., if n is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex} { \left( 1+x \right) }^{ n }=1+^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } }\left( x \right) +^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ \left( x \right) }^{ 2 }+..........+{ \left( x \right) }^{ n }\quad \quad{/tex}
Given that coefficients of {tex} T_{ 2 },~T_{ 3 }{/tex} and {tex} T_{ 4 }{/tex} are in A.P
{tex} \Rightarrow ^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } },^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{/tex} and {tex}\quad ^{ n }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{/tex} are in A.P
{tex} \Rightarrow ^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }-^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } }=^{ n }{ C_{ 3\quad } }-^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }\\ \Rightarrow \frac { n\left( n-1 \right) }{ 2 } -n=\frac { n\left( n-1 \right) \left( n-2 \right) }{ 6 } -\frac { n\left( n-1 \right) }{ 2 } \\ \Rightarrow \frac { n\left( n-1 \right) -2n }{ 2 } =\frac { n\left( n-1 \right) \left( n-2 \right) -3n\left( n-1 \right) }{ 6 } \\ \Rightarrow \frac { n\left( n-3 \right) }{ 2 } =\frac { n\left( n-1 \right) \left( n-5 \right) }{ 6 } \\ \Rightarrow 3n-9={ n }^{ 2 }-6n+5\\ \Rightarrow { n }^{ 2 }-9n+14=0\\ \Rightarrow \left( n-7 \right) \left( n-2 \right) =0\\ \Rightarrow n=2,7{/tex}
But n = 2 is not possible as {tex}\quad ^{ 2 }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{/tex}is not defined. Hence we get n= 7.

questions.question: The coefficient of x^3 in the binomial expansion of $\left(x-\frac{m}{x}\right)^{11}$ is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x3 in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(x-\frac{m}{x}\right)^{11}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of {tex}(x+a)^n {/tex} is {tex}T_{r+1}={ }^n C_r (x)^{n-r} a^r{/tex}
Now consider {tex}\left(x-\frac{m}{x}\right)^{11}{/tex}
Here {tex}T_{r+1}={ }^{11} C_r \quad(x)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{m}{x}\right)^r{/tex}
Comparing the indices of x in x3 and in Tr+1, we get
11 - r - r = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 4
Therefore the required term is T4+1 = {tex}T_5={ }^{11} C_4 \quad(x)^{11-4}\left(-\frac{m}{x}\right)^4=330 m^4 x^3{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^12 in the expansion of ${ \left( { 3-{ \frac { x^{ 3 } }{ { { 6 } } } } } \right) ^{ 7 } }$ is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x12 in the expansion of {tex}{ \left( { 3-{ \frac { x^{ 3 } }{ { { 6 } } } } } \right) ^{ 7 } }{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(3-\frac{x^{3}}{6}\right)^{7}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{7} C_{r} \quad(3)^{7-r}\left(-\frac{x^{3}}{6}\right)^{r}{/tex} 
comparing the indices of x in x12 and in Tr+1, we get
3r = 12 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 4
Therefore the required term is T4+1 = T4 = {tex}^{7} C_{4} (3)^{7-4}\left(-\frac{x^{3}}{6}\right)^{4}{/tex} = {tex}35 \times 3^{3} \times \frac{x^{12}}{6^{4}}=\frac{35}{48} x^{12}{/tex}

questions.question: If the coefficients of (2r +4)th term and (r -2)th term in the expansion of (1 + x)^18 be equal then, find the value of r
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of (2r +4)th term and (r -2)th term in the expansion of (1 + x)18 be equal then, find the value of r
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (1 + x)18 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{18} C_{r}(1)^{18-r}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
T2r+4 = T(2r+ 3)+1 = {tex}^{18} C_{2 r+3}(x)^{2 r+3}{/tex} 
Tr-2 = T(r - 3)+1 = {tex}^{18} C_{r-3} x^{r-3}{/tex} 
Given the coefficient of T2r+4 = coefficient of Tr-2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{18} C_{2 r+3}=^{18} C_{r-3}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}^{n} C_{p}=^{n} C_{q}{/tex} then either p = q or p + q = n]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2r + 3 + r - 3 = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3r = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6

questions.question: If x = 99^50 + 100^50 and y = (101)^50 then
questions.question_raw: If x = 9950 + 10050 and y = (101)50 then
questions.explanation:
Given x = 9950 + 10050 and y = (101)50 
Now y = (101)50 = (100 + 1)50 = {tex}^{50} C_{0} (100)^{50}+^{50} C_{1} (100)^{49}{/tex} + {tex}^{50} C_{2}(100)^{48}{/tex} + .... + {tex}^{50} C_{50}{/tex} .....(i)
Also (99)50 = (100 - 1)50 = {tex}=^{50} C_{0} (100)^{50}-^{50} C_{1} (100)^{49}{/tex} + {tex}^{50} C_{2} (100)^{48}{/tex} - .... + {tex}^{50} C_{50}{/tex} ....(ii)
Now subtract equation (ii) from equation (i), we get
(101)50 - (99)50 = {tex}2\left[^{50} C_{1} \quad(100)^{49}+^{50} C_{3} \quad(100)^{47}+\ldots \right]{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[50(100)^{49}+\frac{50 \times 49 \times 48}{3 \times 2 \times 1}(100)^{47}+\ldots\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}(100)^{50}+2\left(\frac{50 \times 49 \times 48}{3 \times 2 \times 1}(100)^{47}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (101)50 - (99)50 > (100)50
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (101)50 > (100)50 + (99)50 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y > x

questions.question: The greatest coefficient in the expansion of (1 + x)^12 is
questions.question_raw: The greatest coefficient in the expansion of (1 + x)12 is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term in the expansion of (1 + x)12 is given by 
Tr+1 = {tex}^{12} C_{r} (x)^{r}{/tex} 
Let the term with greatest coefficient be the term Tr+1 
Now {tex}\begin{array}{ccc} \text {Coefficient} \ \text {of} \ T_{r+1} \\ \hline \text {Coefficient} \ \text {of} \ T_{r} \end{array}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{^{12} C_{r}}{^{12} C_{r-1}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{12 !}{(12-r) ! \cdot r !} \times \frac{(13-r) !(r-1) !}{12 !}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{(13-r)}{r}{/tex} 
Now {tex}\begin{array}{ccc} \text {Coefficient} \ \text {of} \ T_{r+1} \\ \hline \text {Coefficient} \ \text {of} \ T_{r} \end{array}{/tex} {tex}\ge{/tex} 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(13-r)}{r} \geq 1{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 13 - r {tex}\ge{/tex} r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r {tex}\le{/tex} {tex}\frac{13}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r {tex}\le{/tex} {tex}6\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
Hence the maximum value of r is 6 and hence the greatest term is the seventh term and T7 = T6+1 = {tex}^{12} C_{6}(x)^{6}{/tex} 
Hence greatest coefficient = {tex}^{12} C_{6}{/tex}

questions.question: In the expansion of ${(1 + x)^{60}},$ the sum of coefficients of odd powers of x is
questions.question_raw: In the expansion of {tex}{(1 + x)^{60}},{/tex} the sum of coefficients of odd powers of x is
questions.explanation:
We have{tex}\quad \quad { \left( 1+x \right) }^{ n }=^{ n }{ C_{ 0\quad } }+^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } }\left( x \right) +^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ \left( x \right) }^{ 2 }+..........+{ \left( x \right) }^{ n }\quad .....(i)\quad \\ {/tex}
Also{tex}\quad { \left( 1-x \right) }^{ n }=^{ n }{ C_{ 0\quad } }-^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } }\left( x \right) +^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ \left( x \right) }^{ 2 }-..........+{ \left( -1 \right) }^{ n }{ \left( x \right) }^{ n }\quad ....(ii)\\ {/tex}
Now {tex}\left(1-x\right)^n=\left[{}^nC_{0}+^nC_{2}\left(x\right)^2+^nC_{4}\left(x\right)^4+.....\right]-\left[{}^nC_{1\quad}\left(x\right)+^nC_{3}\left(x\right)^3+^nC_{5}\left(x\right)^5+......\right]\\{/tex}
Let x=1 and n=60
From equation (i), we get
{tex} { 2 }^{ 60 }=^{ 60 }{ C_{ 0\quad } }+^{ 60 }{ C_{ 1\quad } }+^{ 60 }{ C_{ 2\quad } }+..........+^{ 60 }{ C_{ 60\quad } }\\ {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2^{60}{/tex}=( sum of coefficients of even powers of x ) + ( sum of coefficients of odd powers of x) .....(iii) 
From equation (ii), we get {tex}0=\left[^{60} C_{0}+^{60} C_{2}+^{60} C_{4}+\ldots \ldots .\right]-[^{60} C_{1}+^{60} C_{3}+^{60} C_{5}+\ldots \ldots \ldots .]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 0{/tex} =( sum\quad of coefficients of even powers of x ) + ( sum of coefficients of odd powers of x) .....(iii)
Now subtract equation (iv) from equation (iii), we get
{tex}2^{60}-0=2{/tex} (sum of coefficients of odd powers of x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}(sum of coefficients of odd powers of x {tex}x=2^{59}{/tex})

questions.question: Find the middle term in the expansion of ${ \left( { { \frac { x }{ 3 } }+9y } \right) ^{ 10 } }$
questions.question_raw: Find the middle term in the expansion of {tex}{ \left( { { \frac { x }{ 3 } }+9y } \right) ^{ 10 } }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{x}{3}+9 y\right)^{10}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{10} C_{r}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{10-r}(9 y)^{r}{/tex} 
Since n = 10 is even, the middle term is {tex}\left(\frac{n}{2}+1\right){/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{10}{2}+1\right){/tex} = 6th term
Now T6 = T5+1 = {tex}^{10} C_{5}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{10-5}(9 y)^{5}{/tex} = {tex}^{10} C_{5} \left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{5}(9 y)^{5}=^{10} C_{5} \times 3^{5} x^{5} y^{5}{/tex} = 61236 x5y5

questions.question: Find a if the coefficient of x^2 and x^3 in the expansion of ${ \left(3+ax \right) }^{ 9 }\quad $ are equal
questions.question_raw: Find  a if the coefficient of x2 and x3 in the expansion of {tex}{ \left(3+ax \right) }^{ 9 }\quad {/tex} are equal
questions.explanation: {tex}{ \left( 3+ax \right) }^{ 9 }=\quad ^{ 9 }{ C_{ 0\quad } }{ 3 }^{ 9 }+^{ 9 }{ C_{ 1\quad } }{ 3 }^{ 8 }(ax)+^{ 9 }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ 3 }^{ 7 }{ (ax) }^{ 2 }+^{ 9 }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{ 3 }^{ 6 }{ (ax) }^{ 3 }+...........\\ Given\quad that\quad coefficients\quad of\quad { x }^{ 2 }=coefficients\quad of\quad { x }^{ 3 }\\ \Rightarrow ^{ 9 }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ 3 }^{ 7 }{ a }^{ 2 }=^{ 9 }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{ 3 }^{ 6 }{ a }^{ 3 }\\ \Rightarrow \frac { 9! }{ 2!.7! } .3=\frac { 9! }{ 6!.3! } .a\\ \Rightarrow \frac { 3 }{ 7 } =\frac { a }{ 3 } \\ \Rightarrow a=\frac { 9 }{ 7 } {/tex}

questions.question: The middle term in the expansion of (1 + x)^2n is
questions.question_raw: The middle term in the expansion of (1 + x)2n is
questions.explanation:
(1 + x)2n = {tex}1+^{2 n} C_{1}(x)+^{2 n} C_{2}(x)^{2}{/tex} + ....(x)2n 
Since 2n is even, its middle term = {tex}\left(\frac{2 n}{2}+1\right){/tex} th term = Tn+1 
We have the general term of (1 + x)2n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{2 n} C_{r}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
Hence Tn+1 = {tex}^{2 n} C_{n}(x)^{n}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^m in 1 + (1 + x) + (1 + x)^2 + .... (1 + x)^n is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of xm in 1 + (1 + x) + (1 + x)2 + .... (1 + x)n is
questions.explanation:
1, (1 + x), (1 + x)2, ... (1 + x)n is a G.P with a = 1 and r = 1 + x and number of terms = n + 1
Hence, 1 + (1 + x) + (1 + x)2 + ... (1 + x)n = {tex}\frac{1\left[(1+x)^{n+1}-1\right]}{(1+x)-1}=\frac{\left[(1+x)^{n+1}-1\right]}{x}{/tex} 
Hence coefficient of xm in the expansion of 1 + (1 + x) + (1 + x)2 + .... (1 + x)n = coefficient of xm+1 in the expansion of (1 + x)n+ 1 - 1 = {tex}^{n+1} C_{m+1}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^n in the expansion of (1 + x)(1 - x)^n is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of xn in the expansion of (1 + x)(1 - x)n is
questions.explanation:
(1 + x)(1 - x)n = (1 + x) ({tex}1-^{n} C_{1} x+^{n} C_{2} x^{2}-^{n} C_{3} x^{3}+^{n} C_{4} x^{4}{/tex} + ..... + {tex}(-1)^{n-1} \cdot^{n} C_{n-1} x^{n-1}+(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n} x^{n}{/tex} 
Coefficient of xn = {tex}(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n}+(-1)^{n-1} \cdot^{n} C_{n-1}{/tex} = {tex}(-1)^{n} \cdot 1+(-1)^{n-1} \cdot n{/tex} = (-1)n (1 - n)

questions.question: ${\left( {\sqrt 5 + 1} \right)^4} + {\left( {\sqrt 5 - 1} \right)^4}$ is
questions.question_raw: {tex}{\left( {\sqrt 5 + 1} \right)^4} + {\left( {\sqrt 5 - 1} \right)^4}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n + (a - b)n 
= {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0} a^{n}+^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}+^{n} C_{3} \quad a^{n-3} b^{3}\right.{/tex} + ..... + {tex}\left.^{n} C_{n} b^{n}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0} a^{n}-^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}-^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}\right.{/tex} + ..... + {tex}(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n} \quad b^{n}{/tex}]
= 2[{tex}^{n} C_{0} \quad a^{n}+^{n} C_{2} \quad a^{n-2} b^{2}+{/tex} ...]
Let a = {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} and b = 1 and n = 4
Now we get {tex}(\sqrt{5}+1)^{4}+(\sqrt{5}-1)^{4}{/tex} = {tex}2\left[^{4} C_{0} (\sqrt{5})^{4}+^{4} C_{2} (\sqrt{5})^{2} 1^{2}+^{4} C_{4} (\sqrt{5})^{0} 1^{4}\right]{/tex}
= 2[25 + 30 + 1] = 112

questions.question: If the coefficients of 2nd, 3rd and 4th terms in the expansion of (1 + x)^n are in A.P. then
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of 2nd, 3rd and 4th terms in the expansion of (1 + x)n are in A.P. then
questions.explanation:
Given that nC1, nC2 and nC3 are in A.P.
Hence we have {tex}^{n} C_{1}+^{n} C_{3}=2 \cdot^{n} C_{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}n+\frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{6}{/tex} = {tex}2 \cdot \frac{n(n-1)}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}n\left[\frac{6+(n-1)(n-2)}{6}\right]{/tex} = n(n - 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 + (n - 1)(n - 2) = 6(n - 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n2 - 3n + 8 = 6n - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n2 - 9n + 14 = 0

questions.question: Coefficient of a^2b^5 in the expansion of (a + b)^3 (a - 2b)^4 is
questions.question_raw: Coefficient of a2b5 in the expansion of (a + b)3 (a - 2b)4 is
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)3(a - 2b)4 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{llll} ^{3} C_{0} a^{3}+^{3} C_{1} a^{2} b+^{3} C_{2} a^{1} b^{2}+^{3} C_{3} b^{3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} [{tex}^{4} C_{0} (a)^{4}+^{4} C_{1} (a)^{3}(-2 b)+^{4} C_{2} (a)^{2}(-2 b)^{2}{/tex} + {tex}^{4} C_{3} (a)^{1}(-2 b)^{3}+^{4} C_{4} (-2 b)^{4}{/tex}]
= (a3 + 3a2b + 3a1b2 + b3)(a4 - 8a3b + 24a2b2 - 32ab3 + 16b4)
Hence we have the sum of the terms containing a2b5 are 3a2b {tex}\times{/tex} 16b4 - 32ab3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3a1b2 + 24a2b2 {tex}\times{/tex} b3 = a2b5 (48 - 96 + 24) = -24a2b5

questions.question: In the expansion of (1 + x)^11, the 5^th term is 24 times the 3^rd term. The value of x is
questions.question_raw: In the expansion of (1 + x)11, the 5th term is 24 times the 3rd term. The value of x is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (1 + x)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r} (x)^{r}{/tex} 
Consider (1 + x)11 
Hence general term Tr+1 = {tex}^{11} C_{r}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
Given T5 = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} T3 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{11} C_{4}(x)^{4}{/tex} = {tex}24 \times^{11} C_{2} (x)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{24 \times^{11} C_{2}}{^{11} C_{4}}{/tex} = (x)2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{24 \times 55}{330}{/tex} = (x)2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 = (x)2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\pm{/tex}2

questions.question: The sum of coefficients of 1^st, 2^nd, and 3^rd in the expansion of $\left( { { x^{ 2 } }+\frac { 1 }{ x } } \right) ^{ n }$ is 46, find n
questions.question_raw: The sum of coefficients  of 1st, 2nd, and 3rd in the expansion of {tex}\left( { { x^{ 2 } }+\frac { 1 }{ x } } \right) ^{ n }{/tex} is 46, find n
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}^{n} C_{0}+^{n} C_{1}+^{n} C_{2}{/tex} = 46
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + n + {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2}{/tex} = 46
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{n(n-1)+2 n+2}{2}{/tex} = 46
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n2 + n - 90 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (n - 9) (n + 10) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 9 since n cannot be negative.

questions.question: If the rth term in the expansion of $\left(\frac{x}{3}-\frac{2}{x^2}\right)^{10}$ contains x^4 then r is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the rth term in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x}{3}-\frac{2}{x^2}\right)^{10}{/tex} contains x4 then r is equal to

Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{x}{3}-\frac{2}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{10} C_{r} \left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{10-r}\left(-\frac{2}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} 
Hence rth term = Tr = {tex}^{10} C_{r-1}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{2}{x^{2}}\right)^{r-1}{/tex} 
Comparing the indices of x in x4 and in Tr, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 11 - r - 2r + 2 = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3r = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 3

questions.question: If 5th term in the expansion of ${ \left( { x^{ 2 }-{ \frac { 1 }{ { { x^{ 3 } } } } } } \right) ^{ n } }$ is independent of x, n is
questions.question_raw: If 5th term in the expansion of  {tex}{ \left( { x^{ 2 }-{ \frac { 1 }{ { { x^{ 3 } } } } } } \right) ^{ n } }{/tex} is independent of x, n is

We have the general term of  (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}{ ^n}{C_{r}}{\left( x \right)^{n - r}}{a^r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}{\left( {{x^2} - \frac{1}{{{x^3}}}} \right)^n}{/tex} 
Here T5 = T4+1 =  {tex}{ ^n}{C_{4}}{\left( {{x^2}} \right)^{n - 4}}{\left( { - \frac{1}{{{x^3}}}} \right)^4}{/tex} 
The term independent of x means index of x is 0.
Comparing the indices of x in xo and in T5, we get 
2n - 8 - 12 = 0

questions.question: If in the binomial expansion of (a + b)^n, $n\ge 5$ the sum of 5^th and 6^th terms are 0, then $\frac { b }{ a } $ is
questions.question_raw: If in the binomial expansion of (a + b)n, {tex}n\ge 5{/tex}  the sum of 5th and 6th terms are 0, then {tex}\frac { b }{ a } {/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n = {tex}^{n} C_{0}a^{n}+^{n} C_{1}a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2}a^{n-2} b^{2}+^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + .... + {tex}^{n} C_{n}b^{n}{/tex} 
Given T5 + T6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{n} C_{4} a^{n-4} b^{4}+^{n} C_{5} a^{n-5} b^{5}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}=-\frac{n !}{5 !(n-5) !} \times \frac{4 ! \cdot(n-4) !}{n !}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}=-\frac{(n-4)}{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{b}{a}=\frac{5}{4-n}{/tex}

Now consider {tex}\left(2+\frac{x}{3}\right)^{4}{/tex}
Here {tex}x=2, a=\frac{x}{3}, n=4{/tex}
{tex}\left(2+\frac{x}{3}\right)^{4}=^{4} C_{0} (2)^{4}+^{4} C_{1} (2)^{3}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right){/tex}{tex}+^{4} C_{2} \quad(2)^{2}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{2}{/tex}{tex}+^{4} C_{3} (2)^{1}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{3}+...{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficients of x^n in the expansion of ${\left( {1 + 2x + 3{x^2} + ..........} \right)^{{\frac {-n}{ 2}}}}$, where n is an even integer is
questions.question_raw: The coefficients of xn in the expansion of {tex}{\left( {1 + 2x + 3{x^2} + ..........} \right)^{{\frac {-n}{ 2}}}}{/tex}, where n is an even integer is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(1+2 x+3 x^{2}+\ldots \ldots \ldots\right)^{\frac{-n}{2}}{/tex} 
Consider S = 1 + 2x + 3x2 + .....
Multiplying S by x, we get Sx = x + 2x2 + 3x3 +....
Now S(1 - x) = 1 + (x + x2 + x3 + ....)
= {tex}1+\frac{x}{1-x}{/tex} [Sum to infinity of a G.P. with a = x and r = x]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = {tex}\frac{1}{1-x}+\frac{x}{(1-x)^{2}}=\frac{1}{(1-x)^{2}}{/tex}
Hence {tex}\left(1+2 x+3 x^{2}+\ldots \ldots \ldots\right)^{\frac{-n}{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{1}{(1-x)^{2}}\right)^{\frac{-n}{2}}{/tex} = (1 - x)n = {tex}1-^{n} C_{1}(x)+^{n} C_{2} (x)^{2}-^{n} C_{3} (x)^{3}{/tex} + ..... + (-1)n nCn (x)n
Hence Coefficient of xn is (-1)n 
Since n is even we get coefficient of xn = 1

questions.question: Find the number of terms in the expansion of [(2x + y)^10 - (2x - y)^10]
questions.question_raw: Find the number of terms in the expansion of [(2x + y)10 - (2x - y)10]
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n - (a - b)n
= [nC0 an + nC1 an-1 b + nC2 an-2 b2 + nC3 an-3 b3 + ... + nCn bn] - [nC0 an - nC1 an-1 b + nC2 an-2 b2 - nC3 an-3 b3 + ... + (-1)n{tex}\cdot{/tex}nCn bn]
= 2[nC1 an-1 b + nC3 an-3 b3 + ...]
If n is even we have (a + b)n - (a - b)n has number of terms = {tex}\frac{n}{2}{/tex}
Here since n = 10
we havenumber of terms = {tex}\frac{n}{2}=\frac{10}{2}{/tex} = 5

questions.question: The index of the power of x that occurs in the ${{\rm{7}}^{{\rm{th}}}}$ term from the end in the expansion of $\left(\frac{2 x}{3}-\frac{3}{2 x}\right)^9$ is
questions.question_raw: The index of the power of x that occurs in the {tex}{{\rm{7}}^{{\rm{th}}}}{/tex} term from the end in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{3}-\frac{3}{2 x}\right)^9{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is {tex} T_{r+1}=^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex}
Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{3}-\frac{3}{2 x}\right)^{9}{/tex}
Here {tex}T_{r+1}=^{9} C_{r} \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)^{9-r}\left(-\frac{3}{2 x}\right)^{r}{/tex}
We have 7 th term from the end=(12-7+2) t h term from the beginning 
Required term is {tex}T_{7}=T_{6+1}=^{9} C_{6} \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)^{9-6}\left(-\frac{3}{2 x}\right)^{6}{/tex} = {tex}^{9} C_{6} \times(2 x)^{3-6} 3^{6-3}=^{9} C_{6} \left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{3} x^{-3}{/tex}

questions.question: The index of the power of x that occurs in the $ {{\rm{6}}^{{\rm{th}}}}$ term in the expansion of $\left(\frac{4 x}{5}-\frac{8}{5 x}\right)^9$ is
questions.question_raw: The index of the power of x that occurs in the {tex} {{\rm{6}}^{{\rm{th}}}}{/tex} term in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}-\frac{8}{5 x}\right)^9{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of
(x + a)n is {tex} T_{ r+1 }=^{ n }{ C_{ r} }\left( x \right) ^{ n-r }{ a }^{ r }{/tex}
Now consider {tex}{ \left( { { \frac { 4x }{ 5 } }-\frac { 8 }{ 5x } } \right) ^{ 9 } }{/tex}
Here {tex}T_{ r+1 }=^{ 9 }{ C_{ r } }\left( \frac { 4x }{ 5 } \right) ^{ 9-r }{ \left( { -\frac { 8 }{ 5x } } \right) }^{ r }{/tex}
Required term is {tex}T_{ 6 }=T_{ 5+1 }=^{ 9 }{ C_{ 5} }\left( \frac { 4x }{ 5 } \right) ^{ 9-5 }{ \left( { -\frac { 8 }{ 5x } } \right) }^{ 5 }{/tex} = {tex}-^{ 9 }{ C_{ 5} }\times \left( \frac { 4 }{ 5 } \right) ^{ 4 }{ \times \left( \frac { 8 }{ 5 } \right) }^{ 5 }{ x }^{ 4-5 }{/tex}
{tex} ={ -^{ 9 }{ C_{ 5} }\times \left( \frac { 4 }{ 5 } \right) ^{ 4 }{ \times \left( \frac { 8 }{ 5 } \right) }^{ 5 }\times }{ x }^{ -1 }{/tex}
Hence the power of x = -1

questions.question: If in the expansion of (1 + x)^43, the coefficients of (2r + 1)th and (r + 2)th terms are equal, then r is equal to
questions.question_raw: If in the expansion of (1 + x)43, the coefficients of (2r + 1)th and (r + 2)th terms are equal, then r is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (1 + x)43 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{43} C_{r}(1)^{43-r}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
T2r+1 = T(2r)+1 = {tex}^{43} C_{2 r}(x)^{2 r}{/tex} 
Tr+2 = T(r+1)+1 = {tex}^{43} C_{r+1} x^{r+1}{/tex} 
Given the coefficient of T2r+1 = coefficient of Tr+2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{43} C_{2 r}=^{43} C_{r+1}{/tex}  [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}^{n} C_{p}=^{n} C_{q}{/tex} then either p = q or p + q = n]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2r + r + 1 = 43 or 2r = r + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3r = 42 or r = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 14 or r = 1

questions.question: The coefficient of x^7 in the expansion of (1 - x)^4(1 + x)^9 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x7 in the expansion of (1 - x)4(1 + x)9 is
questions.explanation:
(1 - x)4 (1 + x)9 = {tex}\left(\begin{array}{lllll} ^{4} C_{0} +^{4} C_{1} (-x)+^{4} C_{2} (-x)^{2}+^{4} C_{3} (-x)^{3}+^{4} C_{4} \left.(-x)^{4}\right) \end{array}\right.{/tex} {tex}\left(^{9} C_{0}+^{9} C_{1}(x)+^{9} C_{2}(x)^{2}+^{9} C_{3}(x)^{3}\right.{/tex} + {tex}^{9} C_{4}(x)^{4}+^{9} C_{5} (x)^{5}+^{9} C_{6} (x)^{6}{/tex} + {tex}-^{9} C_{7} \quad(x)^{7}+^{9} C_{8} \quad(x)^{8}+^{9} C_{9} \quad(x)^{\text {9 }}){/tex} 
= {tex}\left(1-^{4} C_{1}x+^{4} C_{2} x^{2}\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.^{4} C_{3}x^{3}+x^{4}\right){/tex} {tex}(1+^{9} C_{1} x+^{9} C_{2} x^{2}+^{9} C_{3} x^{3}{/tex} + {tex}^{9} C_{4} x^{4}+^{9} C_{5} x^{5}+^{9} C_{6} x^{6}{/tex} + {tex}\left.^{9} C_{7} x^{7}+^{9} C_{8} x^{8}+x^{9}\right){/tex} 
We have the sum of the terms containing x7 are {tex}^{9} C_{7} x^{7} \times 1-^{9} C_{6} x^{6} \times^{4} C_{1} \quad x{/tex} + {tex}^{9} C_{5} x^{5} \times^{4} C_{2} x^{2}-^{9} C_{4} x^{4} \times^{4} C_{3} x^{3}{/tex} + {tex}^{9} C_{3} \quad x^{3} \times x^{4}{/tex} 
= x7({tex}^{9} C_{7} -^{9} C_{6} \times^{4} C_{1} +^{9} C_{5} \times^{4} C_{2}-^{9} C_{4}\times^{4} C_{3} +^{9} C_{3}{/tex} 
= x7(36 - 336 + 756 - 504 + 84) = 36x7

questions.question: If the three successive terms in the expansion of (1 + x)^n have their coefficients as 28, 56, and 70, then n is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the three successive terms in the expansion of (1 + x)n have their coefficients as 28, 56, and 70, then n is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have (1 + x)n = {tex}^{n} C_{0} 1^{n}+^{n} C_{1}(1)^{n-1}(x){/tex} + {tex}^{n} C_{2}(1)^{n-2}(x)^{2}{/tex} + .... + {tex}^{n} C_{r}(1)^{n-r}(x)^{r}{/tex} .... + {tex}^{n} C_{n}(x)^{n}{/tex} 
Suppose the three consecutive terms be Tr-1, Tr and Tr+1, whose coefficients are 28, 56 and 70 respectively
Now we have Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{r}{/tex}, Tr = {tex}^{n} C_{r-1}(x)^{r-1}{/tex} and Tr-1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r-2}(x)^{r-2}{/tex} 
Now {tex}\frac{T_{r}}{T_{r-1}}=\frac{^n C_{r-1}}{n C_{r-2}}=\frac{56}{28}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{T_{r+1}}{T_{r}}=\frac{^n C_{r}}{n C_{r-1}} \quad(x)^{r}=\frac{70}{56}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{n !}{(n+1-r) !(r-1) !} \times \frac{(n+2-r) !(r-2) !}{n !} {/tex} = 2 and {tex}\frac{n !}{(n-r) ! \cdot r !} \times \frac{(n+1-r) !(r-1) !}{n !}=\frac{5}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(n+2-r)}{(r-1)}{/tex} = 2 and {tex}\frac{(n+1-r)}{r}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n - r + 2 = 2r - 2 and 4n - 4r + 4 = 5r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3r - n = 4 ....(i) and 9r - 4n = 4 ....(ii)
Solving (i) and (ii), we get n = 8

questions.question: The index of the power of x that occurs in the 5th term in the expansion of $\left(2 x^2-\frac{1}{3 x^2}\right)^8$ is
questions.question_raw: The index of the power of x that occurs in the 5th term in the expansion of {tex}\left(2 x^2-\frac{1}{3 x^2}\right)^8{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{3 x^{2}}\right)^{8}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}C_{r} \left(2 x^{2}\right)^{8-r}\left(-\frac{1}{3 x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} 
Required term is T5 = T4+1 = {tex}^{8} C_{4}\left(2 x^{2}\right)^{8-4}\left(-\frac{1}{3 x^{2}}\right)^{4}{/tex} = {tex}^{8} C_{4}\times(2)^{4} \times\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{4} x^{8-8}{/tex} = {tex}^{8} C_{4}\times\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{4} x^{0}{/tex}
Hence the power of x = 0

questions.question: Find the number of terms in the expansion of $\left[ { \left( \sqrt { x } +\sqrt { y } \right) }^{ 11 }+{ \left( \sqrt { x } -\sqrt { y } \right) }^{ 11 } \right] $
questions.question_raw: Find the number of terms in the expansion of {tex}\left[ { \left( \sqrt { x } +\sqrt { y } \right) }^{ 11 }+{ \left( \sqrt { x } -\sqrt { y } \right) }^{ 11 } \right] {/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n + (a - b)n 
= {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0}a^{n}+^{n} C_{1}a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2}a^{n-2} b^{2}\right.{/tex} + {tex}^{n} C_{3}a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + ..... + {tex}\left.^{n} C_{n}b^{n}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0}a^{n}-^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}-^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}\right.{/tex} + .... + {tex}(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n}b^{n}{/tex}]
= 2[{tex}^{n} C_{0}a^{n}+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}{/tex} + ....]
If n is odd we have (a + b)n + (a - b)n has number of terms = {tex}\frac{n+1}{2}{/tex} 
Here since n = 11 we have number of terms = {tex}\frac{n+1}{2}=\frac{11+1}{2}{/tex} = 6

questions.question: If a and b are coefficients of x^n and x^n+1 respectively in the expansion of (1 + x)^2n+1, then
questions.question_raw: If a and b are coefficients of xn and xn+1 respectively in the expansion of (1 + x)2n+1, then
questions.explanation:
(1 + x)2n+1 = {tex}1+^{2 n+1} C_{1}(x)+^{2 n+1} C_{2} (x)^{2}{/tex} + ... + (x)2n+1 
Given that coefficient of xn = a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{2 n+1} C_{n}{/tex} = a ....(i)
And coefficient of xn+1 = b {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{2 n+1} C_{n+1}{/tex} = b ....(ii)
Now {tex}\frac{^{2 n+1} C_{n+1}}{^{2 n+1} C_{n}}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(2 n+1) !}{(n+1) ! \cdot n !} \times \frac{(n+1) ! \cdot n !}{(2 n+1) !}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{b}{a}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = {tex}\frac{b}{a}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = b

questions.question: The coefficient of x^5 in the expansion of (1 + x + x^2)^3 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x5 in the expansion of (1 + x + x2)3 is
questions.explanation:
(1 + x + x2)3 = [1 + (x + x2)]3 = {tex}^{3} C_{0}+^{3} C_{1} \left(x+x^{2}\right){/tex} + {tex}^{3} C_{2}\left(x+x^{2}\right)^{2}+^{3} C_{3}\left(x+x^{2}\right)^{3}{/tex} 
= 1 + 3(x + x2) + 3(x2 + 2x3 + x4) + (x3 + 3x4 + 3x5 + x6)
Hence coefficient of x5 = 3

questions.question: $\sum _{ r=0 }^{ n }{ { 4 }^{ r } } .^{ n }{ C_{ r} }$is equal to
questions.question_raw: {tex}\sum _{ r=0 }^{ n }{ { 4 }^{ r } } .^{ n }{ C_{ r} }{/tex}is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sum_{r=0}^{n} 4^{r} .^{n} C_{r}{/tex} = {tex}4^{0} \cdot^{n} C_{0}+4^{1} \cdot^{n} C_{1}+4^{2} \cdot^{n} C_{2}{/tex} + ... + {tex}4^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n}{/tex} 
= {tex}1+4 .^{n} C_{1}+4^{2} .^{n} C_{2}{/tex} + .... + {tex}4^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n}{/tex} 
= (1 + 4)n = 5n

questions.question: Find the middle term in the expansion of ${ \left( { \frac { 2a }{ 3 } -{ \frac { 3 }{ { 2a } } } } \right) ^{ 6 } }$
questions.question_raw: Find the middle term in the expansion of {tex}{ \left( { \frac { 2a }{ 3 } -{ \frac { 3 }{ { 2a } } } } \right) ^{ 6 } }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since n = 6 is even, its middle term is the 4th term.
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{2 a}{3}-\frac{3}{2 a}\right)^{6}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{6} C_{r}\left(\frac{2 a}{3}\right)^{6-r}\left(-\frac{3}{2 a}\right)^{r}{/tex} 
Required term is T4 = T3+1 = {tex}^{6} C_{3} \quad\left(\frac{2 a}{3}\right)^{6-3}\left(-\frac{3}{2 a}\right)^{3}{/tex} = -20 {tex}\times{/tex} (2a)0 {tex}\times{/tex} (3)0 = -20

questions.question: If the 17^th and 18^th terms in the expansion (2 + a)^50 are equal then a is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the 17th and 18th terms in the expansion (2 + a)50 are equal then a is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (2 + a)50 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{50} C_{r} \quad(2)^{50-r}(a)^{r}{/tex} 
Now T17 = T16+1 = {tex}^{50} C_{16}(2)^{50-16}(a)^{16}{/tex} and T18 = T17+1 = {tex}^{50} C_{17}(2)^{50-17}(a)^{17}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{^{50} C_{17}}{^{50} C_{16}}=\frac{(2)^{34}(a)^{16}}{(2)^{33}(a)^{17}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(50) !}{(33) ! .17 !} \times \frac{(34) ! \cdot 16 !}{(50) !}=\frac{2}{a}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{34}{17}=\frac{2}{a}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 1

questions.question: Find the coefficient of x in the expansion of (1 - 3x + 7x^2)(1 - x)^16
questions.question_raw: Find the coefficient of x in the expansion of (1 - 3x + 7x2)(1 - x)16
questions.explanation:
(1 - 3x + 7x2)(1 - x)16 = (1 - 3x + 7x2)({tex}^{16} C_{0}-^{16} C_{1}(x)+^{16} C_{2} (x)^{2}-^{16} C_{3} (x)^{3}{/tex} + {tex}^{16} C_{4} \quad(x)^{4}-^{16} C_{5} \quad(x)^{5}{/tex} + ..... + {tex}(-1)^{n_{16}} C_{16}(x)^{16}{/tex})
= (1 - 3x + 7x2)(1 - 16x + 120(x)2 - .....)
Therefore the coefficient of x in the product (1 - 3x + 7x2)(1 - x)16 = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-16) + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-3) = -19

questions.question: Find the 9^th term in the expansion of ${ \left( { { \frac { x }{ a } }-\frac { 3a }{ x^{ 2 } } } \right) ^{ 12 } }$
questions.question_raw: Find the 9th term in the expansion of {tex}{ \left( { { \frac { x }{ a } }-\frac { 3a }{ x^{ 2 } } } \right) ^{ 12 } }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r}(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider {tex}\left(\frac{x}{a}-\frac{3 a}{x^{2}}\right)^{12}{/tex} 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}C_{r}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)^{12-r}\left(-\frac{3 a}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} 
Hence 9th term = T6+1 = {tex}^{12} C_{8} \quad\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)^{12-8}\left(-\frac{3 a}{x^{2}}\right)^{8}{/tex} = {tex}^{12} C_{8} \times 3^{8} \times(x)^{-12} a^{4}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^5 in the expansion of (1 - x)^7 (1 + 2x)^6 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x5 in the expansion of (1 - x)7 (1 + 2x)6 is
questions.explanation:
We have (1 - x)7 = {tex}^{7} C_{0}-^{7} C_{1} (x)+^{7} C_{2} (x)^{2}-^{7} C_{3} (x)^{3}{/tex} + {tex}^7 C_{4}(x)^{4}-^{7} C_{5} (x)^{5}+^{7} C_{6} (x)^{6}-^{7} C_{7} (x)^{7}{/tex} 
= 1 - 7x + 21x2 - 35x3 + 35x4 - 21x5 + 7x6 - x7 
(1 + 2x)6 = {tex}^{6} C_{0}+^{6} C_{1} (2 x)+^{6} C_{2}(2 x)^{2}{/tex} + {tex}^{6} C_{3}(2 x)^{3}+^{6} C_{4} (2 x)^{4}{/tex} + {tex}^{6} C_{5} (2 x)^{5}+^{6} C_{6} (2 x)^{6}{/tex} 
= 1 + 12x + 60x2 + 160x3 + 240x4 + 192x5 + 64x6 
Now (1 - x)7(1 + 2x)6 = (1 - 7x + 21x2 - 35x3 + 35x4 - 21x5 + 7x6 - x7)(1 + 12x + 60x2 + 160x3 + 240x4 + 192x5 + 64x6)
Therefore the coefficient of x5 in the product (1 - x)7 (1 + 2x)6 is 192 - 240 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 + 160 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 - 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 + 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 - 21 = 171

questions.question: ${(\sqrt 5 + 1)^{2n + 1}} - {(\sqrt 5 - 1)^{2n + 1}}$ is
questions.question_raw: {tex}{(\sqrt 5 + 1)^{2n + 1}} - {(\sqrt 5 - 1)^{2n + 1}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have {tex}(a+b)^{n}-(a-b)^{n}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccccccccccc}{n} {C_{0}} & {a^{n}+^{n} C_{1}} & {a^{n-1} b+^{n}} & {C_{2}} & {a^{n-2} b^{2}+^{n} C_{3}} & {a^{n-3} b^{3}+\ldots \ldots \ldots +}^{n} C_{n} & {b^{n}}\end{array}\right]-{/tex}
{tex}-\left[ ^{ n }{ C_{ 0\quad } }{ a }^{ n }-^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } }{ a }^{ n-1 }b+^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ a }^{ n-2 }{ b }^{ 2 }-^{ n }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{ a }^{ n-3 }{ b }^{ 3 }+....................+\left( -1 \right) ^{ n }.^{ n }{ C_{ n } }{b }^{ n } \right] {/tex}
{tex} \\ =2\left[ ^{ n }{ C_{ 1\quad } }{ a }^{ n-1 }b+^{ n }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{ a }^{ n-3 }{ b }^{ 3 }+.............. \right]{/tex}
{tex}Let\quad a=\sqrt { 5 } \quad and\quad b=1\quad and\quad n=2n+1{/tex}
Now we get {tex}(\sqrt{5}+1)^{2 n+1}-(\sqrt{5}-1)^{2 n+1}{/tex}
{tex}=2\left[^{2 n+1} C_{1}(\sqrt{3})^{2 n}+^{2 n+1} C_{3}(\sqrt{3})^{2 n-2} 1^{3}+^{2 n+1} C_{5} (\sqrt{3})^{2 n-4} 1^{5}+\ldots \right.]{/tex}
{tex}=2\left[ ^{ 2n+1 }{ C_{ 1\quad } }{ \left( 3 \right) }^{ n }+^{ 2n+1 }{ C_{ 3\quad } }{ \left( 3 \right) }^{ n-1 }+^{ 2n+1 }{ C_{ 5\quad } }{ \left( 3 \right) }^{ n-2 }+.............. \right]{/tex}
=2(a positive integer)
Hence we have {tex}(\sqrt{5}+1)^{2 n}-(\sqrt{5}-1)^{2 n+1}{/tex} is an even positive integer

questions.question: ${(\sqrt 3 + 1)^{2n}} + {(\sqrt 3 - 1)^{2n}}$is
questions.question_raw: {tex}{(\sqrt 3 + 1)^{2n}} + {(\sqrt 3 - 1)^{2n}}{/tex}is
questions.explanation:
We have {tex}(a+b)^{n}+(a-b)^{n}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[{}^nC_0a^n+^nC_1\quad a^{n-1}b+^nC_2\quad a^{n-2}b^2+^nC_3\quad a^{n-3}b^3+\dots\dots\dots\dots+^nC_n\quad b^n\right]+{/tex}
{tex}\begin{bmatrix}{}^nC_0&a^n-^nC_1a^{n-1}b+^nC_2&a^{n-2}b^2-^nC_3&a^{n-3}b^3+\dots\dots+(-1)^n\cdot^nC_n\end{bmatrix}^n{/tex}
{tex}\\ =2\left[ ^{ n }{ C_{ 0\quad } }{ a }^{ n }+^{ n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ a }^{ n-2 }{ b }^{ 2 }+.............. \right] {/tex}
Let {tex}a=\sqrt{3}{/tex} and b=1 and n=2n
{tex}{ (\sqrt { 3 } +1)^{ 2n } }+{ (\sqrt { 3 } -1)^{ 2n } }=2\left[ ^{ 2n }{ C_{ 0 } }{ \left( \sqrt { 3 } \right) }^{ 2n }+^{ 2n }{ C_{ 2 } }{ \left( \sqrt { 3 } \right) }^{ 2n-2 }{ 1 }^{ 2 }+^{ 2n }{ C_{ 4 } }{ \left( \sqrt { 3 } \right) }^{ 2n-4 }{ 1 }^{ 4 }+......\right]{/tex}
{tex} \\ =2\left[ ^{ 2n }{ C_{ 0\quad } }{ \left( 3 \right) }^{ n }+^{ 2n }{ C_{ 2\quad } }{ \left( 3 \right) }^{ n-1 }+^{ 2n }{ C_{ 4\quad } }{ \left( 3 \right) }^{ n-2 }+.............. \right] {/tex}
=2(a positive integer)
Hence we have {tex}(\sqrt{3}+1)^{2 n}+(\sqrt{3}-1)^{2 n}{/tex}} is always an even positive integer

questions.question: ${(\sqrt 3 + 1)^{2n + 1}} + {(\sqrt 3 - 1)^{2n + 1}}$ is
questions.question_raw: {tex}{(\sqrt 3 + 1)^{2n + 1}} + {(\sqrt 3 - 1)^{2n + 1}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n + (a - b)n 
= {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0}a^{n}+^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n}\right.{/tex} + {tex}^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}+^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + .... + {tex}\left.^{n} C_{n}b^{n}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0}a^{n}-^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}\right.{/tex} - {tex}^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + .... + {tex}(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n} \quad b^{n}{/tex}]
= 2[{tex}^{n} C_{0} a^{n}+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}{/tex} + ....]
Let a = {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} and b = 1 and n = 2n + 1
Now we get {tex}(\sqrt{3}+1)^{2 n+1}+(\sqrt{3}-1)^{2 n+1}{/tex} = 2[{tex}^{2 n+1} C_{0}(\sqrt{3})^{2 n+1}+^{2 n+1} C_{2}(\sqrt{3})^{2 n-1} 1^{2}{/tex} + {tex}^{2 n+1} C_{4}(\sqrt{3})^{2 n-3} 1^{4}{/tex} + ....]
Since there are odd powers of {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} we have {tex}(\sqrt{3}+1)^{2 n+1}+(\sqrt{3}-1)^{2 n+1}{/tex} is always an irrational number.

questions.question: ${(\sqrt 3 + 1)^{2n}} - {(\sqrt 3 - 1)^{2n}}$ is
questions.question_raw: {tex}{(\sqrt 3 + 1)^{2n}} - {(\sqrt 3 - 1)^{2n}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n - (a - b)n 
= {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0}a^{n}+^{n} C_{1}a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2}a^{n-2} b^{2}\right.{/tex} + ..... + {tex}^{n} C_{n}b^{n}{/tex}] - [{tex}^{n} C_{0}a^{n}-^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}{/tex} - {tex}^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + .... + {tex}(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n} b^{n}{/tex}]
= 2[{tex}^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + .....]
Let a = {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} and b = 1 and n = 2n
Now we get {tex}(\sqrt{3}+1)^{2 n}+(\sqrt{3}-1)^{2 n}{/tex} = {tex}2\left[^{2 n} C_{1} (\sqrt{3})^{2 n-1} \cdot 1+^{2 n} C_{3} (\sqrt{3})^{2 n-3} 1^{3}\right.{/tex} + {tex}^{2 n} C_{5} (\sqrt{3})^{2 n-5} 1^{5}{/tex} + ....]
Since odd powers of {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} will make it an irrational number.
Hence we have {tex}(\sqrt{3}+1)^{2 n}+(\sqrt{3}-1)^{2 n}{/tex} is always an irrational number and it will be positive.

questions.question: If n is a positive integer, then ${(\sqrt 3 + i)^n} + {(\sqrt 3 - i)^n}$ is
questions.question_raw: If n is a positive integer, then {tex}{(\sqrt 3 + i)^n} + {(\sqrt 3 - i)^n}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have (a + b)n + (a - b)n 
= {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0} a^{n}+^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b\right.{/tex} + {tex}^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}+^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + ..... + {tex}^{n} C_{n} b^{n}{/tex}] + {tex}\left[^{n} C_{0} a^{n}-^{n} C_{1} a^{n-1} b+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}\right.{/tex} - {tex}^{n} C_{3} a^{n-3} b^{3}{/tex} + .....{tex}\left.(-1)^{n} \cdot^{n} C_{n} \quad b^{n}\right]{/tex}
= 2[{tex}^{n} C_{0} a^{n}+^{n} C_{2} a^{n-2} b^{2}{/tex} + ....]
Let a = {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} and b = i
Now we get {tex}(\sqrt{3}+i)^{n}+(\sqrt{3}-i)^{n}{/tex} = 2[{tex}^{n} C_{0}(\sqrt{3})^{n}+^{n} C_{2}(\sqrt{3})^{n-2} i^{2}{/tex} + {tex}^{n} C_{4}(\sqrt{3})^{n-4} i^{4}{/tex} + ....]
Since i2n = (-1)n = {tex}\pm 1{/tex} 
Hence we have {tex}(\sqrt{3}+i)^{n}+(\sqrt{3}-i)^{n}{/tex} is always a real number and it will be positive or negative depending on the value of n is even or odd.

questions.question: Find the coefficient of x^6y^3 in the expansion of ${ \left( { x+2y } \right) ^{ 9 } }$
questions.question_raw: Find the coefficient of  x6y3 in the expansion of {tex}{ \left( { x+2y } \right) ^{ 9 } }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of {tex}(x+a)^{n} \quad \text { is } \quad T_{r+1}=^{n} C_{r} \quad(x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex}
Now consider {tex}(x+2 y)^{9}{/tex}
Here {tex}T_{r+1}=^{9} C_{r} \quad(x)^{9-r}(2 y)^{r}=^{9} C_{r} \quad(2)^{r}(x)^{9-r}(y)^{r}{/tex}
Comparing the indices of x as well as y in {tex}x^{6} y^{3}{/tex} and in {tex}T_{r+1}{/tex} , we get 
{tex}(x)^{9-r}(y)^{r^{2}}=x^{6} y^{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r=3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad T_{4}=T_{3+1}=^{9} C_{3} \quad(x)^{9-3}(2 y)^{3}{/tex}
Hence coefficient of {tex}x^{6} y^{3}=8 .^{9} C_{3}=672{/tex}

questions.question: If the 21st and 22nd terms in the expansion of (1 + x)^44 are equal, find x
questions.question_raw: If the 21st and 22nd terms in the expansion of (1 + x)44 are equal, find x
questions.explanation:
We have the general term of (x + a)n is Tr+1 = {tex}^{n} C_{r} (x)^{n-r} a^{r}{/tex} 
Now consider (1 + x)44 
Here Tr+1 = {tex}^{44} C_{r}(1)^{44-r}(x)^{r}{/tex} 
So T21 = T20+1 = {tex}^{44} C_{20}(x)^{20}{/tex} and T22 = T22+1 = {tex}^{44} C_{21}(x)^{21}{/tex} 
Given T21 = T22 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}^{44} C_{20} (x)^{20}=^{44} C_{21} (x)^{21}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{^{44} C_{20}}{^{44} C_{21}}=\frac{(44) !}{(20) ! ! 24 !} \frac{(21) ! \cdot 23 !}{(44) !}=\frac{21}{24}=\frac{7}{8}{/tex}

questions.question: The integral part of ${(\sqrt 2 + 1)^6}$ is
questions.question_raw: The integral part of {tex}{(\sqrt 2 + 1)^6}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have (1 + x)n = {tex}1+^{n} C_{1} (x)+^{n} C_{2} (x)^{2}{/tex} + ....+ (x)n 
Hence {tex}(\sqrt{2}+1)^{6}{/tex} = 1 + {tex}^{6} C_{1} (\sqrt{2})+^{6} C_{2} (\sqrt{2})^{2}+^{6} C_{3} (\sqrt{2})^{3}{/tex} + {tex}^{6} C_{4} (\sqrt{2})^{4}+^{6} C_{5} (\sqrt{2})^{5}+(\sqrt{2})^{6}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\sqrt{2}+1)^{6}{/tex}  = 1 + 6({tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) + 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 2({tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) + 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 + 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 4({tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}) + 8
= 99 + 70{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} 
Hence integral part of {tex}(\sqrt{2}+1)^{6}{/tex} = 99

questions.question: The greatest term in the expansion of ${(3 + 2x)^9}$,when x = 1, is
questions.question_raw: The greatest term in the expansion of {tex}{(3 + 2x)^9}{/tex},when x = 1, is
questions.explanation:
We have the general term in the expansion of {tex}(3+2 x)^{9}{/tex} is given by 
{tex}T_{r+1}=^{9} C_{r} \quad(3)^{9-r}(2 x)^{r}{/tex}
Now {tex}\frac{T_{r+1}}{T_{r}}=\frac{^{9} C_{r} \quad(3)^{9-r}(2 x)^{r}}{^{9} C_{r-1} \quad(3)^{10-r}(2 x)^{r-1}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{9 !(3)^{9-r}(2 x)^{r}}{(9-r) ! \cdot r !} \times \frac{(10-r) !(r-1) !}{9 !(3)^{10-r}(2 x)^{r-1}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(10-r) \cdot 2 x}{3 r}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2(10-r)}{3 r}{/tex} since given x=1
Now {tex}\frac{T_{r+1}}{T_{r}} \geq 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{(20-2 r)}{3 r} \geq 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 20-2 r \geq 3 r{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r \leq \frac{20}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r \leq 4{/tex}
Hence the maximum value of r is 4
Now {tex}T_{5}=T_{4+1}=^{9} C_{4} \quad(3)^{9-4}(2 x)^{4}=126 \times 243 \times 16(x)^{4}=489888{/tex}

questions.question: The number 111111…………..1 (91 times)
questions.question_raw: The number 111111…………..1 (91 times)
questions.explanation:
111...111(91times) can be expressed as:-
{tex}\frac{1}{9}\left(10^{91}-1\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{9}\left(10^{7}-1\right) \times x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1111111 {tex}\times{/tex} x
Where x = (107)12 + (107)11 + ... + 1

questions.question: The coefficient of x^18 in the product (1 + x)(1 - x)^10(1 + x + x^2)^9 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x18 in the product (1 + x)(1 - x)10(1 + x + x2)9 is
questions.explanation:
Given expression is
(1 + x) (1 - x)10 [(1 + x + x2)9
= (1 + x) (1 - x) [(1 - x) (1 + x + x2)]9
= (1 - x2) (1 - x3)9
Now, coefficient of x18 in the product
(1 + x) (1 - x)10 (1 + x + x2)9
= coefficient of x18 in the product (1 - x2) (1 - x3)9
= coefficient of x18 in (1 - x3)9 - coefficient of x16 in (1 - x3)9
Since, (r + 1)th term in the expansion of
(1 - x3)9 is 9Cr (-x3)r = 9Cr (- 1)r x3r
Now, for x18, 3r = 18 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
and for x16, 3r = 16
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad r=\frac{16}{3} \notin N{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required coefficient is {tex}^{9} C_{6}=\frac{9 !}{6 ! 3 !}=\frac{9 \times 8 \times 7}{3 \times 2}=84{/tex}

questions.question: If the coefficients of x^2 and x^3 are both zero, in the expansion of the expression (1 + ax + bx^2) (1 - 3x)^1^5 in powers of x, then the ordered pair (a, b) is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of x2 and x3 are both zero, in the expansion of the expression (1 + ax + bx2) (1 - 3x)15 in powers of x, then the ordered pair (a, b) is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given expression is (1 + ax + bx2) (1 - 3x)15. In the expansion of binomial (1 - 3x)15, the (r + 1)th term is
{tex}T_{r+1}=^{15} C_{r}(-3 x)^{r}=^{15} C_{r}(-3)^{r} x^{r}{/tex}
Now, coefficient of x2, in the expansion of (1 + ax + bx2) (1 - 3x)15 is
{tex}^{15} C_{2}(-3)^{2}+a^{15} C_{1}(-3)^{1}+b^{15} C_{0}(-3)^{0}=0{/tex} (given)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (105 {tex}\times{/tex} 9) - 45a + b = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 45a - b = 945 ...(i)
Similarly, the coefficient of x3, in the expansion of
(1 + ax + bx2) (1 - 3x)15 is
{tex}^{15} C_{3}(-3)^{3}+a^{15} C_{2}(-3)^{2}+b^{15} C_{1}(-3)^{1}=0{/tex} (given)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -12285 + 945a - 45b = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 63a - 3b = 819
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 21a - b = 273 ...(ii)
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
24a = 672 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 28
So, b = 315
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a, b) = (28, 315)

questions.question: The term independent of x in the expansion of $\left(\frac{1}{60}-\frac{x^{8}}{81}\right) \cdot\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: The term independent of x in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{1}{60}-\frac{x^{8}}{81}\right) \cdot\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Let a binomial {tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}{/tex}, it's (r + 1)th term
{tex}=T_{r+1}=^{6} C_{r}\left(2 x^{2}\right)^{6-r}\left(-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex}
{tex}=^{6} C_{r}(-3)^{r}(2)^{6-r} x^{12-2 r-2 r}{/tex}
{tex}=^{6} C_{r}(-3)^{r}(2)^{6-r} x^{12-4 r}{/tex} ...(i)
Now, the term independent of x in the expansion of
{tex}\left(\frac{1}{60}-\frac{x^{8}}{81}\right)\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}{/tex}
= the term independent of x in the expansion of {tex}\frac{1}{60}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}{/tex} + the term independent of x in the expansion of {tex}-\frac{x^{8}}{81}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{^{6} C_{3}}{60}(-3)^{3}(2)^{6-3} x^{12-4(3)}{/tex} [put r = 3]
{tex}+\left(-\frac{1}{81}\right)^{6} C_{5}(-3)^{5}(2)^{6-5} x^{12-4(5)} x^{8}{/tex} [put r = 5]
{tex}=\frac{1}{3}(-3)^{3} 2^{3}+\frac{3^{5} \times 2(6)}{81}{/tex}
= 36 - 72 = -36

questions.question: The smallest natural number n, such that the coefficient of x in the expansion of $\left(x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{n} \text { is }^{n} C_{23}$, is
questions.question_raw: The smallest natural number n, such that the coefficient of x in the expansion of {tex}\left(x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{n} \text { is }^{n} C_{23}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
Given binomial is {tex}\left(x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{n}{/tex}, its (r + 1)th term, is {tex}T_{r+1}=^{n} C_{r}\left(x^{2}\right)^{n-r}\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{r}=^{n} C_{r} x^{2 n-2 r} \frac{1}{x^{3 r}}{/tex} {tex}=^{n} C_{r} x^{2 n-2 r-3 r}=^{n} C_{r} x^{2 n-5 r}{/tex}
For the coefficient of x,
2n - 5r = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}2n = 5r + 1 ...(i)
As coefficient of x is given as nC23, then either r = 23 or n - r - 23.
If r = 23, then from Eq. (i), we get
2n = 5(23) + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2n = 115 + 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}2n = 116 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}n = 58
If n - r = 23, then from Eq. (i) on replacing the value of ‘
r', we get 2n = 5(n - 23) + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2n = 5n - 115 + 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3n = 114 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 38
So, the required smallest natural number n = 38

questions.question: If some three consecutive coefficients in the binomial expansion of (x+1)^n in powers of x are in the ratio 2:15:70, then the average of these three coefficients is
questions.question_raw: If some three consecutive coefficients in the binomial expansion of (x+1)n in powers of x are in the ratio 2:15:70, then the average of these three coefficients is
questions.explanation:
Given binomial is (x + 1)n, whose general term, is
Tr+1 = nCrxr
According to the question, we have
{tex}^{n} C_{r-1}:^{n} C_{r}:^{n} C_{r+1}{/tex} = 2 : 15 : 70
Now, {tex}\frac{^{n} C_{r-1}}{^{n} C_{r}}=\frac{2}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\frac{n !}{(r-1) !(n-r+1) !}}{\frac{n !}{r !(n-r) !}}=\frac{2}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{r}{n-r+1}=\frac{2}{15} \Rightarrow 15 r=2 n-2 r+2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2n - 17r + 2 = 0 ...(i)
Similarly, {tex}\frac{^{n} C_{r}}{^{n} C_{r+1}}=\frac{15}{70} \Rightarrow \frac{\frac{n !}{r !(n-r) !}}{\frac{n !}{(r+1) !(n-r-1) !}}=\frac{3}{14}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{r+1}{n-r}=\frac{3}{14} \Rightarrow 14 r+14=3 n-3 r{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3n - 17r - 14 = 0 ...(ii)
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
n - 16 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}n = 16 and r = 2
Now, the average {tex}=\frac{^{16} C_{1}+^{16} C_{2}+^{16} C_{3}}{3}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{16+120+560}{3}=\frac{696}{3}=232{/tex}

questions.question: If the fourth term in the binomial expansion of $\left(\frac{2}{x}+x^{\log _{8} x}\right)^{6}(x>0) \text { is } 20 \times 8^{7}$, then the value of x is
questions.question_raw: If the fourth term in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{2}{x}+x^{\log _{8} x}\right)^{6}(x>0) \text { is } 20 \times 8^{7}{/tex}, then the value of x is
questions.explanation:
Given binomial is {tex}\left(\frac{2}{x}+x^{\log _{8} x}\right)^{6}{/tex}
Since, general term in the expansion of (x+ a)n is Tr+1=nCrxn-rar
{tex}\therefore \quad T_{4}=T_{3+1}=^{6} C_{3}\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^{6-3}\left(x^{\log _{8} x}\right)^{3}=20 \times 8^{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 20\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^{3} x^{3 \log _{8} x}=20 \times 8^{7}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} 6C3 = 20]
{tex}\Rightarrow 2^{3} x^{\left[3\left(\log _{8} x\right)-3\right]}=\left(2^{3}\right)^{7} \Rightarrow x^{\left(\frac{3}{3} \log _{2} x-3\right)}=\left(2^{3}\right)^{6}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \log _{a^{n}}(x)=\frac{1}{n} \log _{a} x \text { for } x>0 ; a>0, \neq 1\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{\left(\log _{2} x-3\right)}=2^{18}{/tex}
On taking log2 both sides, we get
(log2x-3) log2x = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (log2x)2 - 3 log2 x - 18 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (log2x)2 - 6 log2x + 3 log2x - 18 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log2x (log2x - 6) + 3 (log2x - 6) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (log2x - 6) (log2x + 3) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log2x = -3, 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2-3, 26 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{8}{/tex}, 82

questions.question: If the fourth term in the binomial expansion of $\left(x^{\sqrt {\left (\frac{1}{1+\log _{10} x}\right)}}+x^{\frac{1}{12}}\right)^{6}$ is equal to 200, and x > 1, then the value of x is
questions.question_raw: If the fourth term in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(x^{\sqrt {\left (\frac{1}{1+\log _{10} x}\right)}}+x^{\frac{1}{12}}\right)^{6}{/tex} is equal to 200, and x > 1, then the value of x is
questions.explanation:
Given binomial is {tex}\left(\sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{1+\log _{10} x}\right)}+x^{\frac{1}{12}}\right)^{6}{/tex}
Since, the fourth term in the given expansion is 200.
{tex}\therefore^{6} C_{3}\left(x^{\frac{1}{1+\log _{10} x}}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(x^{\frac{1}{12}}\right)^{3}=200{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 20 \times x^{\left[\frac{3}{2\left(1+\log _{10} x\right)}+\frac{1}{4}\right]}=200{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{\frac{3}{2\left(1+\log _{10} x\right)}+\frac{1}{4}}=10{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\frac{3}{2\left(1+\log _{10} x\right)}+\frac{1}{4}\right] \log _{10} x=1{/tex}[applying log10 both sides]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}[6 + (1 + log10x)] log10x = 4(1 + log10s)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (7 + log10x) log10x = 4 + 4 log10x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t2 + 7t = 4 + 4t [let log10x = t]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t2 + 3t - 4 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 1, -4 = log10x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 10, 10-4
Since, x > 1
x = 10

questions.question: The sum of the coefficients of all even degree terms is x in the expansion of $(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6},(x>1)$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: The sum of the coefficients of all even degree terms is x in the expansion of {tex}(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6},(x>1){/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given expression is {tex}(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}{/tex}
{tex}=2\left[^{6} C_{0} x^{6}+^{6} C_{2} x^{4}(\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{2}\right.{/tex} {tex}\left.+^{6} C_{4} x^{2}(\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{4}+^{6} C_{6}(\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}\right]{/tex}
{{tex}\because{/tex} (a + b)n + (a - b)n = 2[nC0an + nC2an-2b2 + nC4an-4b4 + ...]}
{tex}=2\left[^{6} C_{0} x^{6}+^{6} C_{2} x^{4}\left(x^{3}-1\right)+6 C_{4} x^{2}\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{2}+^{6} C_{6}\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{3}\right]{/tex}
The sum of the terms with even power of x
{tex}=2\left[^{6} C_{0} x^{6}+^{6} C_{2}\left(-x^{4}\right)+^{6} C_{4} x^{8}+^{6} C_{4} x^{2}+^{6} C_{6}\left(-1-3 x^{6}\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}=2\left[^{6} C_{0} x^{6}-^{6} C_{2} x^{4}+^{6} C_{4} x^{8}+^{6} C_{4} x^{2}-1-3 x^{6}\right]{/tex}
Now, the required sum of the coefficients of even powers of x in
{tex}(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{6}{/tex}
{tex}=2\left[^{6} C_{0}-^{6} C_{2}+^{6} C_{4}+^{6} C_{4}-1-3\right]{/tex}
= 2[1 - 15 + 15 + 15 - 1 - 3] = 2(15 - 3) = 24

questions.question: The total number of irrational terms in the binomial expansion of (7^1/5 - 3^1/10)^60 is
questions.question_raw: The total number of irrational terms in the binomial expansion of (71/5 - 31/10)60 is
questions.explanation:
The general term in the binomial expansion of (a + b)n is Tr+1 = nCr an-rbr
So, the general term in the binomial expansion of (71/5 - 31/10)60 is
{tex}T_{r+1}=^{60} C_{r}\left(7^{1/5}\right)^{60-r}\left(-3^{1 / 10}\right)^{r}{/tex}
{tex}=^{60} C_{r} 7^{\frac{60-r}{5}}(-1)^{r} 3^{\frac{r}{10}}=(-1)^{r}. ^{60} C_{r} 7^{12-\frac{r}{5}} 3^{\frac{r}{10}}{/tex}
The possible non-negative integral values of 'r' for which {tex}\frac{r}{5}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{r}{10}{/tex} are integer, where {tex}r \leq 60{/tex}, are r = 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  There are 7 rational terms in the binomial expansion and remaining 61 - 7 = 54 terms are irrational terms.

questions.question: The ratio of the 5th term from the beginning to the 5th term from the end in the binomial expansion of $\left(2^{\frac{1}{3}}+\frac{1}{2(3)^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{10}$is
questions.question_raw: The ratio of the 5th term from the beginning to the 5th term from the end in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{3}}+\frac{1}{2(3)^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{10}{/tex}is
questions.explanation:
Since, rth term from the end in the expansion of a binomial (x + a)n is same as the (n - r + 2)th term from the beginning in the expansion of same binomial.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required ratio = {tex}\frac{T_{5}}{T_{10-5+2}}=\frac{T_{5}}{T_{7}}=\frac{T_{4+1}}{T_{6+1}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{T_{5}}{T_{10-5+2}}=\frac{^{10} C_{4}\left(2^{1 / 3}\right)^{10-4}\left(\frac{1}{2(3)^{1 / 3}}\right)^{4}}{^{10} C_{6}\left(2^{1 / 3}\right)^{10-6}\left(\frac{1}{2(3)^{1 / 3}}\right)^{6}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} Tr+1 = nCrxn-rar]
{tex}=\frac{2^{6 / 3}\left(2(3)^{1 / 3}\right)^{6}}{2^{4 / 3}\left(2(3)^{1 / 3}\right)^{4}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} 10C4 = 10C6]
= 26/3 - 4/3 (2(3)1/3)6-4
= {tex}2^{2/{3}} \cdot 2^{2} \cdot 3^{2/{3}}=4(6)^{2/{3}}=4(36)^{1 / 3}{/tex}
So, the required ratio is 4(36)1/3 : 1

questions.question: The sum of the real values of x fo which the middle term in the binomial expansion of $\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}+\frac{3}{x}\right)^{8}$ equals 5670 is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the real values of x fo which the middle term in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}+\frac{3}{x}\right)^{8}{/tex} equals 5670 is
questions.explanation:
In the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}+\frac{3}{x}\right)^{8}{/tex}, the middle term is T4+1
[{tex}\therefore{/tex} Here, n = 8, which is even, therefore middle term = {tex}\left(\frac{n+2}{2}\right){/tex}th term]
{tex}\therefore 5670=^{8} C_{4}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right)^{4}\left(\frac{3}{x}\right)^{4}=\frac{8 \cdot 7 \cdot 6 \cdot 5}{1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3 \cdot 4} x^{8}{/tex}
{tex}\left[\because T_{r+1}=^{8} C_{r}\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right)^{8-r}\left(\frac{3}{x}\right)^{r}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{8}=3^{4} \Rightarrow x=\pm \sqrt{3}{/tex}
So, sum of all values of x i.e {tex}+\sqrt{3}{/tex} and {tex}-\sqrt{3}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: The positive value of $\lambda$ for which the coefficient of x^2 in the expression $x^{2}\left(\sqrt{x}+\frac{\lambda}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}$ is 720, is
questions.question_raw: The positive value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the coefficient of x2 in the expression {tex}x^{2}\left(\sqrt{x}+\frac{\lambda}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is 720, is
questions.explanation:
The general term in the expansion of binomial expression (a + b)n is Tr+1 = nCr an-rbr, so the general term in the expansion of binomial expression {tex}x^{2}\left(\sqrt{x}+\frac{\lambda}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is
{tex}T_{r+1}=x^{2}\left(^{10} \mathrm{C}_{r}(\sqrt{x})^{10-r}\left(\frac{\lambda}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}\right)=^{10} \mathrm{C}_{r} x^{2} \cdot x^{\frac{10-r}{2}} \lambda^{r} x^{-2 r}{/tex}
{tex}=^{10} C_{r} \lambda^{r} x^{2+\frac{10-r}{2}-2 r}{/tex}
Now, for the coefficient of x2, put {tex}2+\frac{10-r}{2}-2 r=2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{10-r}{2}-2 r=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10 - r = 4r {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 2
So, the coefficient of x2 is {tex}^{10} C_{2} \lambda^{2}{/tex} = 720 [given]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{10 !}{2 ! 8 !} \lambda^{2}=720 \Rightarrow \frac{10 \cdot 9 \cdot 8 !}{2 \cdot 8 !} \lambda^{2}=720{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 45 \lambda^{2}=720{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \lambda^{2}=16 \Rightarrow \lambda=\pm 4{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \lambda=4{/tex} {tex}[\lambda>0]{/tex}

questions.question: If the third term in the binomial expansion of (1 + x^log_2x)^5 equals 2560, then a possible value of x is
questions.question_raw: If the third term in the binomial expansion of (1 + xlog2x)5 equals 2560, then a possible value of x is
questions.explanation:
The ( r + 1)th term in the expansion of (a + x)n is given by {tex}T_{r+1}=^{n} C_{r} a^{n-r} x^{r}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3rd term in the expansion of {tex}\left(1+x^{\log _{2} x}\right)^{5}{/tex} is{tex}^{5} C_{2}(1)^{5-2}\left(x^{\log _{2} x}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow^{5} C_{2}(1)^{5-2}\left(x^{\log _{2} x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = 2560 (given)
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 10\left(x^{\log _{2} x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = 2560
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{\left(2 \log _{2} x\right)}{/tex} = 256
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \log _{2} x^{2 \log _{2} x}=\log _{2} 256{/tex} (taking log2 on both sides)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(log2x)(log2x) = 8 ({tex}\because{/tex} log2256 = log228 = 8)
(log2x)2 = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log2x = {tex}\pm{/tex}2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log2x = 2 or log2x = -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 4 or s = 2-2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of t^4 in the expansion of $\left(\frac{1-t^{6}}{1-t}\right)^{3}$ is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of t4 in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{1-t^{6}}{1-t}\right)^{3}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Clearly, {tex}\left(\frac{1-t^{6}}{1-t}\right)^{3}{/tex} = (1 - t6)3 (1 - t)-3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Coefficient of t4 in (1 - t6)3 ( 1 - t)-3
= Coefficient of t4 in (1 - t18 - 3t6 + 3t12) (1 - t)-3
= Coefficient of t4 in (1 - t)-3
= 3+4-1C4 = 6C4 = 15
({tex}\because{/tex} coefficient of xr in (1 - x)-n = n+r-1Cr)

questions.question: The sum of the coefficients of all odd degree terms in the expansion of $(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{5}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{5},(x>1)$ is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the coefficients of all odd degree terms in the expansion of {tex}(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{5}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{5},(x>1){/tex} is

We have, {tex}(x+\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{5}+(x-\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{5}, x>1{/tex}
{tex}\left.=2(^{5} C_{0} x^{5}+^{5} C_{2} x^{3}(\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{2}+^{5} C_{4} x(\sqrt{x^{3}-1})^{4}\right){/tex}
= 2(x5 + 10x3 (x3 - 1) + 5x(x3 - 1)2)
= 2(x5 + 10x6 - 10x3 + 5x7 - 10x4 + 5x)
Sum of coefficients of all odd degree terms is
2(1 - 10 + 5 + 5) = 2

questions.question: The value of $\left(^{21} C_{1}-^{10} C_{1}\right)+\left(^{21} C_{2}-^{10} C_{2}\right)$ + $\left(^{21} C_{3}-^{10} C_{3}\right)+\left(^{21} C_{4}-^{10} C_{4}\right)+\ldots+\left(^{21} C_{10}-^{10} C_{10}\right)$ is
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}\left(^{21} C_{1}-^{10} C_{1}\right)+\left(^{21} C_{2}-^{10} C_{2}\right){/tex} + {tex}\left(^{21} C_{3}-^{10} C_{3}\right)+\left(^{21} C_{4}-^{10} C_{4}\right)+\ldots+\left(^{21} C_{10}-^{10} C_{10}\right){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(^{21} C_{1}-^{10} C_{1}\right)+\left(^{21} C_{2}-^{10} C_{2}\right)+\left(^{21} C_{3}-^{10} C_{3}\right){/tex} {tex}+\ldots+\left(^{21} C_{10}-^{10} C_{10}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\left(^{21} C_{1}+^{21} C_{2}+\ldots+^{21} C_{10}\right)-\left(^{10} C_{1}+^{10} C_{2}+\ldots+^{10} C_{10}\right.){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(^{21} C_{1}+^{21} C_{2}+\ldots+^{21} C_{20}\right)-\left(2^{10}-1\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(^{21} C_{1}+^{21} C_{2}+\ldots+^{21} C_{21}-1\right)-\left(2^{10}-1\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(2^{21}-2\right)-\left(2^{10}-1\right)=2^{20}-1-2^{10}+1=2^{20}-2^{10}{/tex}

questions.question: If the number of terms in the expansion of $\left(1-\frac{2}{x}+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)^{n}, x \neq 0$, is 28, then the sum of the coefficients of all the terms in this expansion, is
questions.question_raw: If the number of terms in the expansion of {tex}\left(1-\frac{2}{x}+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)^{n}, x \neq 0{/tex}, is 28, then the sum of the coefficients of all the terms in this expansion, is
questions.explanation:
Clearly, number of terms in the expansion of
{tex}\left(1-\frac{2}{x}+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)^{n} is \frac{(n+2)(n+1)}{2} or ^{n+2} C_{2}{/tex}
[asuming {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex} distinct]
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{(n+2)(n+1)}{2}=28{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (n + 2) (n + 1) = 56 = (6 + 1) (6 + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}n = 6
 Hence, sum of coefficients = (1 - 2 + 4)6 = 36 = 729

questions.question: The sum of coefficients of integral powers of x in the binomial expansion (1 - 2$\sqrt x$)^50 is

Let Tt+1 be the general term in the expension of (1 - 2{tex}\sqrt x{/tex})50
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Tr+1 = 50Cr (1)50-r {tex}\left(-2 x^{\frac{1}{ 2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} = 50 Cr2r {tex}x^\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (-1)r
For the integral power of x, r should be even integer.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Sum of coefficients = {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}{/tex} 50C2r (2)2r
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} [(1 + 2)50 + (1 - 2)50] = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (350 + 1)

questions.question: The term independent of x in expansion of $\left(\frac{x+1}{x^{\frac {2}{3}}-x^{\frac {1}{3}}+1}-\frac{x-1}{x-x^{\frac {1}{2}}}\right)^{10}$ is
questions.question_raw: The term independent of x in expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x+1}{x^{\frac {2}{3}}-x^{\frac {1}{3}}+1}-\frac{x-1}{x-x^{\frac {1}{2}}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\frac{x+1}{x^{2 / 3}-x^{1 / 3}+1}-\frac{(x-1)}{x-x^{1 / 2}}\right]^{10}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac{\left(x^{1 / 3}\right)^{3}+1^{3}}{x^{2/3}-x^{1 / 3}+1}-\frac{\left\{(\sqrt{x})^{2}-1\right\}}{\sqrt{x}(\sqrt{x}-1)}\right]^{10}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac{\left(x^{1 / 3}+1\right)\left(x^{2 / 3}+1-x^{1 / 3}\right)}{x^{2/3}-x^{1 / 3}+1}-\frac{\left\{(\sqrt{x})^{2}-1\right\}}{\sqrt{x}(\sqrt{x}-1)}\right]^{10}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\left(x^{1 / 3}+1\right)-\frac{(\sqrt{x}+1)}{\sqrt{x}}\right]^{10}=\left(x^{1 / 3}-x^{-1 / 2}\right)^{10}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The general term is
{tex}T_{r+1}=^{10} C_{r}\left(x^{1 / 3}\right)^{10-r}\left(-x^{-1 / 2}\right)^{r}=^{10} C_{r}(-1)^{r} x^{\frac{10-r}{3}-\frac{r}{2}}{/tex}
For independent of x , put
{tex}\frac{10-r}{3}-\frac{r}{2}=0 \Rightarrow{/tex} 20 - 2r - 3r = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20 = 5r {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 4
{tex}\therefore \quad T_{5}=^{10} C_{4}=\frac{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1}=210{/tex}

= Coefficient of t24 in {(1 + t2)12 + t12(1 + t2)12 + t24(1 + t2)12}; [neglecting t36 (1 + t2)12]
= Coefficient of t24 = (12C12 + 12C6 + 12C0) = 2 + 12C6

questions.question: In the binomial expansion of (a - b)^n, n $\geq$ 5 the sum of the 5th and 6th terms is zero. Then, a/b equals
questions.question_raw: In the binomial expansion of (a - b)n, n {tex}\geq{/tex} 5 the sum of the 5th and 6th terms is zero. Then, a/b equals
questions.explanation:
Given, T5 + T6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow^{n} C_{4} a^{n-4} b^{4}-^{n} C_{5} a^{n-5} b^{5}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow^{n} C_{4} a^{n-4} b^{4}=^{n} C_{5} a^{n-5} b^{5} \Rightarrow \frac{a}{b}=\frac{n C_{5}}{^{n} C_{4}}=\frac{n-4}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: If in the expansion of (1 + x)^m(1 - x)^n, the coefficients of x and x^2 are 3 and -6 respectively, then m is euqal to
questions.question_raw: If in the expansion of (1 + x)m(1 - x)n, the coefficients of x and x2 are 3 and -6 respectively, then m is euqal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}(1+x)^{m}(1-x)^{n}=\left[1+m x+\frac{m(m-1)}{2} x^{2}+\ldots\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[1-n x+\frac{n(n-1)}{2} x^{2}-\ldots\right]{/tex}
{tex}=1+(m-n) x+\left[\frac{m(m-1)}{2}+\frac{n(n-1)}{2}-m n\right] x^{2}+\ldots{/tex}
term containing power of x {tex}\geq{/tex} 3
Now, m - n = 3 ...(i)
[{tex}\because{/tex} coefficient of x = 3, given]
and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} m(m - 1) + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} n(n - 1) - mn = -6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m(m - 1) + n(n - 1) - 2mn = -12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m2 - m + n2 - n - 2mn = -12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (m - n)2 - (m + n) = - 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m + n = 9 + 12 = 21 ...(ii)
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii). we get m = 12

2[a5 + 10a3b2 + 5ab4]

 {tex}=2\left[x^{5}+10 x^{3}\left(x^{3}-1\right)+5 x\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{2}\right]{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^4 in $\left(\frac{x}{2}-\frac{3}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}$ is

For coefficient of x4, we put 10 - 3r = 4

{tex}=\frac{45 \times 9}{256}=\frac{405}{256}{/tex}

questions.question: Given positive integers r > 1, n > 2 and the coefficient of (3r)th and (r + 2)th terms in the binomial expansion of (1 + x)^2n are equal. Then,
questions.question_raw: Given positive integers r > 1, n > 2 and the coefficient of (3r)th and (r + 2)th terms in the binomial expansion of (1 + x)2n are equal. Then,
questions.explanation:
In the expansion (1 + x)2n, t3r = 2nC3r-1(x)3r-1 and tr+2 = 2nCr+1(x)r+1
Since, binomial coefficients of t3r and tr+2 are equal
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2nC3r-1 = 2nCr+1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3r - 1 = r + 1 or 2n = (3r - 1) + (r + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2r = 2 or 2n = 4r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 1 or n = 2r
But r > 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} We take, n = 2r

questions.question: If the coefficients of x^3 and x^4 in the expansion of (1 + ax + bx^2)(1 - 2x)^18 in powers of x are both zero, then (a, b) is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of x3 and x4 in the expansion of (1 + ax + bx2)(1 - 2x)18 in powers of x are both zero, then (a, b) is equal to
