To find the coefficient of x3 and x4, use the formula of coefficient of xr in (1 - x)n is (-1)r nCr and then simplify
In expansion of (1 + ax + bx2) (1 - 2x)18
Coefficient of x3 = Coefficient of x3 in (1 - 2x)18 + Coefficient of x2 in a (1 -2x)18 + Coefficient of x in b(1 - 2x)18
{tex}=^{18} C_{3} \cdot 2^{3}+a^{18} C_{2} \cdot 2^{2}-b^{18} C_{1} \cdot 2{/tex}
Given, coefficient of x3 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad^{18} C_{3} \cdot 2^{3}+a^{18} C_{2} \cdot 2^{2}-b^{18} C_{1} \cdot 2=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-\frac{18 \times 17 \times 16}{3 \times 2} \cdot 8+a \cdot \frac{18 \times 17}{2} \cdot 2^{2}-b \cdot 18 \cdot 2=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 17 a-b=\frac{34 \times 16}{3}{/tex} ...(i)
Similarly, coefficient of x4 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow^{18} C_{4} \cdot 2^{4}-a^{18} C_{3} 2^{3}+b^{18} C_{2} \cdot 2^{2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 32a - 3b = 240 ...(ii)
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
a = 16, {tex}b=\frac{272}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Let (x + 10)^50 + (x - 10)^50 = a_0 + a_1x + a_2x^2 + ... + a_50x^50, for all x $\in$ R; then $\frac{a_{2}}{a_{0}}$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: Let (x + 10)50 + (x - 10)50 = a0 + a1x + a2x2 + ... + a50x50, for all x {tex}\in{/tex} R; then {tex}\frac{a_{2}}{a_{0}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have, (x + 10)50 + (x - 10)50 = a0 + a1x + a2x2 + ... + a50x50
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a0 + a1x + a2x2 + ... + a50x50
{tex}=\left[^{50} C_{0} x^{50}+^{50} C_{1} x^{49} 10+^{50} C_{2} x^{48} \cdot 10^{2}+\ldots+^{50} C_{50} 10^{50}\right){/tex} {tex}\left.+\left(^{50} C_{0} x^{50}-^{50} C_{1} x^{49} 10+^{50} C_{2} x^{48} 10^{2}-\ldots+^{50} C_{50} 10^{50}\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}=2\left[^{50} C_{0} x^{50}+^{50} C_{2} x^{48} \cdot 10^{2}+^{50} C_{4} x^{46} \cdot 10^{4}\right.{/tex} {tex}\left.+\ldots+^{50} C_{50} \cdot 10^{50}\right]{/tex}
By comparing coefficients, we get
{tex}a_{2}=2^{50} C_{48}(10)^{48} ; a_{0}=2^{50} C_{50}(10)^{50}=2(10)^{50}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{a_{2}}{a_{0}}=\frac{2\left(^{50} C_{2}\right)(10)^{48}}{2(10)^{50}}=2 \frac{50 \cdot 49}{1 \cdot 2} \frac{(10)^{48}}{2 \cdot(10)^{50}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} 50C48 = 50C2]
{tex}=\frac{50 \times 49}{2 \cdot(10 \times 10)}=\frac{5 \times 49}{20}=\frac{245}{20}{/tex}
= 12.25

questions.question: The value of r for which $^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r} \ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{0}+\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r-1}\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{1}+\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r-2}\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{2}+\ldots .+\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{0}\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r}$ is maximum, is
questions.question_raw: The value of r for which {tex}^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r} \ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{0}+\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r-1}\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{1}+\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r-2}\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{2}+\ldots .+\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{0}\ ^{20} \mathrm{C}_{r}{/tex} is maximum, is
questions.explanation:
We know that, (1 + x)20 = 20C0 + 20C1x2 + 20C2x2 + ... + 20Cr-1xr-1 + 20Crxr + ... + 20C20x20
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (1 + x)20 {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x)20 = (20C0 + 20C1x + 20C2x2 + ... + 20Cr-1 xr-1 + 20Crxr + ... + 20C20x20) {tex}\times{/tex} (20C0 + 20C1x + ... + 20Cr-1xr-1 + 20Crxr + ... + 20C20x20)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 + x)40 = (20C0 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20Cr + 20C1 20Cr-1 ... 20Cr 20C0) xr + ...
On comparing the coefficient of xr of both sides, we get
20C0 20Cr + 20C1 20Cr-1 + ... + 20Cr 20C0 = 40Cr
The maximum value of 40Cr is possible only when r = 20 [{tex}\because{/tex} nCn/2 is maximum when n is even]
Thus, required value of r is 20

questions.question: If the fractional part of the number $\frac{2^{403}}{15} \text { is } \frac{k}{15}$, then k is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the fractional part of the number {tex}\frac{2^{403}}{15} \text { is } \frac{k}{15}{/tex}, then k is equal to
questions.explanation:
Consider,
2403 = 2400+3 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 2400 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} (24)100 = 8(16)100 = 8(1 + 15)100 = 8(1 + 100C1 (15) + 100C2(15)2 + ... + 100C100 (15)100)
[By binomial theorem,
(1 + x)n = nC0 + nC1x + nC2x + nC2x2 + ... nCnxn, n{tex}\in{/tex}N]
= 8 + 8 (100C1 (15) + 100C2 (15)2 + ... + 100C100 (15)100)
= 8 + 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 15{tex}\lambda{/tex}
where {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 100C1 + ... + 100C100 (15)99 {tex}\in{/tex}N
{tex}\therefore \frac{2^{403}}{15}=\frac{8+8 \times 15 \lambda}{15}=8 \lambda+\frac{8}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad\left\{\frac{2^{403}}{15}\right\}=\frac{8}{15}{/tex}
(where {•} is the fractional part function)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} k = 8

questions.question: For r = 0, 1, ... , 10, if A_r, B_r and C_r denote respectively, the coefficient of x^r in the expansions of (1 + x)^10, (1 + x)^20 and (1 + x)^30. Then, $\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10} $ A_r (B_10B_r - C_10A_r) is equal to

questions.explanation:
Ar = Coefficient of xr in (1 + x)10 = 10Cr
Br = Coefficient of xr in (1 + x)20 = 20Cr
Cr = Coefficient of xr in (1 + x)30 = 30Cr
{tex}\therefore \sum_\limits{r=1}^{10}{/tex} Ar (B10Br - C10Ar) = {tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{10}{/tex} Ar B10Br - {tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{10}{/tex} Ar C10Ar
{tex}=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10}{/tex} 10Cr 20C10 20Cr - {tex}\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10}{/tex} 10Cr 30C10 10Cr
{tex}=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10}{/tex} 10C10-r 20C10 20Cr - {tex}\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10}{/tex} 10C10-r 30C10 10Cr
20C10 {tex}\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10}{/tex} 10C10-r {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20Cr - 30C10 {tex}\sum\limits_{r=1}^{10}{/tex}10C10-r 10Cr
{tex}{/tex}= 20C10 (30C10 - 1) - 30C10 (20C10 - 1)
= 30C10 - 20C10 = C10 - B10

questions.question: The sum $\sum\limits_{i=0}^{m}\left(\begin{array}{c}{10} \\ {i}\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c}{20} \\ {m-i}\end{array}\right), \text { where }\left(\begin{array}{l}{p} \\ {q}\end{array}\right)$ = 0 if p > q, is maximum when m is equal to

{tex}\sum\limits_{i=0}^{m}\left(\begin{array}{c}{10} \\ {i}\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c}{20} \\ {m-i}\end{array}\right){/tex} is the coefficient of xm in the expansion of (1 + x)10 (x + 1)20,
{tex}\Rightarrow\sum\limits_{i=0}^m\begin{pmatrix}10\\i\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}20\\m-i\end{pmatrix}{/tex} is the coefficient of xm in the expansion of (1 + x)30
i.e. {tex}\sum\limits_{i=0}^{m}\left(\begin{array}{c}{10} \\ {i}\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c}{20} \\ {m-i}\end{array}\right)=^{30} C_{m}=\left(\begin{array}{c}{30} \\ {m}\end{array}\right){/tex} ...(i)
and we know that, {tex}\left(\begin{array}{l}{n} \\ {r}\end{array}\right){/tex}is maximum, when {tex}\left(\begin{array}{l}{n} \\ {r}\end{array}\right)_{\max }=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}{r=\frac{n}{2},} & {\text { if } n \in \text { even }} \\ {r=\frac{n \pm 1}{2},} & {\text { if } n \in \text { odd }}\end{array}\right.{/tex}
Hence, {tex}\left(\begin{array}{c}{30} \\ {m}\end{array}\right){/tex} is maximum when m = 15

questions.question: Let n be an odd integer. If sin n$\theta$ = $\sum\limits_{r = 0}^n {{b_r}} {\sin ^r}\theta $ for every value of $\theta$, then
questions.question_raw: Let n be an odd integer. If sin n{tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\sum\limits_{r = 0}^n {{b_r}} {\sin ^r}\theta {/tex} for every value of {tex}\theta{/tex}, then

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sin n\theta = \sum\limits_{r = 0}^n {{b_r}} {\sin ^r}\theta {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\sin n \theta}{\sin \theta}=\sum_{r=1}^{n} b_{r}(\sin \theta)^{r-1}{/tex}
Taking limit as {tex}\theta \rightarrow 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{\theta \to 0} \frac{{\sin n\theta }}{{\sin \theta }} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{\theta \to 0} \sum\limits_{r = 1}^n {{b_r}} {(\sin \theta )^{r - 1}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{\theta \to 0} \frac{{n\theta \cdot \frac{{\sin n\theta }}{{n\theta }}}}{{\theta \cdot \frac{{\sin \theta }}{\theta }}}{/tex} = b1 + 0 + 0 + 0 + ......
[{tex}\because{/tex} other values becomes zero for higher powers of higher powers of sin {tex}\theta{/tex} ]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{n \cdot 1}{1}=b_{1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b1 = n

questions.question: If $\left(1-x+x^{2}\right)^{n}=a_{0}+a_{1} x+a_{2} x^{2}+\ldots+a_{2 n} x^{2 n}, \text { then } a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots$ + $a_{2 n}$ equals.
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\left(1-x+x^{2}\right)^{n}=a_{0}+a_{1} x+a_{2} x^{2}+\ldots+a_{2 n} x^{2 n}, \text { then } a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots{/tex} + {tex}a_{2 n}{/tex} equals.
questions.explanation:
({tex}\left.1-x+x^{2}\right)^{n}=a_{0}+a_{1} x+a_{2} x^{2}+\ldots+a_{2 n} x^{2 n}{/tex}..(1)
Put x=1 in (1),we get
{tex}1=a_{0}+a_{1}+a_{2}+a_{3}+\ldots+a_{2 n}{/tex}..(2)
Put x=-1 in(1),we get
{tex}3^{n}=a_{0}-a_{1}+a_{2}-a_{3}+\ldots+a_{2 n}{/tex}..(3)
Adding(1) and(2),we get
{tex}3^{n}+1=2\left(a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots+a_{2 n}\right){/tex}
Thus, {tex}a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots+a_{2 n}=\frac{3^{n}+1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The number of terms in the expansion of (a + b + c) ^n , where n ∈ N is

{tex}+\ldots+^{n} C_{n}(b+c)^{n}{/tex}
Further, expanding each term of RHS we note that
First term consist of 1 term 
second term on simplification gives 2 terms.
Third term on expansion gives 3 terms.
Similary, fourth term on expansion gives 4 terms and soon.therefore,
The total number of terms = 1 + 2 + 3 + ....+ ( n + 1)
= {tex}\frac{(n+1)(n+2)}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The ratio of the coefficient of x^15 to the term independent of x in $(x^{2}+\frac{2}{x})$^15 is
questions.question_raw: The ratio of the coefficient of x15 to the term independent of x in {tex}(x^{2}+\frac{2}{x}){/tex}15 is

{tex}\mathrm{T}_{r+1}=^{15} \mathrm{C}_{r}\left(x^{2}\right)^{15-r} \left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^{r}{/tex}
{tex}=^{15} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}(2)^{r} x^{30-3 r}{/tex}
Now, for the coefficient of term containing x15
{tex}30-3 r=15, \quad \text { i.e., } \quad r=5{/tex}
We have to find the term independent of x, put 30 - 3r = 0. So r = 10.
Therefore, {tex}^{15} \mathrm{C}_{10} 2^{10}{/tex} is the term independent of x 
Now the required ratio is {tex}\frac{^{15} C_{5} 2^{5}}{^{15}C_{10} 2^{10}}=\frac{1}{2^{5}}=\frac{1}{32}{/tex}

questions.question: In the expansion of (1 + x)^n, the binomial coefficients of three consecutive terms are respectively 220, 495 and 792. The value of n is
questions.question_raw: In the expansion of (1 + x)n, the binomial coefficients of three consecutive terms are respectively 220, 495 and 792. The value of n is
questions.explanation:
Suppose the three successive terms be Tr, Tr+1 and Tr+2.Then we have
nCr-1 = 220, nCr = 495 and nCr+1 = 792
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{^{n} C_{r}}{^{n} C_{r-1}}=\frac{495}{220}=\frac{9}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{^{n} C_{r+1}}{^{n} C_{r}}=\frac{792}{495}=\frac{8}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{n-r+1}{r}=\frac{9}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{n-(r+1)+1}{r+1}=\frac{8}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 n - 13r + 4 = 0 and 5n - 13r - 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 12 and r = 4.

questions.question: In the expansion of (x + a)^n, if the sum of odd terms be P and the sum of even terms be Q, then 4PQ = ?

questions.question: $\left\{\frac{c_{1}}{c_{0}}+2 \frac{C_{2}}{C_{1}}+\right.$$\left.3 \frac{C_{3}}{C_{2}}+\ldots+n \cdot \frac{C_{n}}{C_{n-1}}\right\}$ = ?

questions.explanation:
We know that {tex}\frac{C_{r}}{C_{r 1}}=\frac{n-r+1}{r}{/tex},
Substituting r = 1,2,3,...,n, we obtain
{tex}\frac{C_{1}}{C_{0}}+2 \cdot \frac{C_{2}}{C_{1}}+3 \cdot \frac{C_{3}}{C_{2}}{/tex} + ... + {tex}n \cdot \frac{C_{n}}{C_{n-1}}{/tex} = n + (n -1) + (n - 2) + ... + 1= {tex}\frac{1}{2} n(n+1){/tex}.

questions.question: {C_1 + 2C_2 + 3C_3 + ... + nC_n} = ?
questions.question_raw: {C1 + 2C2 + 3C3 + ... + nCn} = ?
questions.explanation:
C1 + 2C2 + 3C3 + ... + nCn = n + 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2}+3 \cdot \frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{3 !}{/tex} + ... + n
= n . [1 + (n - 1) {tex}\frac{(n-1)(n-2)}{2 !}{/tex} + ... + 1]
= n.[(n - 1)C0 + (n - 1)C1 + (n-1)C2 + ... + (n-1)Cn-1]
= n .(1 + 1)n-1 = n.2n-1

questions.question: {C_0 + 2C_1 + 3C_2 + ...+ (n + 1)C_n} = ?
questions.question_raw: {C0 + 2C1 + 3C2 + ...+ (n + 1)Cn} = ?
questions.explanation:
Here, C0 + 2C1 + 3C2 + ...+ (n + 1)Cn
= (C0 + C1 + C2 + ... + Cn) + (C1 + 2C2 + 3C3 + ... + nCn)
= 2n + n . 2n-1 = (n + 2) . 2n-1

questions.question: {C_0 + 3C_1 + 5C_2 + ...+ (2n + 1)C_n} = ?
questions.question_raw: {C0 + 3C1 + 5C2 + ...+ (2n + 1)Cn} = ?
questions.explanation:
We have, C0 + 3C1 + 5C2 + ...+ (2n + 1)Cn
= (C0 + C1 + C2 + ... + Cn) + 2(C1 + 2C2 + ... + nCn)
= 2n + 2(n . 2n-1) = (n + 1) . 2n

questions.question: Let T_r be the r^th term of a sequence, for r = 1, 2, 3, ... If 3T_r+1 = T_r and T_7 = $\frac{1}{243}$, then the value of $\sum_{r=1}^{\infty}\left(T_{r} \cdot T_{r+1}\right)$ is:
questions.question_raw: Let Tr be the rth term of a sequence, for r = 1, 2, 3, ... If 3Tr+1 = Tr and T7 = {tex}\frac{1}{243}{/tex}, then the value of {tex}\sum_{r=1}^{\infty}\left(T_{r} \cdot T_{r+1}\right){/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{r}+1}}{\mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{r}}}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} 
If T1 = a; T2 = {tex}\frac{a}{3}{/tex};
T3 = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{a}}{{3}^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} T7 = {tex}\frac{a}{3^{6}}=\frac{1}{243} \Rightarrow \frac{a}{3^{5} \times 3}=\frac{1}{3^{5}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Tr {tex}\cdot{/tex} Tr+1 = {tex}\frac{a}{3^{r-1}} \times \frac{a}{3^{r}}=\frac{a^{2}}{3^{2 r-1}}=\frac{3^{2}}{3^{2 r-1}}{/tex} 
{tex}\sum \limits_{{r}=1}^{\infty} {T}_{{r}} \cdot {T}_{{r}+{1}}{/tex} = {tex}\left(3+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{3^{3}}+\frac{1}{3^{5}}+\ldots \infty\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3}{1-\frac{1}{3^{2}}}=\frac{27}{8}{/tex}

questions.question: Let a and b are two positive real numbers such that a^2 + b = 2, then the maximum value of term independent of x in the expansion of $\left(a x^{\frac{1}{6}}+b x^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right)^{9}$ is:
questions.question_raw: Let a and b are two positive real numbers such that a2 + b = 2, then the maximum value of term independent of x in the expansion of {tex}\left(a x^{\frac{1}{6}}+b x^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right)^{9}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\operatorname{ax}^{\frac{1}{6}}+b x^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right)^{9}{/tex}
(r + 1)th term = {tex}C_{r}\left(\operatorname{ax}^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{r}\left(b x^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right)^{q-r}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{q}_{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}} \mathrm{a}^{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{b}^{\mathrm{q}-\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{x}^{\left(\frac{\mathrm{ax}}{6}-3+\frac{\mathrm{r}}{3}\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{ }^{\mathrm{q}} {C}_{\mathrm{r}} {a}^{\mathrm{r}} {b}^{\mathrm{q}-\mathrm{r}} {x}^{\frac{{r}}{2}-3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{r}}{2}-3{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Independent term = 9C6a6b3 
= {tex}\frac{9 \times 8 \times 7}{3 \times 2}{/tex}= 84a6b3
Now, a2 + b = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 1 & b = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Independent term = 84

19C16 + 19C17 = nC3  
20C17 = nC3 
20C3 = nC3 
n = 20

questions.question: Arrange the expansion of $\left(x^{\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{2 x^{\frac{1}{4}}}\right)^{n}$ in decreasing powers of x. Suppose the coefficient of the first three terms (taken in that order) form an arithmetic progression. Then the number of terms in the expansion having integral powers of x, is:
questions.question_raw: Arrange the expansion of {tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{2 x^{\frac{1}{4}}}\right)^{n}{/tex} in decreasing powers of x. Suppose the coefficient of the first three terms (taken in that order) form an arithmetic progression. Then the number of terms in the expansion having integral powers of x, is:
questions.explanation:
S = {tex}\left(x^{1 / 2}+\frac{1}{2 x^{1 / 4}}\right)^{n}{/tex}
= {tex}\sum^{n} C_{r} \cdot\left(x^{1 / 2}\right)^{n-r} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2 x^{1 / 4}}\right)^r{/tex}
= {tex}\sum^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{\mathrm{r}} \cdot \mathrm{x}^{(\mathrm{n}-\mathrm{r}) / 2} \cdot \mathrm{x}^{-\mathrm{r} / 4}{/tex}
S = {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{\mathrm{n}}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{\mathrm{P}} \mathrm{x}^{(2 \mathrm{n}-3 \mathrm{r}) / 4}{/tex}
We have to expand in decreasing powers of x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S = {tex}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{0}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{0} \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n} / 2}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{1} \mathrm{x}^{(2 \mathrm{n}-3) / 4}{/tex} + {tex}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{2}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2} \mathrm{x}^{(2 \mathrm{n}-6) / 4}{/tex} + ...
First three terms form an A.P.
= {tex}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{0},{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} and {tex}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{2}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} from an A.P.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{0}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{2}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} = {tex}2\left({ }^{n} C_{1}\right) \cdot \frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + {tex}\frac{\mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}-1)}{2} \times \frac{1}{4}{/tex} = n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (n - 1)(n - 8) = 0
n {tex}\ne{/tex} 1 ({tex}\because{/tex} there are at least 3 term)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 8
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S = {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm{r}=0}^{8}{ }^{8} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2} \mathrm{x}^{4-3 \mathrm{r} / 4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4-\frac{3 r}{4}{/tex} = K; where K {tex}\in{/tex} I
Now of such values of r ranging from 0 to 8 are: 0, 4 and 8
{tex}\therefore{/tex} There will be three such terms.
Hence, the answer is 3.

questions.question: The term independent of x in the binomial expansion of $\left(1-\frac{1}{x}+3 x^{5}\right)\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The term independent of x in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{x}+3 x^{5}\right)\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Given expansion of {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{x}+3 x^{5}\right)\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex} 
For this, we write general term in the expansion of {tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}{/tex} 
Tr+1 = {tex}\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\left(2 \mathrm{x}^{2}\right)^{8-\mathrm{r}}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)^{-\mathrm{r}}{/tex} = {tex}^{8} C_{r} 2^{8-r}\left(x^{16-3 r}\right){/tex} 
Now, in the product {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{x}+3 x^{5}\right)\left(2 x^{2}+\left(-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}\right.{/tex}, the term independent of x is 1 {tex}\times{/tex} term not containing x in {tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}-\frac{1}{x} \times{/tex} term containing x in {tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}+3 x^{5} \times{/tex} term containing x-5 in 
{tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{8}{/tex} ...(ii)
Now, by (i), term without x,
16 − 3r = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\frac{16}{3}{/tex} which is not possible.
Hence, there is no term independent of x in {tex}\left(2 x^{2}-\frac{1}{x}\right)^8{/tex} 
Now, again by (i), term containing x
16 - 3r = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 5
So, by (i), T6 = -448x
Now, again by (i), term containing x-5 
16 - 3r = -5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 7
So, by (i), T8 = -16x-5 
Put all these values in (ii), we get
Term independent of x = {tex}1 \times 0-\frac{1}{x}(-448 x)+3 x^{5}\left(-16 x^{-5}\right.){/tex} 
= 448 - 48 = 400

questions.question: If {P} denotes the fractional part of the number p, then $\left\{\frac{3^{200}}{8}\right\}$, is equal to:

{tex}\frac{3^{200}}{8}=\frac{1}{8}{/tex}(9100)
= {tex}\frac 18{/tex}(1 + 8)100 = {tex}\frac 18{/tex}[1 + n {tex}\cdot{/tex} 8 + {tex}\frac{n(n \ + \ 1)}{2} \cdot{/tex} 82 + ...]
= {tex}\frac 18{/tex} + Integer
{tex}\therefore\left\{\frac{3^{200}}{8}\right\}{/tex} = {{tex}\frac 18{/tex} + integer} = {tex}\frac 18{/tex}

questions.question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the coefficients of x^4 and x^2 respectively in the expansion of $\left(x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{6}+\left(x-\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{6}$, then:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} be the coefficients of x4 and x2 respectively in the expansion of {tex}\left(x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{6}+\left(x-\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{6}{/tex}, then:
questions.explanation:
Using Binomial expansion
(x + a)n + (x - a)n = 2(T1 + T3 + T5 + T7 ...)
{tex}\therefore \left(x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{6}+\left(x-\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{6}{/tex} = 2(T1 + T3 + T5 + T7)
= 2[6C0x5 + 6C2x4(x2 - 1) + 6C4x2(x2 - 1)2 + 6C6(x2 - 1)3]
= 2[x6 + 15(x6 - x4) + 15x2(x4 - 2x2 + 1) + (-1 + 3x2 - 3x4 + x6)]
= 2(32x6 - 48x4 + 18x2 - 1)
{tex}\alpha{/tex} = -96 and {tex}\beta{/tex} = 36
{tex}\therefore \alpha{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex} = -132

questions.question: The coefficient of x^2 in the expansion of the product (2 - x^2)$\cdot$((1 + 2x + 3x^2)^6 + (1 - 4x^2)^6) is:
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x2 in the expansion of the product (2 - x2){tex}\cdot{/tex}((1 + 2x + 3x2)6 + (1 - 4x2)6) is:
questions.explanation:
Let a = ((1 + 2x + 3x2)6 + (1 - 4x2)6)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Coefficient of x2 in the expansion of the product
(2 - x2) ((1 + 2x + 3x2)6 + (1 - 4x2)6)
= 2 (Coefficient of x2 in a) - 1 (Constant of expansion)
In the expansion of ((1 + 2x + 3x2)6 + (1 - 4x2)6)
Constant = 1 + 1 = 2
Coefficient of x2 = [Coefficient of x2 in (6C0 (1 + 2x)6 (3x2)0)] + [Cofficient of x2 in (6C1 (1 + 2x)5 (3x2)1)] - [6C1 (4x2)]
= 60 + 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 24 = 54
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The coefficient of x2 in (2 - x2) ((1 + 2x + 3x2)6 + (1 - 4x2)6)
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 54 - 1(2) = 108 - 2 = 106

questions.question: The coefficient of x^-5 in the binomial expansion of $\left(\frac{x \ + \ 1}{x^{\frac{2}{3}} \ - \ x^{\frac{1}{3}} \ + \ 1}-\frac{x \ - \ 1}{x \ - \ x^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right)^{10}$ where x $\ne$ 0, 1, is:
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x-5 in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x \ + \ 1}{x^{\frac{2}{3}} \ - \ x^{\frac{1}{3}} \ + \ 1}-\frac{x \ - \ 1}{x \ - \ x^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right)^{10}{/tex} where x {tex}\ne{/tex} 0, 1, is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\frac{\left(x^{\frac 1 3} + 1\right)\left(x^{\frac 2 3} - x^{\frac 1 3} + 1\right)}{\left(x^{\frac 2 3} - x ^{\frac 1 3} + 1\right)}-\frac{(\sqrt{x} - 1)(\sqrt{x} + 1)}{\sqrt{x}(\sqrt{x} - 1)}\right]^{10}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(x^{\frac 1 3}+1-1-\frac 1 x^{\frac 1 2}\right)^{10}=\left(x^{\frac 1 3}-\frac 1 x^{\frac 1 2}\right)^{10}{/tex}
Tr+1 = 10Cr {tex}x^{\frac{20-5 r}{6}}{/tex}
for r = 10
T11 = 10C10 x-5
Coefficient of x-5 = 10C10 (1)(-1)10 = 1

questions.question: If the coefficients of x^-2 and x^-4 in the expansion of $\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}+\frac{1}{2 x^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{18}$, (x > 0), are m and n respectively, then $\frac mn$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of x-2 and x-4 in the expansion of {tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}+\frac{1}{2 x^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{18}{/tex}, (x > 0), are m and n respectively, then {tex}\frac mn{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Tr+1 = 18Cr{tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{18-r}\left(\frac{1}{2 x^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{r}{/tex} = 18Cr{tex}x^{6-\frac{2 r}{3}} \frac{1}{2^{r}}{/tex}
{tex}\left\{\begin{array}{l} 6-\frac{2 r}{3}=-2 \Rightarrow r=12 \\ \ 6-\frac{2 r}{3}=-4 \Rightarrow r=15 \end{array}\right.{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\text { coefficient of } x^{-2}}{\text { coefficient of } x^{-4}}=\frac{{ }^{18} C_{12} \frac{1}{2^{12}}}{{ }^{18} C_{15} \frac{1}{2^{15}}}{/tex} = 182

questions.question: The number of terms in the expansion of (1 + x)^101 (1 + x^2 - x)^100 in powers of x is:
questions.question_raw: The number of terms in the expansion of (1 + x)101 (1 + x2 - x)100 in powers of x is:
questions.explanation:
Given expansion is (1 + x)101 (1 - x + x2)100
= (1 + x) (1 + x)100 (1 - x + x2)100
= (1 + x) [(1 + x) (1 - x + x2)]100
= (1 + x) [(1 - x3)100]
Expansion (1 - x3)100 will have 100 + 1 = 101 terms
So, (1 + x) (1 - x3)100 will have 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 101 = 202 terms

questions.question: The sum of the rational terms in the binomial expansion of $\left(2^{\frac{1}{2}}+3^{\frac{1}{5}}\right)^{10}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The sum of the rational terms in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{2}}+3^{\frac{1}{5}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}(2^{\frac 12} + 3^{\frac 15})^{10}{/tex} = 10C0{tex}(2^{\frac 12})^{10}{/tex} + 10C1{tex}(2^{\frac 12})^{9}(3^{\frac 15}){/tex} + ... + 10C10{tex}(3^{\frac 15})^{10}{/tex}
There are only two rational terms - first term and last term.
Now sum of two rational terms
= (2)5 + (3)2 = 32 + 9 = 41

questions.question: If the constant term in the binomial expansion of $\left(\sqrt{x}-\frac{k}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}$ is 405, then |k| equals:
questions.question_raw: If the constant term in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(\sqrt{x}-\frac{k}{x^{2}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is 405, then |k| equals:
questions.explanation:
General term = Tr+1 = 10Cr{tex}(\sqrt{x})^{10-r} \cdot\left(-\frac{k}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex}
= 10Cr(-k)r{tex}\cdot x^{\frac{10-r}{2}}-2 r{/tex}
= 10Cr(-k)r{tex}\cdot x^{\frac{10-5r}{2}}{/tex}
Since, it is constant term, then
{tex}{\frac{10-5r}{2}}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 2;
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 10C2(-k)2 = 405
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k2 = {tex}\frac{405 \times 2}{10 \times 9}=\frac{81}{9}{/tex} = 9;
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |k| = 3

questions.question: If for some positive integer n, the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of (1 + x)^n+5 are in the ratio 5 : 10 : 14, then the largest coefficient in this expansion is:
questions.question_raw: If for some positive integer n, the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of (1 + x)n+5 are in the ratio 5 : 10 : 14, then the largest coefficient in this expansion is:
questions.explanation:
Consider the three consecutive coefficients of
(1 + x)n+5 be n+5Cr, n+5Cr+1, n+5Cr+2
{tex}\because \frac{{ }^{n+5} C_{r}}{{ }^{n+5} C_{r+1}}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (Given)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{r+1}{n+5-r}=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow{/tex} 3r = n + 3 ...(i)
and {tex}\frac{{ }^{n+5} C_{r+1}}{{ }^{n+5} C_{r+2}}=\frac{5}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{r+2}{n+4-r}=\frac{5}{7} \Rightarrow{/tex} 12r = 5n + 6 ...(ii)
Solving (i) and (ii) we get r = 4 and n = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Largest coefficient in the expansion is 11C6 = 462

questions.question: If the number of integral terms in the expansion of $(3^{\frac 12} + 5^{\frac 18})^n$ is exactly 33, then the least value of n is:
questions.question_raw: If the number of integral terms in the expansion of {tex}(3^{\frac 12} + 5^{\frac 18})^n{/tex} is exactly 33, then the least value of n is:
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}(3^{\frac 12} + 5^{\frac 18})^n{/tex}
Tr+1 = nCr{tex}(3)^{\frac {n-r}2}(5)^{\frac r8}{/tex}
{tex}\because \frac{n-r}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac r8{/tex} are integer
So, r must be 0, 8, 16, 24 ...
Now n = t33 = a + (n - 1)d = 0 + 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 256
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 256

questions.question: If the term independent of x in the expansion of $\left(\frac{3}{2} x^{2}-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^{9}$ is k, then 18k is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the term independent of x in the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2} x^{2}-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^{9}{/tex} is k, then 18k is equal to:
questions.explanation:
General term = Tr+1 = 9Cr{tex}\left(\frac{3 x^{2}}{2}\right)^{9-r}\left(-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^{r}{/tex}
= 9Cr{tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{9-r}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{r}{/tex} x18-3r
The term is independent of x, then,
18 - 3r = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
{tex}\therefore T_{7}={ }^{9} C_{6}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{3}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{6}={ }^{9} C_{3}\left(\frac{1}{6}\right)^{3}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{9 \times 8 \times 7}{3 \times 2 \times 1}\left(\frac{1}{6}\right)^{3}=\left(\frac{7}{18}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 18k = 18 {tex}\times \frac{7}{18}{/tex} = 7

questions.question: Let $\alpha$ > 0, $\beta$ > 0 be such that $\alpha^{3}+\beta^{2}$ = 4. If the maximum value of the term independent of x in the binomial expansion of $\left(\alpha x^{\frac{1}{9}}+\beta x^{-\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{10}$ is 10k, then k is equal to:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}\alpha{/tex} > 0, {tex}\beta{/tex} > 0 be such that {tex}\alpha^{3}+\beta^{2}{/tex} = 4. If the maximum value of the term independent of x in the binomial expansion of {tex}\left(\alpha x^{\frac{1}{9}}+\beta x^{-\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is 10k, then k is equal to:
questions.explanation:
General term of
{tex}\left(\alpha x^{\frac{1}{9}}+\beta x^{-\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{10}{/tex} = 10Cr{tex}\left(\alpha x^{\frac{1}{9}}\right)^{10-r}\left(\beta x^{\frac{-1}{6}}\right)^{r}{/tex}
= 10Cr {tex}\alpha^{10-r} \beta^{r}(x)^{\frac{10-r}{9}-\frac{r}{6}}{/tex}
Term independent of x if {tex}\frac{10-r}{9}-\frac{r}{6}=0 \Rightarrow{/tex} r = 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Term independent of x = {tex}{ }^{10} C_{4} \alpha^{6} \beta^{4}{/tex}
Since {tex}\alpha^{3}+\beta^{2}{/tex} = 4
Then, by AM-GM inequality
{tex}\frac{\alpha^{3}+\beta^{2}}{2} \geq\left(\alpha^{3} b^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2)2 {tex}\geq \alpha^{3} \beta^{2} \Rightarrow \alpha^{6} \beta^{4} \leq{/tex} 16
{tex}\because{/tex} The maximum value of the term independent of x = 10k
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 10k = 10C4 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 16 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 336

questions.question: In the expansion of $\left(\frac{x}{\cos \theta}+\frac{1}{x \sin \theta}\right)^{16}$, if l_1 is the least value of the term independent of x when $\frac{\pi}{8} \leq {\theta} \leq \frac{\pi}{4}$ and l_2 is the least value of the term independent of x when $\frac{\pi}{16} \leq {\theta} \leq \frac{\pi}{8}$, then the ratio l_2 : l_1 is equal to:
questions.question_raw: In the expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{x}{\cos \theta}+\frac{1}{x \sin \theta}\right)^{16}{/tex}, if l1 is the least value of the term independent of x when {tex}\frac{\pi}{8} \leq {\theta} \leq \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} and l2 is the least value of the term independent of x when {tex}\frac{\pi}{16} \leq {\theta} \leq \frac{\pi}{8}{/tex}, then the ratio l2 : l1 is equal to:
questions.explanation:
General term of the given expansion
Tr+1 = 16Cr {tex}\left(\frac{x}{\sin \theta}\right)^{16-r}\left(\frac{1}{x \cos \theta}\right)^{r}{/tex}
fo r = 8 term is free from ‘x’
T9 = 16C8{tex}\frac{1}{\sin ^{8} \theta \cos ^{8} \theta}{/tex}
T9 = 16C8{tex}\frac{2^{8}}{(\sin 2 \theta)^{8}}{/tex}
When {tex}\theta \in\left[\frac{\pi}{8}, \frac{\pi}{4}\right]{/tex}, then least value of the term independent of x.
l1 = 16C8 28 [{tex}\because{/tex} min. value of l1 at {tex}\theta = \frac {\pi}4{/tex}]
When {tex}\theta \in\left[\frac{\pi}{16}, \frac{\pi}{8}\right]{/tex}, then least value of the term independent of x,
l2 = 16C8 = {tex}\frac{2^{8}}{\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{8}}={ }^{16} \mathrm{C}_{8} \cdot 2^{8} \cdot 2^{4}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} min. value of l2 at {tex}\theta = \frac {\pi}8{/tex}]
Now, {tex}\frac{l_{2}}{l_{1}}=\frac{{ }^{16} C_{8} \cdot 2^{8} \cdot 2^{4}}{{ }^{16} C_{8} \cdot 2^{8}}{/tex} = 16 : 1

questions.question: The value of $\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}{ }^{50-r} C_{6}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}{ }^{50-r} C_{6}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
The given series, {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}{ }^{50-r} C_{6}{/tex}
= 50C6 + 49C6 + 48C6 + 47C6 + ... + 32C6 + 31C6 + 30C6
= (30C7 + 30C6) + 31C6 + 32C6 + ... + 49C6 + 50C6 - 30C7
= (31C7 + 31C6) + 32C6 + ... + 49C6 + 50C6 - 30C7
= (30C7 + 32C6) + ... + 49C6 + 50C6 - 30C7  ...
= 51C7 - 30C7

questions.question: If ^20C_1 + (2^2) ^20C_2 + (3^2) ^20C_3 + .... + (20^2) ^20C_20 = A($2^{\beta}$), then the ordered pair (A, $\beta$) is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If 20C1 + (22) 20C2 + (32) 20C3 + .... + (202) 20C20 = A({tex}2^{\beta}{/tex}), then the ordered pair (A, {tex}\beta{/tex}) is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given, 20C1 + (22) 20C2 + (32) 20C3 + .... + (202) 20C20 = A({tex}2^{\beta}{/tex})
Taking L.H.S.,
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{20} r^{2} \cdot{ }^{20} C_{r}=20 \sum_\limits{r=1}^{20} r_{r}{ }^{19} C_{r-1}{/tex}
= 20{tex}\left[\sum_\limits{r=1}^{20}(r-1)^{19} C_{r-1}+\sum_\limits{r=1}^{20}{ }^{19} C_{r-1}\right]{/tex}
= 20[19{tex}\sum_\limits{r=2}^{20}{/tex} 18Cr-2 + 219] = 20[19 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 218 + 219]
= 420 {tex}\times{/tex} 218
Now, compare it with R.H.S., A = 420 and {tex}\beta{/tex} = 18

questions.question: The sum of the series 2 $\cdot$ ^20C_0 + 5 $\cdot$ ^20C_1 + 11 $\cdot$ ^20C_3 + ... + 62 $\cdot$ ^20C_20 is equal to:
questions.question_raw: The sum of the series 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C0 + 5 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C1 + 11 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C3 + ... + 62 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C20 is equal to:
questions.explanation:
2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C0 + 5 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C1 + 11 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C3 + ... + 62 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 20C20
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}{/tex} (3r + 2) 20Cr = 3{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}r\cdot{/tex} 20Cr + 2{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}{/tex} 20Cr
= 60{tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{20}{/tex}19Cn-1 + 2{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{20}{/tex} 20Cr
= 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 219 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 220 = 221[15 + 1] = 225

questions.question: If $\sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}${^50C_r $\cdot$ ^50-rC_25-r} = K(^50C_25), then K is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}{/tex}{50Cr {tex}\cdot{/tex} 50-rC25-r} = K(50C25), then K is equal to:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}{/tex}{50Cr {tex}\cdot{/tex} 50-rC25-r} = {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}\left(\frac{50!}{50-r! r!} \frac{50-r!}{25!25-r!} \right){/tex}
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}\left(\frac{50!}{ 25!} \times \frac{1}{25!} \times\left(\frac{25!}{25-r ! r!}\right)\right){/tex}
= {tex}{ }^{50} C_{25} \sum_\limits{r=0}^{25}{ }^{25} C_{r}{/tex} = 50C25(225)
Then, by comparison, K = 225

questions.question: The coefficient of x^50 in the polynomial (x + ^50C_0) (x + 3$\cdot$^50C_1) (x + 5$\cdot$^50C_2) ... (x + (2n + 1) ^50C_50), is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x50 in the polynomial (x + 50C0) (x + 3{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C1) (x + 5{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C2) ... (x + (2n + 1) 50C50), is
questions.explanation:
We have,
(x + 50C0) (x + 3{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C1) (x + 5{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C2) ... (x + (2n + 1) 50C50)
= x51 + x50 {50C0 + 3{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C1 + 5{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C2 + ... + 101 50C50} + ...
The coefficient of x50
= 50C0 + 3{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C1 + 5{tex}\cdot{/tex}50C2 + ... + 101 50C50
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{50}{/tex} (2r + 1) 50Cr r
= 2{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{50}{/tex} r{tex}\cdot{/tex}50Cr + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{50}{/tex} 50Cr
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 {tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{50}{/tex} 49Cr-1 + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{50}{/tex} 50Cr ... [{tex}\because{/tex} nCr = {tex}\frac{n}{r}{/tex} n-1Cr-1]
= 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 249 + 250 = 51{tex}\cdot{/tex}250

questions.question: If (1 + x)^n = C_0 + C_1x + C_2x^2 + ... + C_nx^n, then $\frac{C_{1}}{C_{0}}+\frac{2 C_{2}}{C_{1}}+\frac{3 C_{3}}{C_{2}}+\ldots \frac{n C_{n}}{C_{n-1}}$ =
questions.question_raw: If (1 + x)n = C0 + C1x + C2x2 + ... + Cnxn, then {tex}\frac{C_{1}}{C_{0}}+\frac{2 C_{2}}{C_{1}}+\frac{3 C_{3}}{C_{2}}+\ldots \frac{n C_{n}}{C_{n-1}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{C_{1}}{C_{0}}+2 \cdot \frac{C_{2}}{C_{1}}+3 \cdot \frac{C_{2}}{C_{2}}+\ldots n \cdot \frac{C_{n}}{C_{n-1}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{n}{1}+2 \frac{\frac{n(n-1)}{1.2}}{n}+3 \frac{\frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{3.2 .1}}{\frac{n(n-1)}{1.2}}+\ldots n \frac{1}{n}{/tex}
= n + (n - 1) + (n - 2) + ... + 1
= {tex}\sum{/tex}n
= {tex}\frac{n(n+1)}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: $\frac{C_{i}}{C_{0}}+\frac{2 C_{2}}{C_{1}}+\frac{3 C_{3}}{C_{2}}+\ldots+\frac{15 C_{15}}{C_{14}}$ =
questions.question_raw: {tex}\frac{C_{i}}{C_{0}}+\frac{2 C_{2}}{C_{1}}+\frac{3 C_{3}}{C_{2}}+\ldots+\frac{15 C_{15}}{C_{14}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
We know that,
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{C}_{1}}{\mathrm{C}_{0}}+\frac{2 \mathrm{C}_{2}}{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{l}}}+\frac{3 \mathrm{C}_{2}}{\mathrm{C}_{2}}+\ldots+\frac{n \mathrm{C}_{n}}{\mathrm{C}_{n-1}}=\frac{n(n+1)}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{C_{1}}{C_{0}}+\frac{2 C_{2}}{C_{1}}+\frac{3 C_{3}}{C_{2}}+\ldots \frac{15 C_{15}}{C_{14}}=\frac{15 \times(15+1)}{2}{/tex} = 120

questions.question: If (1 + x)^n = C_0 + C_1x + C_2x^2 + ... + C_nx^n, then $\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n} \sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}$ (r + s) C_r C_s is equal to
questions.question_raw: If (1 + x)n = C0 + C1x + C2x2 + ... + Cnxn, then {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n} \sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex} (r + s) Cr Cs is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n} \sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex} (r + s) Cr Cs
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n} \sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex} (rnCr nCs + s nCr nCs)
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n} \sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex} r nCr nCs + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n} \sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex} s nCr nCs
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} r nCr ({tex}\sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex} nCs) + {tex}\sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex}s nCs ({tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr)
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex}r nCr 2n + {tex}\sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex}s nCs 2n
= 2n ({tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex}r (nCr)) + 2n ({tex}\sum_\limits{s=0}^{n}{/tex}s (nCs))
= 2n {tex}\left(\sum_{r=1}^{n} r\left(\frac{n}{r}\right)^{n-1} \mathrm{C}_{r-1}\right){/tex} + 2n {tex}\left(\sum_{s=1}^{n} s \cdot\left(\frac{n}{s}\right)^{n-1} C_{s-1}\right){/tex}
= 2n (n{tex}\cdot{/tex}2n-1) + 2n (n{tex}\cdot{/tex}2n-1)
= n(22n-1) + n(22n-1)
= n{tex}\cdot{/tex}22n

questions.question: The expression $\left(x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{5}+\left(x-\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{5}$ is a polynomial of degree
questions.question_raw: The expression {tex}\left(x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{5}+\left(x-\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{5}{/tex} is a polynomial of degree

{tex}\left(x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{5}+\left(x-\sqrt{x^{2}-1}\right)^{5}{/tex}

= 2x5 + 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 5C2x3 (x2 - 1) + 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 5C4 x(x2 - 1)2

questions.question: The number of terms in the expansion of (a + b + c)^10 is

questions.question: The coefficient of x^n, where n is a positive integer, in the expansion of $\left(1+2 x+3 x^{2}+\ldots\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}$^ is

questions.question: If x is positive, the first negative term in the expansion of $(1+x)^{\frac{27}{5}}$ is
questions.question_raw: If x is positive, the first negative term in the expansion of {tex}(1+x)^{\frac{27}{5}}{/tex} is

Tr+1 = {tex}\frac{n(n-1)(n-2) \ldots(n-r+1)}{r !}{/tex} (x)r
For first negative term n - r + 1 < 0

questions.question: Let f(x) = $\left(\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}\right)^{4}$ + $\left(\frac{2 x^{2}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)^{4}$, then
questions.question_raw: Let f(x) = {tex}\left(\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}\right)^{4}{/tex} + {tex}\left(\frac{2 x^{2}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)^{4}{/tex}, then

options.text: f(x) is a polynomial of degree 8 in x

questions.explanation:
f(x) = {tex}\left(\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}\right)^{4}+\left(\frac{2 x^{2}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)^{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}\right)^{4}+\left(\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}\right)^{4}{/tex} ... [By rationalising denominator]
= 2 [4C0 ({tex}\sqrt{1+x^{2}}{/tex})4 + 4C2 ({tex}\sqrt{1+x^{2}}{/tex})2 ({tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex})2 + 4C4 ({tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex})4]
= 2[(1 + x4 + 2x2) + 6(1 - x4) + (1 + x4 - 2x2)]
= 2(8 - 4x4) = 16 - 8x4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) has exactly two terms.

questions.question: The coefficient of x^n in the expansion of $\frac{1}{x^{2}-5 x+6}$ for |x| < 1 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of xn in the expansion of {tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}-5 x+6}{/tex} for |x| < 1 is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}-5 x+6}=-\frac{1}{x-2}+\frac{1}{x-3}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2\left(1-\frac{x}{2}\right)}-\frac{1}{3\left(1-\frac{x}{3}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(1-\frac{x}{2}\right)^{-1}-\frac{1}{3}\left(1-\frac{x}{3}\right)^{-1}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Coefficient of xn = {tex}\frac{1}{2^{n+1}}-\frac{1}{3^{n+1}}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^4 in the expansion of (1 + x + x^2 + x^3)^11 is

x4 appears as product of (x2, x2) or (x, x, x2) or (x, x, x, x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The coefficient of x4
= 11C2 + 11C2 11C1 + 11C4
= 55 + 11(55) + 330 = 990

= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (23409 - 1785) = 10812

questions.question: The larger between 99^50 + 100^50 and 101^50 is
questions.question_raw: The larger between 9950 + 10050 and 10150 is
questions.explanation:
We have,
10150 = (100 + 1)50
= 10050 + 50{tex}\cdot{/tex}10049 + {tex}\frac{50.49}{2.1}{/tex} 10048 + ...
and 9950 = (100 - 1)50 ...(i)
= 10050 - 50{tex}\cdot{/tex}10049 + {tex}\frac{50.49}{2.1}{/tex} 10048 ...(ii)
Subtracting (ii) from (i), we get
10150 - 9950 = 10050 + 2{tex}\left(\frac{50\cdot49 \cdot48}{1\cdot2 \cdot3}\right){/tex} 10047 > 10050
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10150 > 10050 + 9950

questions.question: If |x| is small so that x^2 and higher powers of x may be neglected, then an approximate value of $\frac{\left(1+\frac{2}{3} x\right)^{-3}(1-15 x)^{-\frac{1}{5}}}{(2-3 x)^{4}}$ is
questions.question_raw: If |x| is small so that x2 and higher powers of x may be neglected, then an approximate value of {tex}\frac{\left(1+\frac{2}{3} x\right)^{-3}(1-15 x)^{-\frac{1}{5}}}{(2-3 x)^{4}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\left(1+\frac{2}{3} x\right)^{-3}(1-15 x)^{\frac{-1}{5}}}{(2-3 x)^{4}}=\frac{\left(1+\frac{2}{3} x\right)^{-3}(1-15 x)^{\frac{-1}{5}}}{2^{4}\left(1-\frac{3 x}{2}\right)^{4}}=\frac{(1-2 x)(1+3 x)}{16(1-6 x)},{/tex} ...[neglecting x2 and higher powers of x]
{tex}=\frac{\left(1+x-6 x^{2}\right)(1-6 x)^{-1}}{16}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{16}{/tex} (1 + x - 6x2) (1 + 6x + ...)
= {tex}\frac{1}{16}{/tex}(1 + 7x)

(1 - 2x + 3x2 - 4x3 + ...)2

Required coefficient
= 2[1 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 + (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} (-10) + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 + (-4) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7] + (-6) (-6)

= 2(125) + 36
= 286

questions.question: If f(x) = 1 - x + x^2 - x^3 + ... - x^15 + x^16 - x^17, then the coefficient of x^2 in f(x - 1) is
questions.question_raw: If f(x) = 1 - x + x2 - x3 + ... - x15 + x16 - x17, then the coefficient of x2 in f(x - 1) is
questions.explanation:
f(x) = 1 - x + x2 - x3 + ... -x15 + x16 - x17
{tex}=\frac{1-(-x)^{18}}{1-(-x)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1-x^{18}}{1+x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} f(x - 1) = {tex}\frac{1-(x-1)^{18}}{1+x-1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1-(x-1)^{18}}{x}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} (x - 1)18
{tex}=\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} (1 - x)18
{tex}=\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} (18C0 - 18C1x + 18C2x2 - ... + 18C18x18)
= x-1 - 18C0x-1 + 18C1x0 - 18C2x + 18C3x2 - ... - 18C18x17
{tex}\therefore{/tex} coefficient of x2 = 18C3 = 816

and p3 (6C3) = {tex}\frac{5}{2} \Leftrightarrow{/tex} p3 (20) = {tex}\frac{5}{2} \Leftrightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} np = 6{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} = 3

questions.question: If $\frac{1}{1-2 x+x^{2}}$ = 1 + a_1x + a_2x^2 + ... , then the value of a_r is

questions.question: The number of rational terms in the expansion of $\left(2^{\frac{1}{ 5}}+3^{\frac{1}{10}}\right)^{55}$ is
questions.question_raw: The number of rational terms in the expansion of {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{ 5}}+3^{\frac{1}{10}}\right)^{55}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
(r + 1)th term of {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{ 5}}+3^{\frac{1}{10}}\right)^{55}{/tex} = 55Cr {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{ 5}}\right)^{r}\left(3^{\frac{1}{10}}\right)^{55-r}{/tex}
To get a rational term, r must be a multiple of 5 and 55 - r must be divisible by 10.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 5, 15, 25, 35, 45, 55
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The number of rational terms = 6

questions.question: Let the integral part of (8 + $3 \sqrt{7}$)^n = 1, then
questions.question_raw: Let the integral part of (8 + {tex}3 \sqrt{7}{/tex})n = 1, then

options.text: Nothing can be said about I

questions.explanation:
Let (8 + {tex}3 \sqrt{7}{/tex})n = p + f, where p {tex}\in{/tex} I and f is a proper fraction.
Let (8 - {tex}3 \sqrt{7}{/tex})n = {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex}, a proper fraction [{tex}\because{/tex} 0 < 8 - {tex}3 \sqrt{7}{/tex} < 1]
Since, (8 + {tex}3 \sqrt{7}{/tex})n + (8 - {tex}3 \sqrt{7}{/tex})n = p + f + {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} is an even integer
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} p + 1 is an even integer ...
[{tex}\because{/tex} 0 < f < 1 and 0 < {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} < 1] [{tex}\therefore{/tex} 0 < f + {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} < 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f + {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p is an odd integer.

questions.question: The sum of the series ^nC_1 - 2(^nC_2) + 3(^nC_3) - 4(^nC_4) + ... + n(-1)^n-1$\cdot$^nC_n is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the series nC1 - 2(nC2) + 3(nC3) - 4(nC4) + ... + n(-1)n-1{tex}\cdot{/tex}nCn is
questions.explanation:
nC1 - 2(nC2) + 3(nC3) - ...+ n(-1)n-1{tex}\cdot{/tex}nCn
= n - 2 {tex}\left(\frac{n(n-1)}{2}\right){/tex} + 3 {tex}\left(\frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{6}\right){/tex} - ... + n(-1)n-1
= n[1 - (n - 1) + {tex}\frac{(n-1)(n-2)}{2}{/tex} - ... + (-1)n-1]
= n[n-1C0 - n-1C1 + n-1C2 - ... + (-1)n-1{tex}\cdot{/tex}n-1Cn-1]
= n(1 - 1)n-1 = 0

questions.question: If the coefficient of 4^th term in the expansion of (a + b)^n is 56, then n is
questions.question_raw: If the coefficient of 4th term in the expansion of (a + b)n is 56, then n is
questions.explanation:
T4 = T3+1 = nC3 an-3 b3
According to the given condition,
nC3 = 56
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{n !}{3 !(n-3) !}{/tex} = 56
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n(n - 1) (n - 2) = 56 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n(n - 1) (n - 2) = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 8

questions.question: $\sum_\limits{k=1}^{n} k\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^{k-1}$ =
questions.question_raw: {tex}\sum_\limits{k=1}^{n} k\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^{k-1}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sum_\limits{k=1}^{n} k\left(1+\frac{i}{n}\right)^{k-1}{/tex}
= 1 + 2{tex}\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^{1}{/tex} + 3{tex}\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^{2}{/tex} + ... upto n terms
= 1 + 2t + 3t2 + ... upto n terms,
where t = 1 + {tex}\frac{1}{n}{/tex}
= (1 - t)-2 = [1 - (1 + {tex}\frac{1}{n}{/tex})]-2 = {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{n}\right)^{-2}{/tex} = n2

questions.question: The value of $\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}$ ^nC_r (sin rx) is equal to
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr (sin rx) is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr sin rx = Im ({tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr eirx)
= Im ({tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr (eix)r)
= Im ((1 + eix)n)
= Im (1 + cosx + i sinx)n
= Im (2 cos2 {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} + 2i sin {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex})n
= Im (2 cos {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} (cos {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} + i sin {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex}))n
= 2n {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosn {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin{tex}\frac{n x}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Let R = (5$\sqrt{5}$ + 11)^2n+1 and f = R - [R], where [$\cdot$] denotes the greatest integer function. The value of R$\cdot$f is
questions.question_raw: Let R = (5{tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} + 11)2n+1 and f = R - [R], where [{tex}\cdot{/tex}] denotes the greatest integer function. The value of R{tex}\cdot{/tex}f is
questions.explanation:
Since, (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11) (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} + 11) = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11 = {tex}\frac{4}{5 \sqrt{5}+11}{/tex}
{tex}\because {/tex} 0 < 5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11 < 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0 < (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11)2n+1 < 1, for positive integral n
(5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} + 11)2n+1 - (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11)2n+1
= 2 [2n+1C1 (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex})2n {tex}\cdot{/tex} 11 + 2n+1C3 (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex})2n-2 {tex}\times{/tex} 113 + ... + 2n+1C2n+1 112n+1]
= 2 [2n+1C1 (125)n {tex}\cdot{/tex} 11 + 2n+1C3 (125)n-1 113 + ... + 2n+1C2n+1 112n+1]
= 2k, (for some positive integer k)
Let {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} = (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11)2n+1, then [R] + f - {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} = 2k
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f - {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} = 2k - [R] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f - {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} is an integer
But, 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} f < 1; 0 < {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} < 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -1 < f - {tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} < 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Rf = R{tex}f^{\prime}{/tex} = (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} + 11)2n+1 (5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} - 11)2n+1
= [(5{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex})2 - 112]2n+1 = 42n+1

questions.question: The sum of the series $\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}$ (-1)^r ^nC_r $\left(\frac{1}{2^{r}}+\frac{3^{r}}{2^{2 r}}+\frac{7^{r}}{2^{3 r}}+\frac{15^{r}}{2^{4 r}}\right.$ + ... m terms) is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the series {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2^{r}}+\frac{3^{r}}{2^{2 r}}+\frac{7^{r}}{2^{3 r}}+\frac{15^{r}}{2^{4 r}}\right.{/tex} + ... m terms) is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2^{r}}+\frac{3^{r}}{2^{2 r}}+\frac{7^{r}}{2^{3 r}}+\frac{15^{r}}{2^{4 r}}\right.{/tex} + ... upto m terms)
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2^{r}}+\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r {tex}\cdot{/tex} nCr {tex}\frac{3^{r}}{2^{2 r}}{/tex} + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\frac{7}{2^{3 r}}{/tex} + ...
= {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{n}+\left(1-\frac{3}{4}\right)^{n}+\left(1-\frac{7}{8}\right)^{n}{/tex} + ... upto m terms
= {tex}\frac{1}{2^{n}}+\frac{1}{4^{n}}+\frac{1}{8^{n}}{/tex} + ... upto m terms
= {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{2^{n}}\left(1-\frac{1}{2^{n n n}}\right)}{\left(1-\frac{1}{2^{n}}\right)}=\frac{2^{m n}-1}{2^{m n}\left(2^{n}-1\right)}{/tex}

questions.question: If the 4^th term in the expansion of ($\frac{1}{x^{\frac{4}{3}}}$ - x^2 log_10x)^6 is $\frac{1}{5}$, then x =
questions.question_raw: If the 4th term in the expansion of ({tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{4}{3}}}{/tex} - x2 log10x)6 is {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}, then x =
questions.explanation:
Given, T4 = {tex}\frac{1}{5} \Leftrightarrow{/tex} - 6C3 {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x^{\frac{4}{3}}}\right)^{3}{/tex} (x2 log10 x)3 = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} - 20 {tex}\cdot{/tex} x2 (log10x)3 = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x2 (log10x)3 = -{tex}\frac{1}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x2 (log10x)3 = 6{tex}\left(\frac{1}{10}\right)^{2}{/tex} (log10 {tex}\frac{1}{10}{/tex})3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{10}{/tex}

questions.question: If the value of x is so small that x^2 and greater powers can be neglected, then $\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt[3]{(1-x)^{2}}}{1+x+\sqrt{1+x}}$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the value of x is so small that x2 and greater powers can be neglected, then {tex}\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt[3]{(1-x)^{2}}}{1+x+\sqrt{1+x}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given expression can be written as
{tex}=\frac{(1+x)^{\frac{1}{2}}+(1-x)^{\frac{2}{3}}}{1+x+(1+x)^{\frac{1}{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\left[1+\frac{1}{2} x-\frac{1}{8} x^{2}+\ldots\right]+\left[1-\frac{2}{3} x-\frac{1}{9} x^{2}-\ldots\right]}{1+x+\left[1+\frac{1}{2} x-\frac{1}{8} x^{2}+\ldots\right]}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2-\frac{1}{6} x}{2+\frac{3}{2} x}{/tex}, neglecting other terms
{tex}=\frac{1-\frac{1}{12} x}{1+\frac{3}{4} x}=\left(1-\frac{1}{12} x\right)\left(1+\frac{3}{4} x\right)^{-1}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{12} x\right)\left(1-\frac{3}{4} x\right){/tex}, neglecting terms of higher degree
= 1 - {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}x - {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex}x, neglecting terms of higher degree
= 1 - {tex}\frac{5}{6}{/tex}x

questions.question: If x^m occurs in the expansion of (x + $\frac{1}{x^{2}}$)^2n, then the coefficient of x^m is
questions.question_raw: If xm occurs in the expansion of (x + {tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex})2n, then the coefficient of xm is
questions.explanation:
Tr+1 = 2nCr x2n-r {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} = 2nCr x2n-3r,
This contains xm, if 2n - 3r = m i.e., if r = {tex}\frac{2 n-m}{3}{/tex}
The coefficient of xm = 2nCr
{tex}=\frac{(2 n) !}{(2 n-r) ! r !}=\frac{(2 n) !}{\left(2 n-\frac{2 n-m}{3}\right) !\left(\frac{2 n-m}{3}\right) !}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(2 n) !}{\left(\frac{4 n+m}{3}\right) !\left(\frac{2 n-m}{3}\right) !}{/tex}

questions.question: If a_n = $\sum_\limits{k=0}^{n} \frac{2^{k}}{k !(n-k) !}$ for n $\geq$ 0, then a_0 + a_1 + a_2 + ... =
questions.question_raw: If an = {tex}\sum_\limits{k=0}^{n} \frac{2^{k}}{k !(n-k) !}{/tex} for n {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, then a0 + a1 + a2 + ... =
questions.explanation:
a0 + a1 + a2 + ...
{tex}=\sum_\limits{n=0}^{\infty} a_{n}=\sum_\limits{n=0}^{\infty}\left(\sum_\limits{k=0}^{n} \frac{2^{k}}{k !(n-k) !}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\sum_\limits{n=0}^{\infty}\left(\frac{1}{n !} \sum_\limits{k=0}^{n} \frac{n !}{k !(n-k) !} 2^{k}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\sum_\limits{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n !} \sum_\limits{k=0}^{n}{/tex} nCk 2k
{tex}=\sum_\limits{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n !}{/tex} (1 + 2)n
{tex}=\sum_\limits{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{3^{n}}{n !}{/tex}
= 1 + {tex}\frac{3}{1 !}+\frac{3^{2}}{2 !}{/tex} + ...
= e3

questions.question: The sum of the series 1 + $\frac{1}{6}+\frac{1.4}{6.12}+\frac{1.4 .7}{6.12 .18}$ + ... is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the series 1 + {tex}\frac{1}{6}+\frac{1.4}{6.12}+\frac{1.4 .7}{6.12 .18}{/tex} + ... is
questions.explanation:
Comparing the given expression with
1 + nx + {tex}\frac{n(n-1)}{2 !}{/tex} x2 + ... we get
nx = {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}\frac{n(n-1) x^{2}}{2}=\frac{1}{18}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n(n - 1)x2 = {tex}\frac{1}{9}{/tex} ...(ii)
(i) and (ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{n^{2} x^{2}}{n(n-1) x^{2}}=\frac{\frac{1}{36}}{\frac{1}{9}} \Leftrightarrow \frac{n}{n-1}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 4n = n - 1 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n = {tex}-\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-\frac{1}{3}{/tex}x = {tex}\frac{1}{6} \Leftrightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ...[From (i)]
The required sum = {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}=(2)^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}

questions.question: Let S_p and S_q be the coefficient of x^p and x^q respectively in (1 + x)^p+q, then
questions.question_raw: Let Sp and Sq be the coefficient of xp and xq respectively in (1 + x)p+q, then
questions.explanation:
Sp = coefficient of xp = p+qCp = {tex}\frac{(p+q) !}{p ! q !}{/tex} and Sq = coefficient of xq = p+qCq = {tex}\frac{(p+q) !}{q ! p !}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Sp = Sq

questions.question: If the sum of coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x^3)^n is p, that of (1 - 4x + 7x^2)^n is q and (1 - 5x + 10x^2 + 2x^3)^n is r, then
questions.question_raw: If the sum of coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x3)n is p, that of (1 - 4x + 7x2)n is q and (1 - 5x + 10x2 + 2x3)n is r, then

options.text: p, q, r are in A.P.
p, q, r are in G.P.

questions.explanation:
Sum of coefficients can be obtained by substituting x = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = (1 + 13)n = 2n, q = (1 - 4 + 7)n = 4n = 22n and r = (1 - 5 + 10 + 2)n = 8n = 23n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p{tex}\cdot{/tex}r = 2n {tex}\cdot{/tex} 23n = 24n = (22n)2 = q2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p, q, r are in G.P.

questions.question: The coefficient of x^5 in the expansion of (1 + x)^21 + (1 + x)^22 + ... + (1 + x)^30 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x5 in the expansion of (1 + x)21 + (1 + x)22 + ... + (1 + x)30 is
questions.explanation:
(1 + x)21 + (1 + x)22 + ... + (1 + x)30
= (1 + x)21 {tex}\left[\frac{(1+x)^{10}-1}{(1+x)-1}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} [(1 + x)31 - (1 + x)21]
The coefficient of x5 in the given expression
= coefficient of x5 in {{tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} [(1 + x)31 - (1 + x)21]}
= coefficient of x6 in [(1 + x)31 - (1 + x)21]
= 31C6 - 21C6

questions.question: If the coefficients of x^7 in (ax^2 + $\frac{1}{b x}$)^11 and x^-7 in (ax - $\frac{1}{b x^{2}}$)^11 are equal, then ab =
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of x7 in (ax2 + {tex}\frac{1}{b x}{/tex})11 and x-7 in (ax - {tex}\frac{1}{b x^{2}}{/tex})11 are equal, then ab =
questions.explanation:
General term in (ax2 + {tex}\frac{1}{b x}{/tex})11 is Tr+1 = 11Cr (ax2)11-r {tex}\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)^{r}{/tex} and this will contain x7 only if 22 - 2r - r = 7 i.e., if r = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T6 = 11C5 (ax2)6 {tex}\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)^{5}{/tex}
= 11C5 {tex}\left(\frac{a^{6}}{b^{5}}\right){/tex} x7
For (ax - {tex}\frac{1}{b x^{2}}{/tex})11
{tex}\mathrm{T}_{r+1}^{\prime}{/tex} = 11Cr (ax)11-r {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{b x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex} and this contains x-7 only if 11 - r - 2r = -7 i.e., if r = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{T}_{7}^{\prime}{/tex} = 11C6 (ax)5 {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{b x^{2}}\right)^{6}{/tex}
= 11C6 {tex}\left(\frac{a^{5}}{b^{6}}\right){/tex} x-7
According to the given condition,
11C5 {tex}\left(\frac{a^{6}}{b^{5}}\right){/tex} = 11C6 {tex}\left(\frac{a^{5}}{b^{6}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a6b6 = a5b5 ...[{tex}\because{/tex} 11C5 = 11C6]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ab = 1

questions.question: Let (1 + x^2)^2 (1 + x)^n = $\sum_\limits{k=0}^{n+4} a_{k} \cdot x^{k}$. If a_1, a_2, a_3 are in A.P., the n is equal to
questions.question_raw: Let (1 + x2)2 (1 + x)n = {tex}\sum_\limits{k=0}^{n+4} a_{k} \cdot x^{k}{/tex}. If a1, a2, a3 are in A.P., the n is equal to
questions.explanation:
L.H.S. = (1 + 2x2 + x4) (1 + C1x + C2x2 + C3x3 + ...)
R.H.S. = a0 + a1x + a2x2 + a3x3 + ...
Comparing the coefficients of x, x2, x3., we get
a1 = C1, a2 = C2 + 2, a3 = C3 + 2C1
a1, a2, a3 are in A.P. {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2a2 = a1 + a3
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2(nC2 + 2) = nC1 + (nC3 + 2 nC1)
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2 n(n-1)}{2}{/tex} + 4 = n + {tex}\frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{1 \times 2 \times 3}{/tex} + 2n
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n3 - 9n2 + 26n - 24 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (n - 2) (n - 3) (n - 4) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n = 2, 3, 4

questions.question: If |x| < 1, then the value of 1 + n $\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)+\frac{n(n+1)}{2 !}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)^{2}$ + ... is
questions.question_raw: If |x| < 1, then the value of 1 + n {tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)+\frac{n(n+1)}{2 !}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)^{2}{/tex} + ... is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[1-\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)\right]^{-n}{/tex} = 1 + (-n) {tex}\left[-\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)\right]+\frac{(-n)(-n-1)}{2 !}\left(-\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)^{2}{/tex} + ...
{tex}\Leftrightarrow\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x}\right)^{-n}{/tex} = 1 + n{tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)+\frac{n(n+1)}{2 !}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)^{2}{/tex} + ...
{tex}\Leftrightarrow\left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right)^{n}{/tex} = 1 + n {tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)+\frac{n(n+1)}{2 !}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x}\right)^{2}{/tex} + ...

questions.question: The sum of the series 1 + $\frac{3}{4}+\frac{3.5}{4.8}+\frac{3.5 .7}{4.8 .12}$ + ... is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the series 1 + {tex}\frac{3}{4}+\frac{3.5}{4.8}+\frac{3.5 .7}{4.8 .12}{/tex} + ... is
questions.explanation:
The factors in the numerator form an A.P. with a common difference 2. We therefore multiply and divide each one of them by 2. Let the sum be S. Then
S = 1 + {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{2}{4}\right)+\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)\left(\frac{5}{2}\right) \cdot \frac{1}{1 \cdot 2}\left(\frac{2}{4}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}+\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)\left(\frac{7}{2}\right) \cdot \frac{1}{1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3}\left(\frac{2}{4}\right)^{3}{/tex} + ...
= 1 + {tex}\frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}+\frac{\frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{5}{2}}{1 \cdot 2} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}+\frac{\frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{5}{2} \cdot \frac{7}{2}}{1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{3}{/tex} + ...
Let n = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}, x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
S = 1 + nx + {tex}\frac{n(n+1)}{2 !} x^{2}+\frac{n(n+1)(n+2)}{3 !}{/tex} x3 + ...
= (1 - x)-n
= {tex}\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{\frac{-3}{2}}=2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of x^50 in the expansion of S = (1 + x)^1000 + 2x(1 + x)^999 + 3x^2(1 + x)^998 + ... + 10001x^1000 is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x50 in the expansion of S = (1 + x)1000 + 2x(1 + x)999 + 3x2(1 + x)998 + ... + 10001x1000 is
questions.explanation:
S = (1 + x)1000 + 2x(1 + x)999 + 3x2(1 + x)998 + ... + 1000(1 + x) x999 + 1001 x1000 ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x S}{1+x}{/tex} = x(1 + x)999 + 2x2(1 + x)998 + ... + 1000 x1000 + 100{tex}\frac{x^{1001}}{1+x}{/tex} ...(ii)
(i) and (ii) give
S - {tex}\frac{x \mathrm{~S}}{1+x}{/tex}
= {(1 + x)1000 + x(1 + x)999 + x2(1 + x)998 + ... + x1000} - 1001{tex}\frac{x^{1001}}{1+x}{/tex}
L.H.S. = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{S}}{1+x}{/tex} 
and R.H.S.
= (1 + x)1000 {tex}\left\{\frac{1-\left(\frac{x}{1+x}\right)^{1001}}{1-\frac{x}{1+x}}\right\}{/tex} - 1001{tex}\frac{x^{1001}}{1+x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{S}{1+x}{/tex} = (1 + x)1001 - x1001 - 1001{tex}\frac{x^{1001}}{1+x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = (1 + x)1002 - x1001 (1 + x) - 1001 x1001
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = (1 + x)1002 - 1002 x1001 - x1002
 The coefficient of x50 in S
= coefficient of x50 in (1 + x)1002
= 1002C50

questions.question: Let S be the sum of the digits of the coefficient of x^6 in the expansion of (1 + 2x - 3x^2)^4. Then which of the following statements is not correct?
questions.question_raw: Let S be the sum of the digits of the coefficient of x6 in the expansion of (1 + 2x - 3x2)4. Then which of the following statements is not correct?

options.text: S is the square of an integer
G. C. D. of Sand 6 is 6

questions.explanation:
The general term of the expansion is
{tex}\frac{4 !}{p ! q ! r !}{/tex} {tex}\cdot{/tex} 1p (2x)q (-3x2)r, where p + q + r = 4
{tex}=\frac{4 !}{p ! q ! r !}{/tex} (2)q (-3)r xq+2r
We require p, q, r such that
p + q + r = 4 and q + 2r = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 1, q = 0 and r = 3
or p = 0, q = 2 and r = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The coefficient of x6
{tex}=\frac{4 !}{1 ! 0 ! 3 !}{/tex} 20 (-3)3 + {tex}\frac{4 !}{0 ! 2 ! 2 !}{/tex} (2)2 (-3)2
= -108 + 216 = 108
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = 9
S is the perfect square of an integer (true)
S is divisible by 3 and 9 (true)
S and 6 have G. C. D. = 3

questions.question: If in the expansion of $\left(x^{3}-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{n}$, n $\in$ N, sum of the coefficients of x^5 and x^10 is zero, then the value of n is
questions.question_raw: If in the expansion of {tex}\left(x^{3}-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{n}{/tex}, n {tex}\in{/tex} N, sum of the coefficients of x5 and x10 is zero, then the value of n is
questions.explanation:
Consider this option, n = 15
Tr+1 = 15Cr (x3)15-r {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}{/tex}
= (-1)r 15Cr x45-5r
Tr+1 will contain x5, if 45 - 5r = 5 i.e., if r = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The coefficient of x5 = (-1)8 15C8 = 15C8
Tr+1 will contain x10, if 45 - 5r = 10 i.e., if r = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The coefficient of x10 = (-1)7 15C7 = -15C7 = -15C8
Here, coefficient of x5 + coefficient of x10 = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} this option is correct.

questions.question: What is the value of $\frac{7^3+2^3+84}{7^2-2^2}$ ?
questions.question_raw: What is the value of {tex}\frac{7^3+2^3+84}{7^2-2^2}{/tex} ?
questions.explanation:
Using binomial theorem we know that {tex}(a+b)^3=a^3+3 a b^2+3 a^2 b+b^3{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}(7+2)^3=7^3+2^3+\left(3 \times 7 \times 2^2\right)+\left(3 \times 2 \times 7^2\right){/tex}

{tex} (9)^3=7^3+2^3+84+\left(3 \times 2 \times 7^2\right) {/tex}
{tex} 729=7^3+2^3+84+294 {/tex}
{tex} 7^3+2^3+84=435 {/tex}
Also {tex}7^2-2^2=(7-2)(7+2){/tex}

{tex} 7^2-2^2=(5)(9) {/tex}
{tex} 7^2-2^2=45 {/tex}

So {tex}\frac{7^3+2^3+84}{7^2-2^2}=435 / 45{/tex}

{tex} =9 \frac{30}{45} {/tex}
{tex} =9 \frac{2}{3} . {/tex}

questions.question: m and n are positive integers. In the expansion of (1 + x )^m+n, the coefficients of x^m and x^n are A and B respectively. Then
questions.question_raw: m and n are positive integers. In the expansion of (1 + x )m+n, the coefficients of xm and xn are A and B respectively. Then
questions.explanation:
Coefficients of xm and xn are m+nCm and m+nCn respectively
A = m+nCm = m+nC(m+n-m)
= m+nCn = B

questions.question: If A and B are the coefficients of x^n in the expansions of (1 + x)^2n and (1 + x)^2n-1 respectively, then
questions.question_raw: If A and B are the coefficients of xn in the expansions of (1 + x)2n and (1 + x)2n-1 respectively, then
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\text { Coefficient of } x^{n} \text { in expansion of }(1+x)^{2 n}}{\text { Coefficient of } x^{n} \text { in expansion of }(1+x)^{2 n-1}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n}}{{ }^{(2 n-1)} \mathrm{C}_{n}}=\frac{(2 n) !}{n ! n !} \times \frac{(n-1) ! n !}{(2 n-1) !}=\frac{2 n}{n}{/tex} = 2
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{A}{B}=\frac{2}{1} \Leftrightarrow{/tex} A = 2B

questions.question: The coefficients of r^th and (r + 1)^th terms in the expansion of (1 + x)^20 are in the ratio 1 : 2, then r =
questions.question_raw: The coefficients of rth and (r + 1)th terms in the expansion of (1 + x)20 are in the ratio 1 : 2, then r =

Tr+1 = 20Cr xr
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} coefficient of (r + 1)th term = 20Cr
and coeffiecient of rth term = 20Cr-1
Given, {tex}\frac{20 !}{(21-r) !(r-1) !} \times \frac{(20-r) ! r !}{20 !}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{r}{21-r}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2r = 21 - r {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} r = 7

questions.question: The value of x, for which the 6^th term in the expansion of $\left\{2^{\log _{2} \sqrt{9^{r-1}+7}}+\frac{1}{2^{\left(\frac{1}{5}\right) \log _{2}\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)}}\right\}^{7}$ is 84, is equal to
questions.question_raw: The value of x, for which the 6th term in the expansion of {tex}\left\{2^{\log _{2} \sqrt{9^{r-1}+7}}+\frac{1}{2^{\left(\frac{1}{5}\right) \log _{2}\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)}}\right\}^{7}{/tex} is 84, is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[2^{\log _{2} \sqrt{9^{x-1}+7}}+\frac{1}{2^{\left(\frac{1}{5}\right) \log _{2}\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)}}\right]^{7}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\sqrt{9^{x-1}+7}+\frac{1}{\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)^{\frac{1}{5}}}\right]^{7}{/tex} ...[{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}a^{\log _{a} m}{/tex} = m]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T6 = 7C5 {tex}\left(\sqrt{9^{x-1}+7}\right)^{7-5}\left[\frac{1}{\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)^{\frac{1}{5}}}\right]^{-5}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 84 = 7C5 (9x-1 + 7) {tex}\frac{1}{\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 7C5 {tex}\frac{\left(9^{x-1}+7\right)}{\left(3^{x-1}+1\right)}{/tex} = 84
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 9x-1 + 7 = 4(3x-1 + 1)
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 32x - 12(3x) + 27 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} y2 - 12y + 27 = 0, where y = 3x
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} y = 3, 9
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 3x = 3, 9 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x = 1, 2

questions.question: In the expansion $\left(\sqrt{2} \sqrt[6]{3}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[3]{3}}\right)^{n}$, if the ratio of 7^th term from the beginning to the 7^th term from the end is $\frac{1}{6}$, then n =
questions.question_raw: In the expansion {tex}\left(\sqrt{2} \sqrt[6]{3}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[3]{3}}\right)^{n}{/tex}, if the ratio of 7th term from the beginning to the 7th term from the end is {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}, then n =
questions.explanation:
7th term is {tex}\left[\sqrt[6]{3} \sqrt{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[3]{3}}\right]^{n}{/tex} = nC6 {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{2}} 3^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{n-6}\left[\frac{1}{3^{\frac{1}{ 3}}}\right]^{6}{/tex}
7th term from the end in {tex}\left[\sqrt[5]{3} \sqrt{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[3]{3}}\right]^{n}{/tex}
= nC6 {tex}\left(\frac{1}{3^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{n-6}\left(3^{\frac{1}{ 6}} 2^{\frac{1}{ 2}}\right)^{6}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{6}\left[2^{\frac{1}{ 2}} 3^{\frac{1}{ 6}}\right]^{n-6}\left[\frac{1}{3^{\frac{1}{ 3}}}\right]^{6}}{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{6}\left[\frac{1}{3^{\frac{1}{ 3}}}\right]^{n-6}\left[2^{\frac{1}{ 2}} 3^{\frac{1}{ 6}}\right]^{6}}=\frac{1}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2^{\frac{n-6}{2}} 3^{\frac{n-6}{6}}}{3^{\frac{6-n}{3}} 2^{3} \cdot 3^{1}} \cdot \frac{1}{3^{2}}=\frac{1}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2^{\frac{n-6}{2}}}{3^{\frac{6-n}{3}}} \cdot \frac{1}{\left.3^{-\left(\frac{n-6}{6}\right.}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{2^{3} 3^{3}}=\frac{1}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2^{\frac{n-6}{2}} .3^{\frac{n-6}{2}}{/tex} = 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 32
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{n-6}{2}{/tex} = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 10

questions.question: If the fifth term in the expansion of $\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{n}$ does not depend on x, then n is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the fifth term in the expansion of {tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{n}{/tex} does not depend on x, then n is equal to

t5 = nC4 {tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{n-4} \cdots\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{4}{/tex}
= nC4 {tex}x^{\frac{n-4}{3}} \cdot x^{-4}{/tex}
= nC4 {tex}x^{\frac{n-4}{3}-4}{/tex}
= nC4 {tex}x^{\frac{n-16}{3}}{/tex}
t5 is independent of x {tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{n-16}{3}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n = 16

questions.question: The ratio of coefficient of x^15 to the term independent of x in the expansion of (x^2 + $\frac{2}{x}$)^15 is:
questions.question_raw: The ratio of coefficient of x15 to the term independent of x in the expansion of (x2 + {tex}\frac{2}{x}{/tex})15 is:
questions.explanation:
General term
Tr+1 = 15Cr (x2)15-r {tex}\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^{r}{/tex}
= 15Cr x30-3r 2r ...(i)
This contains x15 if 30 - 3r = 15 i.e., if r = 5
 The coefficient of x15 = 15C5 25 = a (say)
Also, Tr+1 will be independent of x, if 30 - 3r = 0 i.e., if r = 10
The coefficient of constant term = 15C10 210 = b (say)
Hence, the required ratio = {tex}\frac{a}{b}=\frac{{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{5} 2^{5}}{{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{10} 2^{10}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2^{5}}=\frac{1}{32}{/tex} ...[{tex}\because{/tex} 15C5 = 15C10]

questions.question: The coefficient of the term independent of x in the expansion of (1 + x + 2x^3) $\left(\frac{3 x^{2}}{2}-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^{9}$ is

tr+1 of {tex}\left(\frac{3 x^{2}}{2}-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^{9}{/tex} = 9Cr {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2} x^{2}\right)^{r}\left(-\frac{1}{3 x}\right)^{9-r}{/tex}
= 9Cr {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{r}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{9-r}{/tex} x3r-9
tr+1 is independent of x, if 3r - 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 3
For r = 3, 9Cr {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{r}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{9-r}{/tex} = 9C3 {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{3}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{6}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{7}{18}{/tex}
tr+1 contains {tex}\frac{1}{x^{3}}{/tex}, if 3r - 9 = -3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 2
For r = 2, 9Cr {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{r}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{9-r}{/tex}
= 9C2 {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)^{7}=-\frac{1}{27}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Coefficient of the term independent of x in the given expression = {tex}\frac{-2}{27}+\frac{7}{18}=\frac{-4+21}{54}=\frac{17}{54}{/tex}

questions.question: The greatest term in the expansion of (1 + 3x)^54, when x = $\frac{1}{3}$, is
questions.question_raw: The greatest term in the expansion of (1 + 3x)54, when x = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}, is
questions.explanation:
Let Tr+1 and Tr be the (r + 1)th and rth terms respectively. Then,
Tr+1 = 54Cr (3x)r and Tr = 54Cr-1 (3x)r-1
{tex}\therefore\frac{T_{r+1}}{T_{r}}=\frac{54-r+1}{r} \times{/tex} |3x|
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{T}_{r+1}}{\mathrm{~T}_{r}}=\frac{55-r}{r}{/tex} ...[{tex}\because{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (given)]
Now, {tex}\frac{\mathrm{T}_{r+1}}{\mathrm{~T}_{r}}{/tex} > 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{55-r}{r}{/tex} > 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r < 27{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (27 + 1)th i.e., 28th term is the greatest term.

questions.question_raw: The value of (21C1 - 10C1) + (21C2 - 10C2) + (21C3 - 10C3) + (21C4 - 10C4) + ... + (21C10 - 10C10) is

questions.question: If (2x^2 - x - 1)^5 = a_0 + a_1x + a_2x^2 + ... + a_10x^10, then a_2 + a_4 + a_6 + a_8 + a_10 =
questions.question_raw: If (2x2 - x - 1)5 = a0 + a1x + a2x2 + ... + a10x10, then a2 + a4 + a6 + a8 + a10 =
questions.explanation:
(2x2 - x - 1)5 = a0 + a1x + a2x2 + ... + a10x10 ...(i)
Substituting x = 1, - 1 in (i), we get
0 = a0 + a1 + a2 + a3 + ... + a10 ...(ii)
25 = a0 - a1 + a2 - a3 + ... a10 ...(iii)
Adding (ii) and (iii), we get
25 = 2(a0 + a2 + ... + a10)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a0 + a2 + a4 + ... a10 = 24 = 16
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + a4 + a6 + a8 + a10 = 16 - a0
Substituting x = 0 in (i), we get
(-1)5 = a0
a0 = -1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + a4 + a6 + a8 + a10 = 16 + 1 = 17

questions.question: If the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (a^2x^2 - 6ax + 11)^10, where a is constant, is 1024, then the value of a is
questions.question_raw: If the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (a2x2 - 6ax + 11)10, where a is constant, is 1024, then the value of a is
