Since, sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (a2x2 - 6ax + 11)10 is 1024
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (a2 - 6a + 11)10 = 1024 = 210
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 - 6a + 11 = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 - 6a + 9 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - 3)2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 3

questions.question: If C_0, C_1, C_2, ..., C_n are the binomial coefficients, then 2$\cdot$C_1 + 2^3$\cdot$C_3 + 2^5$\cdot$C_5 + ... equals
questions.question_raw: If C0, C1, C2, ..., Cn are the binomial coefficients, then 2{tex}\cdot{/tex}C1 + 23{tex}\cdot{/tex}C3 + 25{tex}\cdot{/tex}C5 + ... equals
questions.explanation:
We know that,
[(1 + x)n - (1 - x)n] = 2[C1x + C3x3 + C5x5 + ...]
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}{/tex} [(1 + x)n - (1 - x)n] = C1x + C3x3 + C5x5 + ...
Substituting x = 2, we get,
2{tex}\cdot{/tex}C1 + 23{tex}\cdot{/tex}C3 + 25{tex}\cdot{/tex}C5 + ... = {tex}\frac{3^{n}-(-1)^{n}}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: (^10C_0)^2 - (^10C_1)^2 + ... - (^10C_9)^2 + (^10C_10)^2 equals
questions.question_raw: (10C0)2 - (10C1)2 + ... - (10C9)2 + (10C10)2 equals
questions.explanation:
If n is even, then
{tex}\mathrm{C}_{0}{ }^{2}-\mathrm{C}_{1}{}^{2}+\mathrm{C}_{2}{}^{2}-\mathrm{C}_{3}{}^{2}+\ldots=(-1)^{\frac{n}{2}}\left({ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{\frac{n}{2}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{0}{ }^{2}-{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{1}{ }^{2}+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{2}{ }^{2}-\ldots+{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{10}{ }^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=(-1)^{\frac{10}{2}}\left({ }^{10} \mathbf{C}_{\frac{10}{2}}\right){/tex}
= (-1)5 (10C5)
= - 10C5

questions.question: What are the coefficients of the first and the last term of (a + b)^n?
questions.question_raw: What are the coefficients of the first and the last term of (a + b)n?
questions.explanation: The coefficient of the first term and last term is same. The first term is nC1 an and the last term is nC0 bn unless, a and b are numbers that change the value of the coefficient.

questions.question: If the second term in the expansion of $\left(\sqrt[13]{a}+\frac{a}{\sqrt{a^{-1}}}\right)^{n}$ is 14$a^{\frac{5}{2}}$, then the value of $\frac{{ }^{n} C_{3}}{{ }^{n} C_{2}}$ is
questions.question_raw: If the second term in the expansion of {tex}\left(\sqrt[13]{a}+\frac{a}{\sqrt{a^{-1}}}\right)^{n}{/tex} is 14{tex}a^{\frac{5}{2}}{/tex}, then the value of {tex}\frac{{ }^{n} C_{3}}{{ }^{n} C_{2}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have,
nC1 {tex}(\sqrt[13]{a})^{n-1}\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{a^{-1}}}\right)^{\prime}{/tex} = 14{tex}a^{\frac{5}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow n a^{\frac{n-1}{13}+\frac{3}{2}}{/tex} = 14{tex}a^{\frac{5}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow n a^{\frac{n-1}{13}}{/tex} = 14a
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} n = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{3}}{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{2}}=\frac{n-2}{3}{/tex} ... {tex}\left[\because \frac{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{r}-1}=\frac{n-r+1}{r}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{14-2}{3}{/tex} = 4

questions.question: The coefficients of three successive terms in the expansion of (1 + x)^n are 165, 330 and 462 respectively, then the value of n is
questions.question_raw: The coefficients of three successive terms in the expansion of (1 + x)n are 165, 330 and 462 respectively, then the value of n is
questions.explanation:
Let the coefficient of three consecutive terms be (r + 1)th, (r + 2)th, (r + 3)th in the expansion of (1 + x)n
Then, coefficient of (r + 1)th term = nCr = 165, coefficient of (r + 2)th term = nCr+1 = 330 and coefficient of (r + 3)th term = nCr+2 = 462
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{r+1}}{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{r}}=\frac{n-r}{r+1}{/tex}  = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n - r = 2(r + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (n - 2) ...(i)
and {tex}\frac{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{r+2}}{{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{r+1}}=\frac{n-r-1}{r+2}=\frac{231}{165}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 165(n - r - 1) = 231(r + 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 165n - 627 = 396r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 165n - 627 = 396 {tex}\times \frac{1}{3} \times{/tex} (n - 2) ...[From (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 165n - 627 = 132(n - 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 11

questions.question: If (1 + x)^n = C_0 + C_1x + C_2x^2 + ... + C_nx^n, then the value of C_0 + 2C_1 + 3C_2 + ... + (n + 1)C_n is
questions.question_raw: If (1 + x)n = C0 + C1x + C2x2 + ... + Cnxn, then the value of C0 + 2C1 + 3C2 + ... + (n + 1)Cn is
questions.explanation:
We have,
C0 + 2C1 + 3C2 + ... + (n + 1) Cn
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (r + 1)Cr
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (r + 1) nCr
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (r{tex}\cdot{/tex}nCr + nCr)
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} r{tex}\cdot{/tex}nCr + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr
{tex}=\sum_\limits{r=1}^{n} r \cdot \frac{n}{r}{/tex} n-1Cr-1 + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr ... [{tex}\because{/tex} nCr = {tex}\frac{n}{r}\cdot{/tex} n-1Cr-1]
= n({tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{n}{/tex} n-1Cr-1) + ({tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} nCr)
= n(n-1C0 + n-1C1 + ... + n-1Cn-1) + (nC0 + nC1 + ... + nCn)
= n{tex}\cdot{/tex}2n-1 + 2n
= n{tex}\cdot{/tex}2n-1 + 2{tex}\cdot{/tex}(2n-1)
= (n + 2){tex}\cdot{/tex}2n-1

questions.question: Let S_n = C_0 + 3C_1 + 5C_2 + 7C_3 + ... + (2n + 1) C_n where C_r = ^nC_r. If S_9 = 2^a3^b5^c, then
questions.question_raw: Let Sn = C0 + 3C1 + 5C2 + 7C3 + ... + (2n + 1) Cn where Cr = nCr. If S9 = 2a3b5c, then
questions.explanation:
Sn = C0 + 3C1 + 5C2+ 7C3 + ... + (2n + 1) Cn
= (C0 + C1 + C2 + C3 + ... + Cn) + 2(C1 + 2C2 + 3C3 + ... + nCn)
= 2n + 2[n + n(n - 1) + {tex}\frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{2}{/tex} + ... + n]
= 2n + 2n [1 + (n - 1) + {tex}\frac{(n-1)(n-2)}{2}{/tex} + ... + 1]
= 2n + 2n (1 + 1)n-1
= 2n + n(2n) = (n + 1) 2n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S9 = 10(29) = 5(210)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 10, b = 0, c = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} abc = 0, a + c = 11,a + b + c = 11
and ab + bc + ca = 10

questions.question: If C_0, C_1, C_2, ..., C_n denote the binomial coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x)^n, then $\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}$ (-1)^r ^nC_r = $\frac{1+r \log _{e} 10}{\left(1+\log _{e} 10^{n}\right)^{r}}$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If C0, C1, C2, ..., Cn denote the binomial coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x)n, then {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr = {tex}\frac{1+r \log _{e} 10}{\left(1+\log _{e} 10^{n}\right)^{r}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Let loge 10 = x. Then,
{tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\frac{1+r \log _{e} 10}{\left(1+\log _{e} 10^{n}\right)^{r}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\frac{1+r x}{(1+n x)^{r}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\left(\frac{1}{1+n x}\right)^{r}{/tex} + {tex}\sum_\limits{r=1}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r {tex}\frac{n}{r}{/tex} (n-1Cr-1) {tex}\frac{r x}{(1+n x)^{r}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sum_\limits{r=0}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r nCr {tex}\left(\frac{1}{1+n x}\right)^{r}{/tex}- {tex}\frac{n x}{1+n x} \cdot \sum_\limits{r=1}^{n}{/tex} (-1)r-1 n-1Cr-1 {tex}\left(\frac{1}{1+n x}\right)^{r-1}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(1-\frac{1}{1+n x}\right)^{n}-\frac{n x}{1+n x}\left(1-\frac{1}{1+n x}\right)^{n-1}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{n x}{1+n x}\right)^{n}-\left(\frac{n x}{1+n x}\right)^{n}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: The sum of the series ^20C_0 - ^20C_1 + ^20C_2 - ^20C_3 + ... + ^20C_10 is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the series 20C0 - 20C1 + 20C2 - 20C3 + ... + 20C10 is
questions.explanation:
We know that,
(1 + x)20 = 20C0 + 20C1x + 20C2x2 + 20C3x3 + ... + 20C10x10 + ... + 20C20x20
Substituting x = -1, we get
0 = 20C0 - 20C1 + 20C2 - 20C3 + ... + 20C10 - ... + 20C20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0 = 2 (20C0 - 20C1 + 20C2 - 20C3 + ... - 20C9) + 20C10 [{tex}\because{/tex} 20C0 - 20C20, 20C1 = 20C19, ..., 20C9 = 20C11]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20C0 - 20C1 + 20C2 - 20C3 + ... - 20C9
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (20C10)
Adding 20C10 to both sides, we get
20C0 - 20C1 + 20C2 - 20C3 + ... - 20C9 + 20C10
= 20C10 - {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (20C10) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (20C10)

questions.question: The sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (x + y)^n is 4096. The greatest coefficient in the expansion is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (x + y)n  is 4096. The greatest coefficient in the expansion is
questions.explanation:
Sum of coefficients of (x +y)n = 2n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2n = 4096 = 212
{tex}\therefore{/tex} n = 12
Since n is even, hence greatest coefficient
= {tex}{ }^{n} \mathrm{C}_{\frac{n}{2}}={ }^{12} \mathrm{C}_{6}=\frac{12.11 .10 .9 .8 .7}{1.2 .3 .4 .5 .6}{/tex} = 924

questions.question: If the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (1 - 3x + 10x^2)^n is a and if the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x^2)^n is b, then
questions.question_raw: If the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (1 - 3x + 10x2)n is a and if the sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x2)n is b, then
questions.explanation:
We have, a = sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (1 - 3x + 10x2)n
Putting x = 1, we get
a = (1 - 3 + 10)n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = (8)n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = (2)3n = (2n)3 ...(i)
and b = sum of the coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x2)n
Putting x = 1, we get
b = (1 + 1)n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 2n ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
a = b3

questions.question: C_0 - C_1 + C_2 - C_3 + ... + (-1)^n C_n is equal to
questions.question_raw: C0 - C1 + C2 - C3 + ... + (-1)n Cn is equal to
questions.explanation:
We know that,
(1 + x)n = nC0 + nC1x + nC2x2 + ... + nCnxn
Substituting x = -1, we get
(1 - 1)n = nC0 - nC1 + nC2 - ... (-1)n nCn
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C0 - C1 + C2 - C3 + ... (-1)n Cn = 0

questions.question: If n is an integer greater than 1, then a - ^nC_1 (a - 1) + ^nC_2 (a - 2) - ... + (-1)^n (a - n) is equal to
questions.question_raw: If n is an integer greater than 1, then a - nC1 (a - 1) + nC2 (a - 2) - ... + (-1)n (a - n) is equal to
questions.explanation:
a[C0 - C1 + C2 - C3 + .. + (-1)n Cn] + [C1 - 2C2 + 3C3 - ... + (-1)n-1 n {tex}\cdot{/tex} Cn]
= a{tex}\cdot{/tex}0 + 0 = 0

questions.question: The sum of the last 30 coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x)^59 is
questions.question_raw: The sum of the last 30 coefficients in the expansion of (1 + x)59 is
questions.explanation:
Required sum = 59C30 + 59C31 + 59C32 + ... + 59C59
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} [(59C29 + 59C30) + (59C28 + 59C31) + ... + (59C0 + 59C59)] ... [{tex}\because{/tex} nCr = nCn-r]
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (59C0 + 59C1 + 59C2 + ... + 59C59)
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (259) = 258

questions.question: In the expansion of $\left(1+\frac{x}{1 !}+\frac{x^{2}}{2 !}+\ldots+\frac{x^{n}}{n !}\right)^{2}$, the coefficient of x^n is
questions.question_raw: In the expansion of {tex}\left(1+\frac{x}{1 !}+\frac{x^{2}}{2 !}+\ldots+\frac{x^{n}}{n !}\right)^{2}{/tex}, the coefficient of xn is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(1+\frac{x}{1 !}+\frac{x^{2}}{2 !}+\ldots+\frac{x^{n}}{n !}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(1+x+\frac{x^{2}}{2 !}+\ldots+\frac{x^{n}}{n !}\right) \cdot\left(1+x+\frac{x^{2}}{2 !}+\ldots+\frac{x^{n}}{n !}\right){/tex}
Coefficient of xn
{tex}=\frac{1}{n !}+\frac{1}{(n-1) !}+\frac{1}{2 !(n-2) !}+\ldots+\frac{1}{n !}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{n !}\left[1+\frac{n !}{1 !(n-1) !}+\frac{n !}{2 !(n-2) !}+\ldots+\frac{n !}{n ! 0 !}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{n !}{/tex} [nC0 + nC1 + nC2 + ... + nCn]
= {tex}\frac{2^{n}}{n !}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of ^14C_1 + ^14C_3 + ^14C_5 + ... + ^14C_11 is
questions.question_raw: The value of 14C1 + 14C3 + 14C5 + ... + 14C11 is
questions.explanation:
14C1 + 14C3 + ... + 14C11 + 14C13 = 214-1 = 213
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 14C1 + 14C3 + ... + 14C11 = 213 - 14C13
= 213 - 14

questions.question: Sum of odd terms is A and sum of even terms is B in the expansion (x + a)^n, then
questions.question_raw: Sum of odd terms is A and sum of even terms is B in the expansion (x + a)n, then
questions.explanation:
(x + a)n = nC0 xn + nC1 xn-1 a + nC2 xn-2 a2 + nC3 xn-3 a3 + ...
According to the given condition,
A = nC0 xn + nC2 xn-2 a2 + nC4 xn-4 a4 + ... and
B = nC1 xn-1 a + nC3 xn-3 a3 + ...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + a)n = A + B and (x - a)n = A - B
But, 4AB = (A + B)2 - (A - B)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4AB = (x + a)2n - (x - a)2n

questions.question: If the ratio of the fifth term from the beginning to the fifth term from the end in the expansion of $\left(\sqrt[4]{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[4]{3}}\right)^n$ is $\sqrt 6$ : 1, then the third term from the beginning is:
questions.question_raw: If the ratio of the fifth term from the beginning to the fifth term from the end in the expansion of {tex}\left(\sqrt[4]{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[4]{3}}\right)^n{/tex} is {tex}\sqrt 6{/tex} : 1, then the third term from the beginning is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac {\text {Fifth term from begining}}{\text {Fifth term from end}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{ }^n C_4 2^{\frac{n-4}{4}} \cdot\left(3^{\frac{-1}{4}}\right)^4}{{ }^n C_4 3^{-\left(\frac{n-4}{4}\right)} \cdot\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)^4}=\frac{\sqrt{6}}{1} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 10
So T3 = T2+1 = 10C2{tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)^8 \cdot\left(3^{-\frac{1}{4}}\right)^2=\frac{45.4}{\sqrt{3}}=60 \sqrt{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of $\frac{1}{1 ! 50 !}+\frac{1}{3 ! 48 !}+\frac{1}{5 ! 46 !}$ +...+ $\frac{1}{49 ! 2 !}+\frac{1}{51 ! 1 !}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}\frac{1}{1 ! 50 !}+\frac{1}{3 ! 48 !}+\frac{1}{5 ! 46 !}{/tex} +...+ {tex}\frac{1}{49 ! 2 !}+\frac{1}{51 ! 1 !}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{26}{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{(2 r-1) !(51-(2 r-1)) !}{/tex}
= {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{26}{/tex}{tex}\frac{51 !}{51 !(2 r-1) !(51-(2 r-1)) !}{/tex} = {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{26}{/tex} 51C(2r - 1) {tex}\frac{1}{51 !}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{51 !}{/tex}{51C1 + 51C3 +...+ 51C51} = {tex}\frac{1}{51 !}{/tex}(250)

questions.question: If the coefficient of x^15 in the expansion of $\left(a x^3+\frac{1}{b x^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{15}$ is equal to the coefficient of x^-15 in the expansion of $\left(\mathrm{ax}^{\frac{1}{3}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{bx}^3}\right)^{15}$, where a and b are positive real numbers, then for each such ordered pair (a, b):
questions.question_raw: If the coefficient of x15 in the expansion of {tex}\left(a x^3+\frac{1}{b x^{\frac{1}{3}}}\right)^{15}{/tex} is equal to the coefficient of x-15 in the expansion of {tex}\left(\mathrm{ax}^{\frac{1}{3}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{bx}^3}\right)^{15}{/tex}, where a and b are positive real numbers, then for each such ordered pair (a, b):
questions.explanation:
Since general term in the expansion of {tex}\left(a x^3+\frac{1}{b x^{1 / 3}}\right)^{15}{/tex} is given by
15Cr(ax3)15-r{tex}\left(\frac{1}{b x^{1 / 3}}\right)^r{/tex}
Now 45 - 3r - {tex}\frac r3{/tex} = 15 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 30 = {tex}\frac {10r}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 9
So, coefficient of x15 = 15C9 a6b-9
and general term in {tex}\left(\mathrm{ax}^{1 / 3}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{bx}^3}\right)^{15}{/tex}
= 15Cr{tex}\left(\mathrm{ax}^\frac {1}{ 3}\right)^{15-\mathrm{r}}\left(-\frac{1}{\mathrm{bx}^3}\right)^{\mathrm{r}}{/tex}
Now, 5 - {tex}\frac r3{/tex} - 3r = -15 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac {10r}{3}{/tex} = 20 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
So coefficient = 15C6 a9b-6
Since, given {tex}\frac{a^9}{b^6}=\frac{a^6}{b^9} \Rightarrow{/tex} a3b3 = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ab = 1

questions.question: Let x = $(8 \sqrt{3}+13)^{13}$ and y = $(7 \sqrt{2}+9)^9$. If [t] denotes the greatest integer $\leq$ t, then
questions.question_raw: Let x = {tex}(8 \sqrt{3}+13)^{13}{/tex} and y = {tex}(7 \sqrt{2}+9)^9{/tex}. If [t] denotes the greatest integer {tex}\leq{/tex} t, then
questions.explanation:
x = {tex}(8 \sqrt{3}+13)^{13}{/tex} = 13C0({tex}8 \sqrt{3}{/tex})13 + 13C1({tex}8 \sqrt{3}{/tex})12(13)1+ ...(i)
x' = ({tex}8 \sqrt{3}{/tex} - 13)13 = 13C0({tex}8 \sqrt{3}{/tex})13 - 13C1({tex}8 \sqrt{3}{/tex})12(13)1 + ...(ii)
By (i) - (ii)
x - x' = {tex}2\left[{ }^{13} \mathrm{C}_1(8 \sqrt{3})^{12}(13)^1+{ }^{13} \mathrm{C}_3(8 \sqrt{3})^{10} \cdot(13)^3 \ldots\right]{/tex}
therefore, x - x' is even integer, hence [x] is even
Now, y = {tex}(7 \sqrt{2}+9)^9{/tex} = 9C0{tex}(7 \sqrt{2})^9{/tex} + 9C1 {tex}(7 \sqrt{2})^8{/tex} (9)1 + ...(iii)
y' = {tex}(7 \sqrt{2}-9)^9{/tex} = 9C0{tex}(7 \sqrt{2})^9{/tex} - 9C1{tex}(7 \sqrt{2})^8{/tex} (9)1 + ...(iv)
By (iii) - (iv)
y - y' = {tex}2\left[{ }^9 \mathrm{C}_1(7 \sqrt{2})^8(9)^1+{ }^9 \mathrm{C}_3(7 \sqrt{2})^6(9)^3+...\right]{/tex}
y - y' = Even integer hence [y] is even.

questions.question: If a_r is the coefficient of x^10-r in the Binomial expansion of (1 + x)^10, then $\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{10}$r^3$\left(\frac{a_r}{a_{r-1}}\right)^2$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If ar is the coefficient of x10-r in the Binomial expansion of (1 + x)10, then {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{10}{/tex}r3{tex}\left(\frac{a_r}{a_{r-1}}\right)^2{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
As per given condition,
ar = 10C10-r = 10Cr
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{10}{/tex}r3{tex}\left(\frac{{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}}\right)^2{/tex} = {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{10}{/tex}r3{tex}\left(\frac{11-r}{r}\right)^2{/tex} = {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{10}{/tex}r(11 - r)2
{tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm r=1}^{10}{/tex}(121r + r3 - 22r2)
= 121 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 + 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 121 - 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 1210

questions.question: For two positive real numbers a and b such that $\frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{1}{b^3}$ = 4, the minimum value of the constant term in the expansion of $\left(a x^{\frac{1}{8}}+b x^{-\frac{1}{12}}\right)^{10}$ is:
questions.question_raw: For two positive real numbers a and b such that {tex}\frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{1}{b^3}{/tex} = 4, the minimum value of the constant term in the expansion of {tex}\left(a x^{\frac{1}{8}}+b x^{-\frac{1}{12}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
General term of {tex}\left(a x^{\frac {1}{8}}+b x^{\frac{-1}{12}}\right)^{10}{/tex} is
= 10Cr{tex}\left(a x^{\frac {1}{8}}\right)^{10-r}\left(b x^{\frac {-1}{12}}\right)^r{/tex}
On solving r = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(a^4 b^6\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \geq \frac{4}{2\left(\frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{1}{b^2}\right)} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a4b6 {tex}\geq{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10C6{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^4{/tex} = {tex}\frac{210}{16} \Rightarrow \frac{105}{8}{/tex}

questions.question: If n is the number of irrational terms in the expansion of $\left(3^{\frac{1}{4}}+5^{\frac{1}{8}}\right)^{60}$, then (n - 1) is divisible by:
questions.question_raw: If n is the number of irrational terms in the expansion of {tex}\left(3^{\frac{1}{4}}+5^{\frac{1}{8}}\right)^{60}{/tex}, then (n - 1) is divisible by:
questions.explanation:
General term:
Tr+1 = {tex}{ }^{60} C_r \cdot\left(3^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)^{60-r} \cdot\left(5^{\frac{1}{8}}\right)^r{/tex} = {tex}{ }^{60} C_r \cdot 3^{15-\frac{r}{4}} \cdot 5^{\frac{r}{8}}{/tex}
Term will be rational if r is divisible by 8.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} r = 0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56
So, total number of irrational terms = n = 61 - 8 = 53
Hence, n - 1 = 52 is divisible by 26.

questions.question: The coefficient of x^5 in the expansion of $\left(2 x^3-\frac{1}{3 x^2}\right)^5$ is
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of x5 in the expansion of {tex}\left(2 x^3-\frac{1}{3 x^2}\right)^5{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
The general term is
Tr+1 = {tex}{ }^5 C_r\left(2 x^3\right)^{5-r}\left(\frac{-1}{3 x^2}\right)^r{/tex} = {tex}{ }^5 C_r \frac{(2)^{5-r}}{(-3)^r}(x)^{15-5 r}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 15 - 5r = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T3 = 10{tex}\left(\frac{8}{9}\right){/tex}x5
So, coefficient is {tex}\frac{80}{9}{/tex}

questions.question: The sum, of the coefficients of the first 50 terms in the binomial expansion of (1 - x )^100, is equal to
questions.question_raw: The sum, of the coefficients of the first 50 terms in the binomial expansion of (1 - x )100, is equal to
questions.explanation:
By Binomial expression
(1 - x)100 = C0 - C1x + C2x2 - ... C99x99 + C100x100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C0 - C1 + C2 - C3 + ... -C99 + C100 = 0
2(C0 - C1 + C2 + ... -C9 + C50 = 0
C0 - C1 + C2 + ...C99 = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}C50
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \frac{100 !}{50 ! 50 !}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{100 ! \times 99 !}{50 ! 50 !}{/tex} = -99C49

questions.question: The sum of the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of (1 + x)^n+2, which are in the ratio 1 : 3 : 5, is equal to
questions.question_raw: The sum of the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the binomial expansion of (1 + x)n+2, which are in the ratio 1 : 3 : 5, is equal to
questions.explanation:
n+2Cr-1 : n+2Cr : n+2Cr+1 = 1 : 3 : 5
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{ }^{n+2} C_{r-1}}{{ }^{n+2} C_r}=\frac{1}{3} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 4r - 3 ...(i)
{tex}\frac{{ }^{n+2} C_r}{{ }^{n+2} C_{r+1}}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8r - 1 = 3n ...(ii)
From, (i) and (ii), r = 2 and n = 5
Required sum = 63

questions.question: If the coefficient of x^7 in $\left(a x-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^{13}$ and the coefficient of x^-5 in $\left(a x+\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^{13}$ are equal, then a^4b^4 is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the coefficient of x7 in {tex}\left(a x-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^{13}{/tex} and the coefficient of x-5 in {tex}\left(a x+\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^{13}{/tex} are equal, then a4b4 is equal to:
questions.explanation:
The general term is Tr+1 = 13Cr(ax)13-r{tex}\left(-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^r{/tex}
= 13Cr(a)13-r{tex}\left(-\frac{1}{b}\right)^r{/tex}x13-3r
Since 13 - 3r = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 2
Coefficient of x7 = 13C2(a)11{tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{b^2}{/tex}
Now, Tr+1 = 13Cr(ax)13-r{tex}\left(\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^{\mathrm{r}}{/tex}
13 - 3r = -5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
Coefficient of x-5 = 13C6(a)7{tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{b^6}{/tex}
Since, {tex}{ }^{13} C_2 \frac{a^{11}}{b^2}={ }^{13} C_6 \frac{a^7}{b^6}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a4b4 = {tex}\frac{{ }^{13} C_6}{{ }^{13} C_2}{/tex} = 22

questions.question: If the coefficients of x and x^2 in (1 + x)^p (1 - x)^q are 4 and -5 respectively, then 2p + 3q is equal to
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of x and x2 in (1 + x)p (1 - x)q are 4 and -5 respectively, then 2p + 3q is equal to
questions.explanation:
(1 + x)P (1 - x)q [{tex}\because{/tex} using binomial expansion]
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(1+p x+\frac{p(p-1)}{2 !} x^2+\ldots . .\right) \times{/tex}{tex}\left(1-q x+\frac{q(q-1)}{2 !} x^2-\ldots \ldots\right){/tex}
Comparing coefficient of x and x2
p - q = 4, ...(i)
{tex}\frac{p(p-1)}{2}+\frac{q(q-1)}{2}-p q{/tex} = -5 ...(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p2 + q2 - p - q - 2pq = -10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (p - q)2 - p - q = -10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 - p - q = -10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p + q = 26 ...(iii)
q = 11, p = 15
Now, 2p + 3q = 2(15) + 3(11) = 63

questions.question: Let the number (22)^2022 + (2022)^22 leave the remainder a when divided by 3 and $\beta$ when divided by 7. Then ($\alpha^2$ + $\beta^2$) is equal to
questions.question_raw: Let the number (22)2022 + (2022)22 leave the remainder a when divided by 3 and {tex}\beta{/tex} when divided by 7. Then ({tex}\alpha^2{/tex} + {tex}\beta^2{/tex}) is equal to
questions.explanation:
(22)2022 + (2022)22
divided by 3,
(21 + 1)2022 + (2022)22 [{tex}\therefore{/tex} As 2022 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 674]
3k + 2 (Remainder = {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 1)
Divided by 7
(21 + 1)2022 + (2023 - 1)22
7k + 2({tex}\beta{/tex} = 2) [{tex}\therefore{/tex} As 2023 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 289]
So, {tex}\alpha^2 + \beta^2{/tex} = 5

questions.question: If the coefficients of the three consecutive terms in the expansion of (1 + x )^n are in the ratio 1 : 5 : 20, then the coefficient of the fourth term is
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of the three consecutive terms in the expansion of (1 + x )n are in the ratio 1 : 5 : 20, then the coefficient of the fourth term is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{" \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{{ }^{\prime \prime} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}}=5\space \& \space \frac{{ }^{\prime \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}+1}}}{{ }^{\prime \prime} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{/tex} = 4
{tex}\frac{n !}{r !(n-r) !} \times \frac{(r-1) !(n-r+1) !}{n !}{/tex} = 5 & {tex}\frac{n !}{(r+1) !(n-r-1) !} \times \frac{r !(n-r) !}{n !}{/tex} = 4
{tex}\frac{n-r+1}{r}{/tex} = 5 & n = 5r + 4
n = 6r - 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} n = 29, r = 5
Coefficient of 4th term = 29C3 = 3654

questions.question: The absolute difference of the coefficients of x^10 and x^7 in the expansion of $\left(2 x^2+\frac{1}{2 x}\right)^{11}$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: The absolute difference of the coefficients of x10 and x7 in the expansion of {tex}\left(2 x^2+\frac{1}{2 x}\right)^{11}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Tr+1 = {tex}{ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\left(2 \mathrm{x}^2\right)^{11-\mathrm{r}}\left(\frac{1}{2 \mathrm{x}}\right)^{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} = 11Cr211-2r x22-3r
For coefficients of x10 & x7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 22 - 3r = 10 and 22 - 3r = 7
r = 4 and r = 5; So, coefficient of x10 = 11C4{tex}\cdot{/tex}23
and coefficient of x7 = 11C5{tex}\cdot{/tex}21
difference = 11C4{tex}\cdot{/tex}23 - 11C5{tex}\cdot{/tex}2
= {tex}\frac{11 \times 10 \times 9 \times 8}{24} \times 8-\frac{11 \times 10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{120} \times 2{/tex}
= 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 - 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 7
= 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (20 - 7) = 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 12(12 - 1)(12 + 1)
= 123 - 12

questions.question: If the coefficients of x^7 in $\left(a x^2+\frac{1}{2 b x}\right)^{11}$ and x^-7 in $\left(a x-\frac{1}{3 b x^2}\right)^{11}$ are equal, then
questions.question_raw: If the coefficients of x7 in {tex}\left(a x^2+\frac{1}{2 b x}\right)^{11}{/tex} and x-7 in {tex}\left(a x-\frac{1}{3 b x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex} are equal, then
questions.explanation:
General terms of expansion
{tex}\left(a x^2+\frac{1}{2 b x}\right)^{11} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Tr+1 = {tex}{ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_r\left(a x^2\right)^{11-r} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2 b x}\right)^r{/tex}
= {tex}{ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_r a^{11-r} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2 b}\right)^r \cdot x^{22-2 r-r}{/tex} = {tex}{ }^{11} C_r \cdot a^{11-r} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{2 b}\right)^r \cdot x^{22-3 r}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2 - 3r = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 5
Again, general term of expansion of {tex}\left(a x-\frac{1}{3 b x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Tr+1 = 11Cr(ax)11-r{tex}\left(-\frac{1}{3 b x^2}\right)^r{/tex}
= 11Cra11-r{tex}\left(\frac{-1}{3 b}\right)^r{/tex}x11-r-2r {tex}\therefore{/tex} 11 - 3r = -7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6
Now, {tex}\frac{{ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_5 a^6}{32 b^5}=\frac{{ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_6 \cdot a^5}{3^6 \cdot b^6} \Rightarrow{/tex} 729 ab = 32

(30C0)2 + 1{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C1)2 + 2{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C2)2 +... 30{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C30)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = 30{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C0)2 + 29{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C1)2 + 28{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C2)2 +...+ 0{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C0)2
then, 2S = 30{tex}\cdot{/tex}(30C02 + 30C12 +...+ 30C302)
S = 15{tex}\cdot{/tex}60C30 = 15{tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\frac{60 !}{(30 !)^2}{/tex}; {tex}\frac{15.10 !}{(30 !)^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\alpha 60 !}{(30 !)^2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \alpha{/tex} = 15

questions.question: The remainder when (2021)^2022 + (2022)^2021 is divided by 7 is
questions.question_raw: The remainder when (2021)2022 + (2022)2021 is divided by 7 is
questions.explanation:
(2021)2022 + (2022)2021 [ {tex}\because{/tex} 2023 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 289]
= (2023 - 2)2022 + (2023 - 1)2021
= 7k1 + 22022 + 7k2 - 1 = 7(k1 + k2) + 8674 - 1
= 7(k1 + k2) + (7 - 1)674 - 1 = 7(k1 + k2) + 7k3 + 1 - 1
= k1 + k2 + k3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Given number is divisible by 7 hence remainder is zero.

questions.question: Let (a + bx + cx^2)^10 = $\sum \limits_{\mathrm i=0}^{20}$P_ix^i, a, b, c $\in$ N. If p_1 = 20 and p_2 = 210, then 2(a + b + c) is equal to

questions.explanation:
Given, (a + bx + cx2)10 = {tex}\sum\limits _{\mathrm{i}=0}^{20} \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{i}}{/tex}
Coefficient of x1 = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20 = {tex}\frac{10 !}{9 ! 1 !} \times{/tex} a9 {tex}\times{/tex} b1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a9{tex}\cdot{/tex}b = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 1, b = 2
Coefficient of x2 = 210
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 210 = {tex}\frac{10 !}{9 ! 1 !} \times{/tex} a9 {tex}\times{/tex} c1 + {tex}\frac{10 !}{8 ! 2 !} \times{/tex} a8b2
210 = 10.c + 45 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10c = 30 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = 3
So, 2(a + b + c) = 12

questions.question:
The coefficient of x^101 in the expression
(5 + x)^500 + x(5 + x)^499 + x^2(5 + x)^498 +...x^500, x > 0, is
questions.question_raw:
The coefficient of x101 in the expression
(5 + x)500 + x(5 + x)499 + x2(5 + x)498 +...x500, x > 0, is
questions.explanation:
Given expression is
(5 + x)500 + x(5 + x)499 + x2(5 + x)498 +...+ x500
Take (5 + x)500 common, then it forms GP.
{tex}\left[(5+x)^{500}\left(\left(\frac{x}{5+x}\right)^{501}-1\right)\right]\left(\frac{x}{5+x}\right)-1{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(5+x)^{501}-x^{501}}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Coefficient x101 in given expression is calculated by
{tex}\left(\frac{1}{5}(x+5)^{501}\right){/tex} only = {tex}\frac{{ }^{501} C_{101} 5^{400}}{5}{/tex} = 501C1015399

questions.question: Let K be the sum of the coefficients of the odd powers of x in the expansion of (1 + x)^99. Let a be the middle term in the expansion of $\left(2+\frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}\right)^{200}$. If $\frac{{ }^{200} \mathrm{C}_{99} \mathrm{~K}}{\mathrm{a}}=\frac{2^{\ell} \mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{n}}$, where m and n are odd numbers, then the ordered pair. $(\ell, n)$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: Let K be the sum of the coefficients of the odd powers of x in the expansion of (1 + x)99. Let a be the middle term in the expansion of {tex}\left(2+\frac{1}{\sqrt{1}}\right)^{200}{/tex}. If {tex}\frac{{ }^{200} \mathrm{C}_{99} \mathrm{~K}}{\mathrm{a}}=\frac{2^{\ell} \mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{n}}{/tex}, where m and n are odd numbers, then the ordered pair. {tex}(\ell, n){/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given expansion is (1 + x)99
{tex}\because{/tex} K = nC1 + nC3 +...+ nC99 = 298
a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Middle term in the expansion of {tex}\left(2+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{200}{/tex}
i.e., {tex}\left(\frac{n}{2}+1\right)^{\text {th }}{/tex} = (100 + 1)th term.
T100+1 = 200C100(2)100{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{100}{/tex} = 200C100{tex}\cdot{/tex}250
So, {tex}\frac{{ }^{200} C_{99} \times 2^{98}}{{ }^{200} C_{100} \times 2^{50}}=\frac{100}{101} \times 2^{48}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{25}{101} \times 2^{50}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{m}{n} 2^{\ell} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = 25, n = 101 and {tex}\ell{/tex} = 50
{tex}(\ell,n){/tex} become (50, 101)

20(1 + x)19 = C1 + 2C2x +... + 20C20x19
Multiply by x both side
20x(1 + x)19 + 20. x. 19(1 + x)18
= C1 + 4C2x +...+ 202C20x19
Let x = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 19.218 + 20.219 = C1 + 4C2 +...+ 202C20
= 420 {tex}\times{/tex} 218

questions.question: $\sum \limits_{\mathrm t=1}^{20}$(r^2 + 1) (r!) is equal to:
questions.question_raw: {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm t=1}^{20}{/tex}(r2 + 1) (r!) is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given expression is {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm x=1}^{20}{/tex}(r2 + 1)r!
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm x=1}^{20}{/tex} ((r + 1)2 - 2r)r!
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm x=1}^{20}{/tex} ((r + 1)(r + 1)! - r{tex}\cdot{/tex}r!) - {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm x=1}^{20}{/tex}r{tex}\cdot{/tex}r!
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm x=1}^{20}{/tex} ((r + 1)(r + 1)! - r{tex}\cdot{/tex}r!) - {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm x=1}^{20}{/tex}((r + 1)! - r!)
= (21{tex}\cdot{/tex}21! - 1) - (21! - 1) = 20.21! = 22! - 2.21!

questions.question: If n is the degree of the polynomial, $\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8+\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8$ and m is the coefficient of x^n in it, then the ordered pair (n, m) is equal to
questions.question_raw: If n is the degree of the polynomial, {tex}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8+\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8{/tex} and m is the coefficient of xn in it, then the ordered pair (n, m) is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8+\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8{/tex}
After rationalise the polynomial we get
{tex}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}} \times \frac{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}} \times \frac{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}\right]^8{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\frac{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}{\left(5 x^3+1\right)-\left(5 x^3-1\right)}\right]^8+\left[\frac{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}{\left(5 x^3+1\right)-\left(5 x^3-1\right)}\right]^8{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2^8}\left[\left(\sqrt{5 x^3+1}+\sqrt{5 x^3-1}\right)^8\right.{/tex}{tex}+\left[\frac{\sqrt{5 x^3+1}-\sqrt{5 x^3-1}}{\left(5 x^3+1\right)-\left(5 x^3-1\right)}\right]^8{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2^8}\left[\begin{array}{l} { }^8 C_0\left(\sqrt{5 x^3+1}\right)^8+{ }^8 C_2\left(\sqrt{5 x^3+1}\right)^6\left(\sqrt{5 x^3-1}\right)^2 \\ +{ }^8 C_4\left(\sqrt{5 x^3+1}\right)^4\left(\sqrt{5 x^3-1}\right)^4 \\ +{ }^8 C_6\left(\sqrt{5 x^3+1}\right)^2\left(\sqrt{5 x^3-1}\right)^6+{ }^8 C_8\left(\sqrt{5 x^3-1}\right)^8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2^8}\left[\begin{array}{l} { }^8 C_0\left(5 x^3+1\right)^4+{ }^8 C_2\left(5 x^3+1\right)^3\left(5 x^3-1\right)+8_{C_4} \\ \left(5 x^3+1\right)^2\left(5 x^3-1\right)^2 \\ +{ }^8 C_6\left(5 x^3+1\right)\left(5 x^3-1\right)^3+8_{C_8}\left(5 x^3-1\right)^4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
So, the degree of polynomial is 12,
Now, coefficient of x12
= [8C054 + 8C254 + 8C454 + 8C654 + 8C854]
= 54 {tex}\times \frac{2^8}{2}{/tex} = 54 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times \frac{2^4}{2}{/tex} = 104 {tex}\times{/tex} 23 = 8(10)4

questions.question: If (27)^999 is divided by 7, then the remainder is:

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Remainder = 6

questions.question: If $\sum \limits_{\mathrm k=1}^{31}$ (^31C_k)(^31C_k-1) - $\sum \limits_{\mathrm k=1}^{30}$(^30C_k)(^30C_k-1) = $\frac{\alpha(60 !)}{(30 !)(31 !)}$, where $\alpha \in$ R, then the value of $16\alpha$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm k=1}^{31}{/tex} (31Ck)(31Ck-1) - {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm k=1}^{30}{/tex}(30Ck)(30Ck-1) = {tex}\frac{\alpha(60 !)}{(30 !)(31 !)}{/tex}, where {tex}\alpha \in{/tex} R, then the value of {tex}16\alpha{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given expression is {tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm k=1}^{31}{/tex} 31Ck{tex}\cdot{/tex}31Ck-1
= 31C1{tex}\cdot{/tex}31C0 + 31C2{tex}\cdot{/tex}31C1 +...+ 31C31{tex}\cdot{/tex}31C30
Here, nLr = nLn-r
= 31C0{tex}\cdot{/tex}31C30 + 31C1{tex}\cdot{/tex}31C29 +...+ 31C30{tex}\cdot{/tex}31C0 = 62C30
Similarly
{tex}\sum \limits_{\mathrm k=1}^{30}{/tex}(30Ck{tex}\cdot{/tex}30Ck-1) = 60C29
= 1 62C30 - 60C29 = {tex}\frac{62 !}{30 ! 32 !}-\frac{60 !}{29 ! 31 !}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{60 !}{29 ! 31 !}\left\{\frac{62.61}{30.32}-1\right\}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{60 !}{30 ! 31 !}\left(\frac{2822}{32}\right){/tex}
compare above equation with {tex}\frac{\alpha(60 !)}{(30 !)(31 !)}{/tex}
So, {tex}\alpha=\frac{2822}{32}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}16 \alpha{/tex} = {tex}16 \times \frac{2822}{32}{/tex} = 1411

questions.question_raw: The number of ways to distribute 30 identical candies among four children C1, C2, C3 and C4 so that C2 receives atleast 4 and atmost 7 candies, C3 receives atleast 2 and atmost 6 candies, is equal to
questions.explanation:
We are given that t1 + t2 + t3 + t4 = 30
Now coefficient of x30 in (12 + x3 + x2 +...+ x30)2
(x4 + x5 + x6 + x7) (x2 + x3 + x4 + x5 + x6)
{tex}x^6\left(\frac{1-x^{31}}{1-x}\right)^2{/tex}(1 + x + x2 + x3) (1 + x + x2 + x3 + x4)
x6(1 - x31)2 (1 - x4) (1 - x5) (1 - x)4
x6(1 - x4 - x5 + x9) (1 + x62 - 2x31) (1 - x )-4
x6(1 - x4 - x5 + x9) (1 - x)-4
Coefficient of xn in (1 - x)-r is n+r-1Cr-1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 27C3 - 23C3 - 22C3 - 18C3
2925 - 1771 - 1540 + 816 = 430
Alternate
x2 {tex}\in{/tex} [4, 7], x3 {tex}\in{/tex} [2, 6]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t1 + t2 + t3 + t4 = 24
total ways = 24 + 4 - 1C4-1 - 20 + 4 - 1C4-1 - 19 + 4 - 1C4-1 + 15 + 4 - 1C4-1
= 27C3 - 23C3 - 22C3 + 18C3 = 430

questions.question: The term independent of x in the expression of (1 - x^2 + 3x^3)$\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}$, x $\neq$ 0 is
questions.question_raw: The term independent of x in the expression of (1 - x2 + 3x3){tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex}, x {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is
questions.explanation:
We are given that the expression is
(1 - x2 + 3x3){tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex}; x {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
{tex}\because{/tex} General term of {tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex}
11Cr{tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3\right)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^r{/tex}
[{tex}\because{/tex} General term of (x + y)n is nCr (x)n-r {tex}\cdot{/tex} yr]
Now general term of 11Cr{tex}\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{1}{5}\right)^r{/tex}x33-5r
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Term independent of x is
1 {tex}\times{/tex} coefficient of x0 in {tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex} +
-1 {tex}\times{/tex} coefficient of x-2 in {tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex} +
3 {tex}\times{/tex} coefficient of x-3 in {tex}\left(\frac{5}{2} x^3-\frac{1}{5 x^2}\right)^{11}{/tex}
for coefficient of x0
33 - 5r = 0 not possible
for coefficient of x-2
33 - 5r = -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35 = 5r {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 7
for coefficient of x-3 
33 - 5r = -3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 36 = 5r not possible
So term independent of x is
(-1)11C7{tex}\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^4\left(-\frac{1}{5}\right)^7{/tex} = {tex}\frac{33}{200}{/tex}

questions.question: Let $[x]$ denote greatest integer less than or equal to $x$. If for $n \in {~N},\left(1-x+x^{3}\right)^{n}$ $=\sum_\limits{j=0}^{3 n} a_{j} x^{j}$, then $\sum_\limits{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n}{2}\right]} a_{2 j}+4 \sum_\limits{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n-1}{2}\right]} a_{2 j+1}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}[x]{/tex} denote greatest integer less than or equal to {tex}x{/tex}. If for {tex}n \in {~N},\left(1-x+x^{3}\right)^{n}{/tex} {tex}=\sum_\limits{j=0}^{3 n} a_{j} x^{j}{/tex}, then {tex}\sum_\limits{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n}{2}\right]} a_{2 j}+4 \sum_\limits{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n-1}{2}\right]} a_{2 j+1}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given that
{tex}\left(1-x+x^{3}\right)^{n}=\sum\limits_\limits{j=0}^{3 n} a_{j} x^{j}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left(1-x+x^3\right)^n{/tex} {tex}=a_0+a_1 x+a_2 x^2+a_3 x^3+{/tex} ... {tex}a_{3 n} x^{3 n}{/tex} ...(i)
We have to find the value of
{tex}\sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n}{2}\right]} a_{2 j}+4 \sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n-1}{2}\right]} a_{2 j+1}{/tex}
Here, {tex}\sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n}{2}\right]} a_{2 j}=a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots .{/tex}. and
{tex}\sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n-1}{2}\right]} a_{2 j+1}=a_{1}+a_{3}+a_{5}+\ldots .{/tex}
Put {tex}x=1{/tex} in equation (i)
{tex}1=a_{0}+a_{1}+a_{2}+a+\ldots+a_{3 n}{/tex} ...(ii)
Put {tex}x=-1{/tex} in equation (i)
{tex}1=a_{0}-a_{1}+a_{2}{/tex} {tex}-a_{3}+\ldots(-1)^{3 n} a^{3 n}{/tex} ...(iii)
After adding (ii) and (iii) we get
{tex}2=2\left(a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots \ldots\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow a_{0}+a_{2}+a_{4}+\ldots . .=1{/tex}
i.e. {tex}\sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n}{2}\right]} a_{2 j}=1{/tex} ...(iv)
and {tex}a_{1}+a_{3}+a_{5}+\ldots=0{/tex}
i.e. {tex}4^{\left[\frac{3 n-1}{2}\right]} a_{j=0}=0{/tex} ...(v)
Add equation (iv) and (v)
{tex}\Rightarrow \sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n}{2}\right]} a_{2 j+1}+4 \sum\limits_{j=0}^{\left[\frac{3 n-1}{2}\right]} a_{2 j+1}=1{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of $x^{256}$ in the expansion of $(1-x)^{101}\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)^{100}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of {tex}x^{256}{/tex} in the expansion of {tex}(1-x)^{101}\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)^{100}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}(1-x)^{101}\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)^{100}{/tex}
{tex}=(1-x)^{100}\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)^{100}(1-x){/tex}
{tex}=\left[(1-x)\left(1+x+x^{2}\right)\right]^{100}(1-x){/tex}
{tex}=\left(1-x^{3}\right)^{100}(1-x){/tex}
{tex}=(1-x)\left({ }^{100} {C}_{0}-{ }^{100} {C}_{1} x^{3}+{ }^{100} {C}_{2} x^{6}-\ldots \ldots+\right.{/tex}{tex}\left.{ }^{100} {C}_{84} x^{252}-{ }^{100} {C}_{85} x^{255}+{ }^{100} {C}_{86} x^{258}+\ldots.\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Coefficient of {tex}x^{256}{/tex} is {tex}{ }^{100} {C}_{85}={ }^{100} {C}_{100-85}={ }^{100} {C}_{15}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of $-{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_1+2 .{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_2-3 .{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_3+\ldots$ $ -15 .{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{15}+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_3+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_5+\ldots +{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_{11}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The value of {tex}-{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_1+2 .{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_2-3 .{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_3+\ldots{/tex} {tex} -15 .{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{15}+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_3+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_5+\ldots +{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_{11}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(-{ }^{15} {C}_1+2 \cdot{ }^{15} {C}_2-3 \cdot{ }^{15} {C}_3+\ldots -15 \cdot{ }^{15} {C}_{15}\right){/tex} {tex}+\left({ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_3+\ldots .+{ }^{14} \mathrm{C}_{11}\right){/tex}
{tex} =\sum\limits_{{r}=1}^{15}(-1)^{{r}} \cdot {r}^{15} {C}_{{r}}+\left({ }^{14} {C}_1+{ }^{14} {C}_3+\ldots+{ }^{14} {C}_{11}+{ }^{14} {C}_{13}\right)-{ }^{14} {C}_3 {/tex}
{tex} =\sum\limits_{{r}=1}^{15}(-1)^{{r}} 15 \cdot{ }^{14} {C}_{{r}-1}+2^{13}-14 {/tex}
{tex} =15\left(-{ }^{14} {C}_0+{ }^{14} {C}_1 \ldots \ldots . .-{ }^{14} {C}_{14}\right)+2^{13}-14 {/tex}
{tex} =2^{13}-14 {/tex}

questions.question: If A denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of $\left(1-3 x+10 x^{2}\right)^{n}$ and $B$ denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of $\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{n}$, then:
questions.question_raw: If A denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of {tex}\left(1-3 x+10 x^{2}\right)^{n}{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex} denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of {tex}\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{n}{/tex}, then:
questions.explanation:
Put {tex}x=1{/tex}, we get
{tex}{A}=(1-3+10)^{n}={B}^{n}{/tex} and {tex}{B}=(1+1)^{n}=2^{n}{/tex}
Clearly {tex}{A}=8^{n}=\left(2^{n}\right)^{3}={B}^{3}{/tex}

questions.question: $25^{190}-19^{190}-8^{190}+2^{190}$ is divisible by
questions.question_raw: {tex}25^{190}-19^{190}-8^{190}+2^{190}{/tex} is divisible by
questions.explanation:
The given expression is divisible by 6 and 17.
Also, {tex}25^{190}-8^{190}{/tex} is not divisible by 7
but {tex}19^{190}-2^{190}{/tex} is divisible by 7,
So, {tex}25^{190}-19^{190}-8^{190}+2^{190}{/tex} is divisible by 34 but not by 14.

questions.question:
Among the statements:
(S1): $2023^{2022}-1999^{2022}$ is divisible by 8
(S2): $13(13)^{n}-11 n-13$ is divisible by 144 for infinitely many $n \in {~N}$
questions.question_raw:
Among the statements:
(S1): {tex}2023^{2022}-1999^{2022}{/tex} is divisible by 8
(S2): {tex}13(13)^{n}-11 n-13{/tex} is divisible by 144 for infinitely many {tex}n \in {~N}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^{n}-y^{n}=(x-y)\left[x^{n-1}+x^{n-2} y+x^{n-3} y^{2}+\ldots+y^{n-1}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left({S}_{1}\right) \rightarrow(2023)^{2022}-(1999)^{2022}{/tex}
{tex}\rightarrow(2023)-(1999)=24{/tex} is divisible by {tex}8^{\prime}{/tex}
{tex}\left({S}_{2}\right) \rightarrow(13)(1+12)^{n}-11 n-13{/tex}
{tex}=13\left[1+{ }^{n} {C}_{1}(12)+{ }^{n} {C}_{2}(12)^{2}+\ldots\right]-11 n-13{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 145 n+13 .{ }^{n} {C}_{2}(12)^{2}+13^{n} {C}_{3}(12)^{3}+\ldots{/tex}

questions.question: If ${ }^{2 n} {C}_{3}:{ }^{n} {C}_{3}: 10: 1$, then the ratio $\left(n^{2}+3 n\right):\left(n^{2}-3 n\right.$ $+4)$ is:
questions.question_raw: If {tex}{ }^{2 n} {C}_{3}:{ }^{n} {C}_{3}: 10: 1{/tex}, then the ratio {tex}\left(n^{2}+3 n\right):\left(n^{2}-3 n\right.{/tex} {tex}+4){/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{ }^{2 n} {C}_{3}}{{ }^{n} {C}_{3}}=10 \Rightarrow \frac{2 n!(n-3)!}{(2 n-3)!n!}=10 \left[\because{ }^{n} {C}_{r}=\frac{n!}{r(n-r)!}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4(2 n-1)}{n-2}=10 \Rightarrow n=8{/tex}
Now {tex}\frac{n^{2}+8 n}{n^{2}-3 n+4}=\frac{64+24}{64-24+4}=\frac{2}{1}{/tex}

questions.question: The coefficient of $x^{301}$ in $(1+x)^{500}+x(1+x)^{499}+$ $x^{2}(1+x)^{498}+\ldots .+x^{500}$ is:
questions.question_raw: The coefficient of {tex}x^{301}{/tex} in {tex}(1+x)^{500}+x(1+x)^{499}+{/tex} {tex}x^{2}(1+x)^{498}+\ldots .+x^{500}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}(1+x)^{500}+x(1+x)^{499}+x^{2}(1+x)^{498}+\ldots+x^{500}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1+x)^{500}\left[\left(\frac{x}{1+x}\right)^{501}-1\right]}{\frac{x}{1+x}-1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1+x)^{500} \frac{\left[x^{501}-(1+x)^{501}\right]}{(1+x)^{501}}}{\frac{-1}{1+x}}{/tex}
{tex}=(1+x)^{501}-x^{501}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Coefficient of {tex}x^{301}{/tex} in given expression is {tex}{ }^{501} {C}_{301}{/tex} or {tex}{ }^{501} {C}_{200}{/tex}

questions.question: If $\frac{1}{n+1}{ }^{n} C_{n}+\frac{1}{n}{ }^{n} C_{n-1}+\ldots$ $+\frac{1}{2}{ }^{n} C_{1}+{ }^{n} C_{0}=\frac{1023}{10}$ then $n$ is equal to
questions.question_raw: If {tex}\frac{1}{n+1}{ }^{n} C_{n}+\frac{1}{n}{ }^{n} C_{n-1}+\ldots{/tex} {tex}+\frac{1}{2}{ }^{n} C_{1}+{ }^{n} C_{0}=\frac{1023}{10}{/tex} then {tex}n{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given that
{tex}\frac{1}{n+1}{ }^{n} {C}_{n}+\frac{1}{n}{ }^{n} {C}_{n-1}+\ldots{/tex} {tex}+\frac{1}{2}{ }^{n} {C}_{1}+{ }^{n} {C}_{0}=\frac{1023}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\sum_{r=0}^{n} \frac{{ }^{n} {C}_{r}}{r+1}=\frac{1023}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\sum_{r=0}^{n} \frac{(r+1)}{(n+1)} \frac{{ }^{n+1} {C}_{r+1}}{(r+1)}=\frac{1023}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{n+1} \sum_{r=0}^{n}{ }^{n+1} {C}_{r+1}=\frac{1023}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{n+1}\left(2^{n+1}-1\right)=\frac{1023}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2^{n+1}-1}{n+1}=\frac{1024-1}{10}=\frac{2^{10}-1}{10}{/tex}

questions.question: If the $1011^{\text {th }}$ term from the end in the binominal expansion of $\left(\frac{4 x}{5}-\frac{5}{2 x}\right)^{2022}$ is 1024 times $1011^{\text {th }}$ term from the beginning, then $|x|$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the {tex}1011^{\text {th }}{/tex} term from the end in the binominal expansion of {tex}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}-\frac{5}{2 x}\right)^{2022}{/tex} is 1024 times {tex}1011^{\text {th }}{/tex} term from the beginning, then {tex}|x|{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given that, in exp of {tex}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}-\frac{5}{2 x}\right)^{2022}{/tex}
{tex}{T}_{1011}^{\prime}=1024 \times {T}_{1011}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{ }^{2022} {C}_{1010}\left(\frac{-5}{2 x}\right)^{2022-1010}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}\right)^{1010}{/tex} {tex}=2^{10} \times{ }^{2022} {C}_{1010}{/tex}{tex}\left(\frac{-5}{2 x}\right)^{1010}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}\right)^{1012}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{-5}{2 x}\right)^{2}=2^{10} \times\left(\frac{4 x}{5}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{4}=\frac{5^{4}}{2^{16}}=\frac{5^{4}}{\left(2^{4}\right)^{4}} \text { or }|x|=\frac{5}{16}{/tex}
